```{r}
                                      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
                                      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
                                      axis.line.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
                                      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12.5, angle = 0, vjust = 0.5),
                                      axis.ticks.y = element_line(linewidth = 1.25, color = "black"),
                                      axis.ticks.length.y = unit(1.25, units = "mm"),
                                      plot.margin = unit(c(0.1,0.1,0.1,0.1), "mm"))
  }

  # Add x-axis theme
  if(x_axis_theme == "full") {
    gg_region_track <- gg_region_track +
                         theme(axis.line.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
                               axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                               axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
                               axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))
    
  } else if(x_axis_theme == "ticks") {
    gg_region_track <- gg_region_track +
                         theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                               axis.text.x = element_blank(),
                               axis.line.x = element_blank(),
                               axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
                               axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))
    
  } else if(x_axis_theme == "blank") {
    gg_region_track <- gg_region_track +
                         theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                               axis.text.x = element_blank(),
                               axis.line.x = element_blank(),
                               axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
  }
  
  # Output plot
  return(gg_region_track)
}

# TODO: incorporate this to plotting tracks
# Remove first tick mark from y axis
# https://stackoverflow.com/questions/66279940/remove-first-label-of-y-axis
breaks_minus_one <- function(x) {
  x <- scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3)
  x[x<=0] <- NA
  x
}

geom_count_mutations <- function(data = NULL, maf_file = NULL, vcf_file = NULL, 
                                 window_of_interest = NULL, flank = 0,
                                 aes_y_column, aes_fill_color = "gray70",
                                 x_axis_theme = "full") {
  # TODO: multiple forms of input, like point to diff file types and use readers (VCF, MAF, etc)
  
  # First, subset to mutations of interest plus flank on both ends of genomic interval of WoI
  moi_query <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(data)) %&% (gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) + flank)
  
  # Convert RoI query GR to DT
  moi_query_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(moi_query)
  
  # Count the number of mutations at single locus using chromosome, start site, and reference allele
  moi_query_counts <- moi_query_dt %>%
                        dplyr::group_by(seqnames, start, .data[[aes_y_column]]) %>%
                        dplyr::count()
  
  # Get the settings for the locus view x-axis
  locus_x_axis <- get_locus_axis(input_window = window_of_interest, input_flank = flank)
  
  # Create plot
  gg_mutation_count_track <- ggplot2::ggplot(data = moi_query_counts) +
                               ggplot2::geom_segment(aes(x = start, xend = start, y = 0, yend = n), color = "gray10", alpha = 0.65, linewidth = 0.5) +
                               ggplot2::geom_point(aes(x = start, y = n, size = n), shape = 21, fill = aes_fill_color, color = aes_fill_color) +
                               ggplot2::scale_size(guide = "none") +
                               ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(name = locus_x_axis$name, labels = locus_x_axis$labels, limits = locus_x_axis$limits, expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
                               ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0,max(moi_query_counts$n) + 1), expand = c(0.03,0.03),
                                                           breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3)) +
                               ggplot2::labs(y = "Mutation\nCount") +
                               ggplot2::theme(panel.background = element_blank(),
                                              panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
                                              panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
                                              axis.line.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
                                              axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12.5, angle = 0, vjust = 0.5),
                                              axis.ticks.y = element_line(linewidth = 1.25, color = "black"),
                                              axis.ticks.length.y = unit(1.25, units = "mm"),
                                              plot.margin = unit(c(0.1,0.1,0.1,0.1), "mm"))
  
  # Add x-axis theme
  if(x_axis_theme == "full") {
    gg_mutation_count_track <- gg_mutation_count_track +
                                 theme(axis.line.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
                                       axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                                       axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
                                       axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))
    
  } else if(x_axis_theme == "ticks") {
    gg_mutation_count_track <- gg_mutation_count_track +
                                 theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                                       axis.text.x = element_blank(),
                                       axis.line.x = element_blank(),
                                       axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
                                       axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))
    
  } else if(x_axis_theme == "blank") {
    gg_mutation_count_track <- gg_mutation_count_track +
                                 theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                                       axis.text.x = element_blank(),
                                       axis.line.x = element_blank(),
                                       axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
  }
  
  # Output plot
  return(gg_mutation_count_track)
}

# columns exepect within muts file: start, VAF, seqnames
geom_vaf_landscape <- function(data = NULL, mut_file = NULL, vcf_file = NULL,
                               window_of_interest = NULL, flank = 0, point_size = 3,
                               contrast = T, x_axis_theme = "full") {
  # TODO: multiple forms of input, like point to diff file types and use readers (VCF, MAF, etc)
  
  # First, subset to mutations of interest plus flank on both ends of genomic interval of WoI
  moi_query <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(data)) %&% (gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) + flank)
  
  # Convert RoI query GR to DT
  moi_query_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(moi_query)
  
  # Get the settings for the locus view x-axis
  locus_x_axis <- get_locus_axis(input_window = window_of_interest, input_flank = flank)
  
  # Create plot
  gg_vaf_track <- ggplot2::ggplot(moi_query_dt) +
                    ggpointdensity::stat_pointdensity(aes(x = start, y = VAF), method = "kde2d", size = point_size) +
                    ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn(name = "Density", colours = c(paletteer::paletteer_c(palette = "grDevices::Lajolla", n = 15))) +
                    ggplot2::labs(y = "VAF") +
                    ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(name = locus_x_axis$name, labels = locus_x_axis$labels, limits = locus_x_axis$limits, expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
                    ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,1), expand = c(0.01,0.01), breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3)) +
                     ggplot2::theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
                                    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
                                    axis.line.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
                                    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12.5, angle = 0, vjust = 0.5),
                                    axis.ticks.y = element_line(linewidth = 1.25, color = "black"),
                                    axis.ticks.length.y = unit(1.25, units = "mm"),
                                    plot.margin = unit(c(0.1,0.1,0.1,0.1), "mm"))
  
  # Add background
  if(contrast == T) {
    gg_vaf_track <- gg_vaf_track +
                      theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "gray50"))
    
  } else if(contrast == F) {
    gg_vaf_track <- gg_vaf_track +
                      theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA))
  }
  
  # Add x-axis theme
  if(x_axis_theme == "full") {
    gg_vaf_track <- gg_vaf_track +
                      theme(axis.line.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
                            axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                            axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
                            axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))
    
  } else if(x_axis_theme == "ticks") {
    gg_vaf_track <- gg_vaf_track +
                      theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                            axis.text.x = element_blank(),
                            axis.line.x = element_blank(),
                            axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
                            axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))
    
  } else if(x_axis_theme == "blank") {
    gg_vaf_track <- gg_vaf_track +
                      theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                            axis.text.x = element_blank(),
                            axis.line.x = element_blank(),
                            axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
  }
  
  # Output plot
  return(gg_vaf_track)
}

geom_epilogo_col <- function(data = NULL, epilogo_file = NULL, window_of_interest = NULL,
                             flank = 0, x_axis_theme = "full", contrast = T) {
  # TODO: # For alternate route of reading files from scratch and not already as obj
  #mm_epilogos <- aggregate_these(path_to_files = "~/morganLab/mgp1000/development/nonCoding/epiLogo/results/",
  #                             pattern_to_grab = "*_mm_epilogos.*.txt.gz",
  #                             delim = "\t",
  #                            has_header = F)
  #chromhmm_metadata <- fread(file = "nonCoding/epiLogo/metadata.tsv",
  #                         sep = "\t",
  #                         header = T)
  #colnames(mm_epilogos) <- c("chrom", "start", "end", chromhmm_metadata$long_name)
  
  # First, subset to epilogos of interest plus flank on both ends of genomic interval of WoI
  eoi_query <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(data)) %&% (gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) + flank)
  
  # Restrict the beginning and end of the GoI window if the genes extend out of the window
  # If GoI start is less than the WoI start (i.e. out-of-bounds), rrestrict start
  if(sum(GenomicRanges::start(eoi_query) < GenomicRanges::start(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) + flank)) > 0) {
    eoi_query <- GenomicRanges::restrict(x = eoi_query, start = GenomicRanges::start(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) + flank))
  }
  # If GoI end is greater than the WoI end (i.e. out-of-bounds), restrict end
  if(sum(GenomicRanges::end(eoi_query) > GenomicRanges::end(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) + flank)) > 0) {
    eoi_query <- GenomicRanges::restrict(x = eoi_query, end = GenomicRanges::end(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) + flank))
  }
  
  # Get midpoint of EoI query GR ranges then convert to DT
  eoi_query_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(gUtils::gr.mid(eoi_query))
  
  # Melt the EoI DT
  eoi_query_melt <- data.table::melt(eoi_query_dt,
                                     id.vars = c("seqnames", "start", "end"),
                                     measure.vars = colnames(eoi_query_dt)[6:length(colnames(eoi_query_dt))],
                                     variable.name = "chromhmm_state",
                                     variable.factor = T,
                                     value.name = "bits",
                                     value.factor = F)
  
  # Get the settings for the locus view x-axis
  locus_x_axis <- get_locus_axis(input_window = window_of_interest, input_flank = flank)
  
  # Remove interactions that are outside the window
  eoi_query_melt_bound <- eoi_query_melt %>%
                            dplyr::filter(start > locus_x_axis$limits[1] & end < locus_x_axis$limits[2])
  
  # Order the ChromHMM states
  eoi_query_melt_bound$chromhmm_state <- stringr::str_replace_all(string = eoi_query_melt_bound$chromhmm_state,
                                                                  pattern = "_",
                                                                  replacement = " ")
  eoi_query_melt_bound$chromhmm_state <- factor(eoi_query_melt_bound$chromhmm_state,
                                             levels = rev(c("Heterochromatin Low Signal","H3K27me3 Repressed","H3K9me3 Repressed",
                                                        "Weak Transcription","Transcription Elongation","Transcription Transition",
                                                        "Weak Enhancer","Strong Enhancer2","Strong Enhancer1",
                                                        "Poised Promoter","Weak Promoter","Active Promoter")))
  
  # Color blind friendly
  chromhmm_state_colors <- c("#ff6db6","#ffb6db","#b66dff",
                             "#920000","#924900","#db6d00",
                             "#006ddb","#6db6ff","#b6dbff",
                             "#009292","#004949","#000000")
  
                            #c("#260F99","#6551CC","#BFB2FF",
                            #  "#0F6B99","#51A3CC","#B2E5FF",
                            #  "#6B990F","#A3CC51","#E5FFB2",
                            #  "#99540F","#CC8E51","#FFD8B2")
  
  # Create plot
  gg_epilogo_track <- ggplot(eoi_query_melt_bound) +
                        ggplot2::geom_col(aes(x = start, y = bits, fill = chromhmm_state), na.rm = T, width = 200) +
                        ggplot2::labs(y = "Epilogos") +
                        scale_fill_manual(name = "ChromHMM\nStates", values = chromhmm_state_colors) +
                        ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
                        ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(name = locus_x_axis$name, labels = locus_x_axis$labels, limits = locus_x_axis$limits, expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
                        ggplot2::theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
                                       panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
                                       axis.line.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
                                       axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12.5, angle = 0, vjust = 0.5),
                                       axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
                                       axis.text.y = element_blank(),
                                       plot.margin = unit(c(0.051,0.05,0.05,0.05), "mm"))
  
  # Add background
  if(contrast == T) {
    gg_epilogo_track <- gg_epilogo_track +
                          theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "black"))
    
  } else if(contrast == F) {
    gg_epilogo_track <- gg_epilogo_track +
                          theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"))
  }
  
  # Add x-axis theme
  if(x_axis_theme == "full") {
    gg_epilogo_track <- gg_epilogo_track +
                      theme(axis.line.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
                            axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                            axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
                            axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))
    
  } else if(x_axis_theme == "ticks") {
    gg_epilogo_track <- gg_epilogo_track +
                      theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                            axis.text.x = element_blank(),
                            axis.line.x = element_blank(),
                            axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
                            axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))
    
  } else if(x_axis_theme == "blank") {
    gg_epilogo_track <- gg_epilogo_track +
                      theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                            axis.text.x = element_blank(),
                            axis.line.x = element_blank(),
                            axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
  }
  
  # Output plot
  return(gg_epilogo_track)
}

geom_interactions <- function(data = NULL, bedpe_file = NULL, window_of_interest = NULL,
                              flank = 0, aes_start_column, aes_end_column, aes_thickness_column,
                              link_curvature = -0.33, x_axis_theme = "full") {
  # TODO: multiple forms of input, like point to diff file types and use readers (VCF, MAF, etc)
  
  # First, subset to interactions of interest plus flank on both ends of genomic interval of WoI
  ioi_query <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(data)) %&% (gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) + flank)
  
  # Convert RoI query GR to DT
  ioi_query_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(ioi_query)
  
  # Create a DT of necessary columns as input for MintChip
  ioi_mintchip_dt <- ioi_query_dt %>%
                       dplyr::select(all_of(c(aes_start_column, aes_end_column, aes_thickness_column))) %>%
                       dplyr::rename("start" = all_of(aes_start_column),
                                     "end" = all_of(aes_end_column),
                                     "thickness" = all_of(aes_thickness_column))
  
  # Need to convert integer format to numeric
  ioi_mintchip_dt$start <- as.numeric(ioi_mintchip_dt$start)
  ioi_mintchip_dt$end <- as.numeric(ioi_mintchip_dt$end)
  
  # Get the settings for the locus view x-axis
  locus_x_axis <- get_locus_axis(input_window = window_of_interest, input_flank = flank)
  
  # Remove interactions that are outside the window
  ioi_mintchip_dt <- ioi_mintchip_dt %>%
                       dplyr::filter(start > locus_x_axis$limits[1] & end < locus_x_axis$limits[2])
  
  # Create plot
  gg_interaction_track <- ggplot(data = ioi_mintchip_dt) +
      ggplot2::geom_curve(aes(x = start, xend = end, y = 0, yend = 0, linewidth = thickness, color = thickness),
                           lineend = "round",
                           alpha = 0.8,
                           curvature = link_curvature,
                           ncp = 20) +
      ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn(name = "EPIraction\nScore", colours = c(paletteer::paletteer_c("grDevices::Berlin", 30)[15:30])) +
      ggplot2::scale_linewidth(guide = NULL) +
      ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(name = locus_x_axis$name, labels = locus_x_axis$labels, limits = locus_x_axis$limits, expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
      ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,0.01), expand = c(0,0)) +
      ggplot2::labs(y = "Interactions") +
      ggplot2::theme(panel.background = element_blank(),
                     panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
                     panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
                     axis.line.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
                     axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
                     axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
                     axis.text.y = element_blank(),
                     plot.margin = unit(c(0.1,0.1,0.1,0.1), "mm"))
  
  # Add x-axis theme
  if(x_axis_theme == "full") {
    gg_interaction_track <- gg_interaction_track +
                              theme(axis.line.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
                                    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                                    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
                                    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))
    
  } else if(x_axis_theme == "ticks") {
    gg_interaction_track <- gg_interaction_track +
                              theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                                    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
                                    axis.line.x = element_blank(),
                                    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
                                    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))
    
  } else if(x_axis_theme == "blank") {
    gg_interaction_track <- gg_interaction_track +
                              theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                                    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
                                    axis.line.x = element_blank(),
                                    axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
  }
  
  # Output plot
  return(gg_interaction_track)
}

# add functionality to connect in-window DEL, INV, and DUP BPs
# but maintain distinct SV bps
geom_sv_breakpoints <- function(data = NULL, bedpe_file = NULL, window_of_interest = NULL,
                                flank = 0, aes_y_column, aes_fill_column, aes_shape_column,
                                x_axis_theme = "full") {
  # TODO: # For alternate route of reading files from scratch and not already as obj
  #mm_epilogos <- aggregate_these(path_to_files = "~/morganLab/mgp1000/development/nonCoding/epiLogo/results/",
  #                             pattern_to_grab = "*_mm_epilogos.*.txt.gz",
  #                             delim = "\t",
  #                            has_header = F)
  #chromhmm_metadata <- fread(file = "nonCoding/epiLogo/metadata.tsv",
  #                         sep = "\t",
  #                         header = T)
  #colnames(mm_epilogos) <- c("chrom", "start", "end", chromhmm_metadata$long_name)
  
   # First, subset to breakpoints of interest plus flank on both ends of genomic interval of WoI
  boi_query <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(data)) %&% (gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) + flank)
  
  # Convert BoI query GR to DT
  boi_query_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(boi_query)
  
  # Get the settings for the locus view x-axis
  locus_x_axis <- get_locus_axis(input_window = window_of_interest, input_flank = flank)
  
  # Set the color palette
  sv_type_colors <- data.table::data.table("sv_type" = c("DEL","DUP","INV","TRA"),
                                           "colors" = paletteer::paletteer_d("ggsci::default_jama")[1:4])
  
  # TODO: for different y-axis variables need to account for when setting y-axis scale
  # also account for use of color when provided shape, while fill used for no shape
  # Create plot
  gg_breakpoint_track <- ggplot2::ggplot(data = boi_query_dt) +
                     ggplot2::geom_point(aes(x = start,
                                             y = .data[[aes_y_column]],
                                             fill = .data[[aes_fill_column]],
                                             color = .data[[aes_fill_column]]),
                                         shape = 21, size = 4) +
                     scale_fill_manual(name = "SV\nClass",
                                       values = sv_type_colors %>%
                                                  dplyr::filter(sv_type %in% unique(boi_query_dt %>%
                                                                                      dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(aes_fill_column)) %>%
                                                                                      purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                                                                      unique())) %>%
                                                                  dplyr::select(colors) %>%
                                                                  purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                                                  as.character()) +
                    scale_color_manual(guide = "none",
                                      values = sv_type_colors %>%
                                                dplyr::filter(sv_type %in% unique(boi_query_dt %>%
                                                                                   dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(aes_fill_column)) %>%
                                                                                   purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                                                                   unique())) %>%
                                                dplyr::select(colors) %>%
                                                purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                                as.character()) +
                     ggplot2::labs(y = "Breakpoints") +
                     ggplot2::scale_y_discrete(expand = c(0.03,0.03)) +
                     ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(name = locus_x_axis$name, labels = locus_x_axis$labels, limits = locus_x_axis$limits, expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
                      ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "right",
                                     panel.background = element_blank(),
                                     panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
                                     panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
                                     axis.line.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
                                     axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
                                     axis.ticks.length.y = unit(1.25, units = "mm"),
                                     axis.text.y = element_blank(),
                                     axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12.5, angle = 0, vjust = 0.5),
                                     plot.margin = unit(c(0.05,0.05,0.05,0.05), "mm"))
  
  # Add x-axis theme
  if(x_axis_theme == "full") {
    gg_breakpoint_track <- gg_breakpoint_track +
                            theme(axis.line.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
                                  axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                                  axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
                                  axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))
    
  } else if(x_axis_theme == "ticks") {
    gg_breakpoint_track <- gg_breakpoint_track +
                            theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                                  axis.text.x = element_blank(),
                                  axis.line.x = element_blank(),
                                  axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
                                  axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))
    
  } else if(x_axis_theme == "blank") {
    gg_breakpoint_track <- gg_breakpoint_track +
                            theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                                  axis.text.x = element_blank(),
                                  axis.line.x = element_blank(),
                                  axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
  }
  
  # Output plot
  return(gg_breakpoint_track)
}

# TODO: take bigwig/bedgraph-like/extended BED like
# designed to take files that look like:
#chr     start   end     MM59300 MM58698 ... MM54
#chr1    10052   10447   3.36224889424292        5.15650463271808
#chr1    180676  181211  3.77952639507682        5.03959938
geom_ridge_track <- function(data = NULL, file = NULL, window_of_interest = NULL,
                             flank = 0, aes_y_column = NULL, aes_facet_y_column = NULL,
                             fill_color = "darkred", x_axis_theme = "full") {
  # TODO: multiple forms of input, like point to diff file types and use readers (bigWig, bedGraph, etc)
  
  # TODO: this requies loading from scratch each time, too slow
  #       for now, let user load GTF as GRanges obj anyway
  #if(!is.null(data)) {
  #  # Quickset the data like a regular ggplot geom
  #  bed_gr <- data
  #} else if(is.null(data) & !is.null(bed_file)) {
  #  # Read in the GTF data
  #  bed_gr <- devgru::read_bed_file(gtf_file_path = bed_file)
  #}
  
  # First, subset to region of interest plus flank on both ends of genomic interval of WoI
  roi_query <- data %&% (gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) + flank)
  
  # Restrict the beginning and end of the GoI window if the genes extend out of the window
  # If GoI start is less than the WoI start (i.e. out-of-bounds), rrestrict start
  if(sum(GenomicRanges::start(roi_query) < GenomicRanges::start(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) + flank)) > 0) {
    roi_query <- GenomicRanges::restrict(x = roi_query, start = GenomicRanges::start(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) + flank))
  }
  # If GoI end is greater than the WoI end (i.e. out-of-bounds), restrict end
  if(sum(GenomicRanges::end(roi_query) > GenomicRanges::end(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) + flank)) > 0) {
    roi_query <- GenomicRanges::restrict(x = roi_query, end = GenomicRanges::end(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) + flank))
  }
  
  # Find midpoint of range for each value
  roi_mid_query <- gUtils::gr.mid(roi_query)
  
  # Convert RoI query GR to DT
  roi_query_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(roi_mid_query)
  
  # Get the settings for the locus view x-axis
  locus_x_axis <- get_locus_axis(input_window = window_of_interest, input_flank = flank)
  
  # Need to melt the RoI query DT get into ggplot format with multiple samples
  # with columns for scores
  if(!is.null(aes_facet_y_column)) {
    melt_roi_query_dt <- data.table::melt(data = roi_query_dt[,-c("strand", "end", "width")],
                                          id.vars = c("seqnames", "start"),
                                          value.name = eval(aes_y_column),
                                          variable.name = aes_facet_y_column)
  } else if(is.null(aes_facet_y_column)){
    melt_roi_query_dt <- roi_query_dt %>%
                          dplyr::select(seqnames,start,eval(aes_y_column))
  }
  
  gg_ridge_plot <- ggplot2::ggplot(data = melt_roi_query_dt,
                                     aes(x = start, y = .data[[aes_y_column]])) +
                      ggplot2::geom_col(fill = fill_color, width = 150) +
                      ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(name = locus_x_axis$name, labels = locus_x_axis$labels, limits = locus_x_axis$limits, expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
                      ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3), expand = c(0.02,0.02)) +
                      ggplot2::theme(panel.background = element_blank(),
                                     panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
                                     panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
                                     axis.line.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
                                     axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12.5, angle = 0, vjust = 0.5),
                                     axis.ticks.y = element_line(linewidth = 1.25, color = "black"),
                                     axis.ticks.length.y = unit(1.25, units = "mm"),
                                     plot.margin = unit(c(0.1,0.1,0.1,0.1), "mm"))
    # Add x-axis theme
  if(x_axis_theme == "full") {
    gg_ridge_plot <- gg_ridge_plot +
                         theme(axis.line.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
                               axis.title.x = element_blanks(),
                               axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
                               axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))
    
  } else if(x_axis_theme == "ticks") {
    gg_ridge_plot <- gg_ridge_plot +
                         theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                               axis.text.x = element_blank(),
                               axis.line.x = element_blank(),
                               axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
                               axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))
    
  } else if(x_axis_theme == "blank") {
    gg_ridge_plot <- gg_ridge_plot +
                         theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                               axis.text.x = element_blank(),
                               axis.line.x = element_blank(),
                               axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
  }
  
  # first, zoom to region provided
  # TODO: can take input DT or GR
  #zoom_bed_gr <- data %&% (gUtils::parse.gr(window) + pad)
  # Find midpoint of range for each value
  #zoom_mid_bed_gr <- gUtils::gr.mid(zoom_bed_gr)
  # Convert to data.table
  #zoom_mid_bed_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(zoom_mid_bed_gr)
  
  # Need to melt to get into ggplot format
  #melt_zoom_bed_dt <- data.table::melt(data = zoom_mid_bed_dt[,-c("strand", "width")],
  #                                     id.vars = c("seqnames", "start", "end"),
  #                                     value.name = aes_y_axis_column, # TODO: PARAMETERIZE
  #                                     variable.name = aes_facet_y_column) # TODO: PARAMETRIZE
  
  #gg_track_plot <- ggplot(data = melt_zoom_bed_dt) +
  #                   ggHoriPlot::geom_horizon(aes(x = start, y = .data[[aes_y_axis_column]], fill = ..Cutpoints..),
  #                                            origin = 0,
  #                                            horizonscale = c(0,0.01, round(max(melt_zoom_bed_dt[[aes_y_axis_column]]), digits = 0))) +
  #                   scale_fill_manual(values = c(aes_fill_color, "transparent")) +
  #                   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_bytes(accuracy = 0.001), limits = c(get_window_limits(input_window = window)$left - pad, get_window_limits(input_window = window)$right + pad), expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) +
  #                   scale_y_continuous(n.breaks = 3,
  #                                      labels = scales::label_number(scale = round(max(melt_zoom_bed_dt[[aes_y_axis_column]]) + 1, digits = 0),
  #                                                                    accuracy = 1)) +
  #                   facet_grid(.data[[aes_facet_y_column]] ~ ., switch = "y") +
  #                   theme(legend.position = "none",
  #                         panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA),
  #                         panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray80"),
  #                         axis.title.x = element_blank(),
  #                         axis.line.x = element_blank(),
  #                         axis.text.x = element_blank(),
  #                         axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
  #                         axis.ticks.length.x = unit(3, units = "mm"),
  #                         panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines"),
  #                         strip.text.y.left = element_text(size = 14, angle = 0, hjust = 0),
  #                         strip.placement = "outside",
  #                         strip.background = element_rect(fill = NA),
  #                         axis.ticks.y = element_line(linewidth = 3, color = "black"),
  #                         axis.ticks.length.y = unit(2, units = "mm"),
  #                         axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
  #                         axis.title.y = element_blank())
  
  return(gg_ridge_plot)
}



################################################################################
# Integrating plyinteractions and plyranges for working with GRs

#' Convert GInteractions to BEDPE format
#'
#' @param gi GInteractions object
#' @param include_metadata Logical, whether to include metadata columns (default: TRUE)
#' @param zero_based Logical, whether to use 0-based coordinates (default: TRUE)
#' @param sort_output Logical, whether to sort output (default: TRUE)
#' @return data.table in BEDPE format
gi_to_bedpe <- function(gi, include_metadata = TRUE, zero_based = TRUE, sort_output = TRUE) {
  
  require(plyinteractions)
  require(InteractionSet)
  require(data.table)
  
  if(!is(gi, "GInteractions")) {
    stop("Input must be a GInteractions object")
  }
  
  # Convert to data.table using plyinteractions
  bedpe_dt <- plyinteractions::as_tibble(gi) %>% 
    as.data.table()
  
  # Standardize column names to BEDPE format
  col_mapping <- c(
    "seqnames1" = "chr1",
    "start1" = "start1", 
    "end1" = "end1",
    "seqnames2" = "chr2",
    "start2" = "start2",
    "end2" = "end2",
    "strand1" = "strand1",
    "strand2" = "strand2"
  )
  
  # Rename columns that exist
  for(old_name in names(col_mapping)) {
    if(old_name %in% colnames(bedpe_dt)) {
      setnames(bedpe_dt, old_name, col_mapping[old_name], skip_absent = TRUE)
    }
  }
  
  # Convert chromosomes to character
  bedpe_dt[, chr1 := as.character(chr1)]
  bedpe_dt[, chr2 := as.character(chr2)]
  
  # Convert to 0-based if requested
  if(zero_based) {
    bedpe_dt[, start1 := start1 - 1]
    bedpe_dt[, start2 := start2 - 1]
  }
  
  # Add type and score placeholders if not present
  if(!"type" %in% colnames(bedpe_dt)) {
    bedpe_dt[, type := "."]
  }
  if(!"score" %in% colnames(bedpe_dt)) {
    bedpe_dt[, score := "."]
  }
  
  # Handle strand conversion
  bedpe_dt[strand1 == "*", strand1 := "."]
  bedpe_dt[strand2 == "*", strand2 := "."]
  
  # Reorder columns - core BEDPE columns first
  core_cols <- c("chr1", "start1", "end1", "chr2", "start2", "end2", 
                 "type", "score", "strand1", "strand2")
  other_cols <- setdiff(colnames(bedpe_dt), core_cols)
  bedpe_dt <- bedpe_dt[, c(intersect(core_cols, colnames(bedpe_dt)), other_cols), with = FALSE]
  
  # Sort if requested
  if(sort_output) {
    bedpe_dt <- sort_bedpe(bedpe_dt)
  }
  
  return(bedpe_dt)
}

#' Convert BEDPE to GInteractions
#'
#' @param bedpe_file Path to BEDPE file or data.table/data.frame
#' @param zero_based Logical, whether input is 0-based (default: TRUE)
#' @param keep_metadata Logical, whether to preserve metadata (default: TRUE)
#' @return GInteractions object
bedpe_to_gi <- function(bedpe_file, zero_based = TRUE, keep_metadata = TRUE) {
  
  require(plyinteractions)
  require(InteractionSet)
  require(data.table)
  
  # Read file
  if(is.character(bedpe_file)) {
    message("Reading BEDPE file: ", bedpe_file)
    bedpe_dt <- fread(bedpe_file, header = TRUE)
  } else {
    bedpe_dt <- as.data.table(bedpe_file)
  }
  
  # Dynamic column detection for breakpoint 1
  chr1_col <- grep("^([Cc]hr1)|([Cc]hrom1)|(seqnames1)|(#chr1)", colnames(bedpe_dt), value = TRUE)[1]
  start1_col <- grep("^[Ss]tart1", colnames(bedpe_dt), value = TRUE)[1]
  end1_col <- grep("^[Ee]nd1", colnames(bedpe_dt), value = TRUE)[1]
  strand1_col <- grep("^[Ss]trand1", colnames(bedpe_dt), value = TRUE)[1]
  
  # Dynamic column detection for breakpoint 2
  chr2_col <- grep("^([Cc]hr2)|([Cc]hrom2)|(seqnames2)", colnames(bedpe_dt), value = TRUE)[1]
  start2_col <- grep("^[Ss]tart2", colnames(bedpe_dt), value = TRUE)[1]
  end2_col <- grep("^[Ee]nd2", colnames(bedpe_dt), value = TRUE)[1]
  strand2_col <- grep("^[Ss]trand2", colnames(bedpe_dt), value = TRUE)[1]
  
  # Check required columns
  missing_cols <- character()
  if(is.na(chr1_col)) missing_cols <- c(missing_cols, "chr1/chrom1/seqnames1")
  if(is.na(start1_col)) missing_cols <- c(missing_cols, "start1")
  if(is.na(end1_col)) missing_cols <- c(missing_cols, "end1")
  if(is.na(chr2_col)) missing_cols <- c(missing_cols, "chr2/chrom2/seqnames2")
  if(is.na(start2_col)) missing_cols <- c(missing_cols, "start2")
  if(is.na(end2_col)) missing_cols <- c(missing_cols, "end2")
  
  if(length(missing_cols) > 0) {
    stop("Missing required columns: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
  }
  
  # Standardize column names for plyinteractions
  setnames(bedpe_dt,
           c(chr1_col, start1_col, end1_col, chr2_col, start2_col, end2_col),
           c("seqnames1", "start1", "end1", "seqnames2", "start2", "end2"),
           skip_absent = TRUE)
  
  # Handle strand columns
  if(!is.na(strand1_col)) {
    setnames(bedpe_dt, strand1_col, "strand1", skip_absent = TRUE)
  } else {
    bedpe_dt[, strand1 := "*"]
  }
  
  if(!is.na(strand2_col)) {
    setnames(bedpe_dt, strand2_col, "strand2", skip_absent = TRUE)
  } else {
    bedpe_dt[, strand2 := "*"]
  }
  
  # Convert "." to "*" for strand
  bedpe_dt[strand1 == ".", strand1 := "*"]
  bedpe_dt[strand2 == ".", strand2 := "*"]
  
  # Convert to 1-based if input is 0-based
  if(zero_based) {
    bedpe_dt[, start1 := start1 + 1]
    bedpe_dt[, start2 := start2 + 1]
  }
  
  # Convert to character
  bedpe_dt[, seqnames1 := as.character(seqnames1)]
  bedpe_dt[, seqnames2 := as.character(seqnames2)]
  
  # Identify metadata columns
  core_cols <- c("seqnames1", "start1", "end1", "strand1",
                 "seqnames2", "start2", "end2", "strand2")
  metadata_cols <- setdiff(colnames(bedpe_dt), core_cols)
  
  # Create GInteractions using plyinteractions
  if(keep_metadata && length(metadata_cols) > 0) {
    gi <- bedpe_dt %>%
      plyinteractions::as_ginteractions(
        seqnames1 = seqnames1,
        start1 = start1,
        end1 = end1,
        strand1 = strand1,
        seqnames2 = seqnames2,
        start2 = start2,
        end2 = end2,
        strand2 = strand2,
        keep.extra.columns = TRUE
      )
  } else {
    gi <- bedpe_dt %>%
      dplyr::select(all_of(core_cols)) %>%
      plyinteractions::as_ginteractions(
        seqnames1 = seqnames1,
        start1 = start1,
        end1 = end1,
        strand1 = strand1,
        seqnames2 = seqnames2,
        start2 = start2,
        end2 = end2,
        strand2 = strand2
      )
  }
  
  message("Created GInteractions with ", length(gi), " interactions")
  
  return(gi)
}

#' Sort BEDPE data.table by genomic coordinates
#'
#' @param bedpe_dt data.table in BEDPE format
#' @return Sorted data.table
sort_bedpe <- function(bedpe_dt) {
  
  # Detect chromosome column names
  chr1_col <- grep("^([Cc]hr1)|([Cc]hrom1)", colnames(bedpe_dt), value = TRUE)[1]
  chr2_col <- grep("^([Cc]hr2)|([Cc]hrom2)", colnames(bedpe_dt), value = TRUE)[1]
  
  if(is.na(chr1_col) || is.na(chr2_col)) {
    warning("Cannot find chromosome columns for sorting")
    return(bedpe_dt)
  }
  
  # Helper function to extract numeric chromosome value
  extract_chr_number <- function(chr_vec) {
    chr_clean <- stringr::str_remove(chr_vec, "^chr")
    chr_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(chr_clean))
    
    chr_num[chr_clean == "X"] <- 23
    chr_num[chr_clean == "Y"] <- 24
    chr_num[chr_clean %in% c("M", "MT")] <- 25
    
    remaining_na <- is.na(chr_num)
    if(any(remaining_na)) {
      unique_other <- unique(chr_clean[remaining_na])
      chr_num[remaining_na] <- 26 + match(chr_clean[remaining_na], unique_other)
    }
    
    return(chr_num)
  }
  
  # Add sorting columns
  bedpe_dt[, chr1_sort := extract_chr_number(get(chr1_col))]
  bedpe_dt[, chr2_sort := extract_chr_number(get(chr2_col))]
  
  # Sort
  setorderv(bedpe_dt, c("chr1_sort", "start1", "end1", "chr2_sort", "start2", "end2"))
  
  # Remove sorting columns
  bedpe_dt[, c("chr1_sort", "chr2_sort") := NULL]
  
  return(bedpe_dt)
}


#' Convert GInteractions to GenomicRangesList
#'
#' @param gi GInteractions object
#' @param keep_metadata Logical, whether to preserve metadata (default: TRUE)
#' @return GenomicRangesList where each element contains a pair of breakpoints
gi_to_grl <- function(gi, keep_metadata = TRUE) {
  
  require(InteractionSet)
  require(GenomicRanges)
  
  if(!is(gi, "GInteractions")) {
    stop("Input must be a GInteractions object")
  }
  
  # Extract anchors (breakpoints)
  anchor1 <- anchors(gi, type = "first")
  anchor2 <- anchors(gi, type = "second")
  
  # Add metadata to anchors if requested
  if(keep_metadata && length(mcols(gi)) > 0) {
    mcols(anchor1) <- mcols(gi)
    mcols(anchor2) <- mcols(gi)
  }
  
  # Create list of GRanges pairs
  grl_list <- vector("list", length(gi))
  
  for(i in seq_along(gi)) {
    grl_list[[i]] <- c(anchor1[i], anchor2[i])
    if(i %% 100 == 0) { cli::cli_alert_info("Done ... {i}") }
  }
  
  # Convert to GRangesList
  grl <- GRangesList(grl_list)
  
  # Preserve names if present
  if(!is.null(names(gi))) {
    names(grl) <- names(gi)
  } else {
    names(grl) <- paste0("pair_", seq_along(grl))
  }
  
  message("Created GRangesList with ", length(grl), " breakpoint pairs")
  
  return(grl)
}

#' Convert GenomicRangesList to GInteractions
#'
#' @param grl GenomicRangesList where each element contains exactly 2 ranges
#' @param keep_metadata Logical, whether to preserve metadata (default: TRUE)
#' @return GInteractions object
grl_to_gi <- function(grl, keep_metadata = TRUE) {
  
  require(GenomicRanges)
  require(InteractionSet)
  
  if(!is(grl, "GRangesList")) {
    stop("Input must be a GenomicRangesList object")
  }
  
  # Check that all elements have exactly 2 ranges
  pair_lengths <- lengths(grl)
  if(!all(pair_lengths == 2)) {
    warning("Some elements do not contain exactly 2 ranges. These will be skipped.")
    grl <- grl[pair_lengths == 2]
  }
  
  if(length(grl) == 0) {
    stop("No valid breakpoint pairs found (each element must contain exactly 2 ranges)")
  }
  
  # Extract first and second breakpoints
  anchor1_list <- vector("list", length(grl))
  anchor2_list <- vector("list", length(grl))
  
  for(i in seq_along(grl)) {
    gr_pair <- grl[[i]]
    anchor1_list[[i]] <- gr_pair[1]
    anchor2_list[[i]] <- gr_pair[2]
  }
  
  # Combine into GRanges objects
  anchor1 <- do.call(c, anchor1_list)
  anchor2 <- do.call(c, anchor2_list)
  
  # Create GInteractions
  gi <- GInteractions(anchor1, anchor2)
  
  # Add metadata from first anchor if requested
  # (assumes metadata is shared between both anchors)
  if(keep_metadata && length(mcols(anchor1)) > 0) {
    mcols(gi) <- mcols(anchor1)
  }
  
  # Preserve names
  if(!is.null(names(grl))) {
    names(gi) <- names(grl)
  }
  
  message("Created GInteractions with ", length(gi), " interactions")
  
  return(gi)
}

#' Complete conversion: BEDPE -> GInteractions -> GRangesList
#'
#' @param bedpe_file Path to BEDPE file or data.table/data.frame
#' @param zero_based Logical, whether input is 0-based (default: TRUE)
#' @param keep_metadata Logical, whether to preserve metadata (default: TRUE)
#' @return GenomicRangesList
bedpe_to_grl <- function(bedpe_file, zero_based = TRUE, keep_metadata = TRUE) {
  
  # First convert to GInteractions
  gi <- bedpe_to_gi(bedpe_file, zero_based = zero_based, keep_metadata = keep_metadata)
  
  # Then convert to GRangesList
  grl <- gi_to_grl(gi, keep_metadata = keep_metadata)
  
  return(grl)
}

#' Complete conversion: GRangesList -> GInteractions -> BEDPE
#'
#' @param grl GenomicRangesList object
#' @param include_metadata Logical, whether to include metadata (default: TRUE)
#' @param zero_based Logical, whether to use 0-based coordinates (default: TRUE)
#' @param sort_output Logical, whether to sort output (default: TRUE)
#' @return data.table in BEDPE format
grl_to_bedpe <- function(grl, include_metadata = TRUE, zero_based = TRUE, sort_output = TRUE) {
  
  # First convert to GInteractions
  gi <- grl_to_gi(grl, keep_metadata = include_metadata)
  
  # Then convert to BEDPE
  bedpe <- gi_to_bedpe(gi, include_metadata = include_metadata, 
                       zero_based = zero_based, sort_output = sort_output)
  
  return(bedpe)
}



###############################################################################
# Non-chrom based parallelization

#' @name chunkpar
#' @title Scaffold function to run workflows in parallel by chunk size
#'
#' @description
#' A generalizable scaffold function that provides easy API to `easypar` functionality
#' and executes a function in parallel by splitting input iterations into chunks.
#'
#' This function is useful for parallelizing any iterative workflow where you want to
#' process a large number of items (e.g., rows, samples, files) in parallel batches.
#'
#' @param par_function The function that will be executed in parallel, written
#'  as the function name without the `()` at the end. This function should accept
#'  a vector of indices as its first argument.
#' @param n_iterations Total number of iterations to process (e.g., number of rows,
#'  samples, or files)
#' @param chunk_size Number of iterations to process per parallel job. If NULL,
#'  will automatically determine optimal chunk size based on n_iterations and par_cpus
#' @param ... Additional input parameters that will be passed to `par_function` for
#'  execution (after the index vector)
#' @param run_in_par Run the jobs in parallel and not sequentially, default: TRUE
#' @param par_cpus Number of CPUs to use for parallel execution. Default NULL will
#'  use 80% of available cores
#' @param par_packages Vector of character strings designating special packages needed
#'  for executing the `par_function`
#' @param output_type Type of output to expect: "data.table", "granges", "list", or "custom"
#'  Default: "data.table"
#' @param combine_function Custom function to combine outputs if output_type = "custom"
#'
#' @details
#' The function splits n_iterations into chunks and passes a vector of indices to
#' par_function for each chunk. Your par_function should be written to accept an
#' index vector as its first argument and process those indices.
#'
#' @examples
#' # Example 1: Process mutation records in chunks
#' # Assuming you have a function that processes a subset of rows:
#' # process_mutations <- function(row_indices, mutation_dt, ...) {
#' #   result <- mutation_dt[row_indices, ]
#' #   # ... do processing ...
#' #   return(result)
#' # }
#' #
#' # results <- chunkpar(
#' #   par_function = process_mutations,
#' #   n_iterations = nrow(mutation_dt),
#' #   chunk_size = 1000,
#' #   par_cpus = 8,
#' #   mutation_dt = mutation_dt
#' # )
#'
#' # Example 2: Process VCF files in chunks
#' # vcf_results <- chunkpar(
#' #   par_function = process_vcf_batch,
#' #   n_iterations = length(vcf_files),
#' #   chunk_size = 50,
#' #   par_cpus = 4,
#' #   vcf_files = vcf_files,
#' #   output_type = "granges"
#' # )
#'
#' @returns Combined output based on output_type: data.table, GRanges, or custom
#' @export
#' @keywords core
chunkpar <- function(par_function, 
                     n_iterations, 
                     chunk_size = NULL,
                     ..., 
                     run_in_par = TRUE, 
                     par_cpus = NULL, 
                     par_packages = NULL,
                     output_type = c("data.table", "granges", "list", "custom"),
                     combine_function = NULL) {
  
  require(easypar)
  require(data.table)
  require(cli)
  
  # Match output type
  output_type <- match.arg(output_type)
  
  # Determine number of cores
  available_cores <- parallel::detectCores()
  if(!is.null(par_cpus)) {
    cores_to_use <- min(par_cpus, available_cores)
    par_cores_ratio <- cores_to_use / available_cores
  } else {
    par_cores_ratio <- 0.8
    cores_to_use <- floor(available_cores * par_cores_ratio)
  }
  
  # Auto-determine chunk size if not provided
  if(is.null(chunk_size)) {
    # Aim for at least 2 chunks per core, but no more than 100 chunks total
    optimal_chunks <- min(cores_to_use * 2, 100)
    chunk_size <- max(1, ceiling(n_iterations / optimal_chunks))
  }
  
  # Create chunks
  chunk_starts <- seq(1, n_iterations, by = chunk_size)
  chunk_ends <- c(chunk_starts[-1] - 1, n_iterations)
  n_chunks <- length(chunk_starts)
  
  # Function CLI
  cli::cli_alert_info(
    text = "Launching {.emph {.pkg {as.character(substitute(par_function))}()}} in parallel"
  )
  cli::cli_text("  Total iterations: {.strong {n_iterations}}")
  cli::cli_text("  Chunk size: {.strong {chunk_size}}")
  cli::cli_text("  Number of chunks: {.strong {n_chunks}}")
  cli::cli_text("  Cores to use: {.strong {cores_to_use}}")
  cat("\n")
  cli::cli_text("****************")
  cli::cli_ul(paste0("Chunk ", 1:n_chunks, ": indices ", chunk_starts, "-", chunk_ends), .close = TRUE)
  cli::cli_text("****************")
  cat("\n")
  
  # Build the parameters for the parallel run
  # Each parameter set includes the index range for that chunk
  par_params <- list()
  for(i in 1:n_chunks) {
    chunk_indices <- chunk_starts[i]:chunk_ends[i]
    par_params[[i]] <- c(list(chunk_indices), list(...))
  }
  
  # Run easypar by chunks
  cli::cli_alert_info("Executing parallel jobs {crayon::white(clisymbols::symbol$ellipsis)}")
  par_out_list <- easypar::run(
    FUN = par_function, 
    PARAMS = par_params, 
    parallel = run_in_par, 
    cores.ratio = par_cores_ratio,
    outfile = NULL, 
    progress_bar = FALSE, 
    silent = FALSE, 
    packages = par_packages, 
    filter_errors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Combine the output based on type
  cat("\n")
  cli::cli_alert_success(
    "Parallel jobs completed. Combining {n_chunks} chunk outputs {crayon::white(clisymbols::symbol$ellipsis)}"
  )
  
  par_out <- switch(
    output_type,
    "data.table" = {
      combined <- data.table::rbindlist(par_out_list, fill = TRUE)
      cli::cli_alert_success("Combined into data.table with {nrow(combined)} rows")
      combined
    },
    "granges" = {
      require(GenomicRanges)
      combined_dt <- data.table::rbindlist(par_out_list, fill = TRUE)
      par_out_gr <- dt_to_gr(combined_dt)
      par_out_gr <- GenomicRanges::sort(par_out_gr)
      cli::cli_alert_success("Combined into sorted GRanges with {length(par_out_gr)} ranges")
      par_out_gr
    },
    "list" = {
      cli::cli_alert_success("Returning list of {length(par_out_list)} chunk outputs")
      par_out_list
    },
    "custom" = {
      if(is.null(combine_function)) {
        cli::cli_alert_warning("No combine_function provided. Returning raw list.")
        par_out_list
      } else {
        combined <- do.call(combine_function, list(par_out_list))
        cli::cli_alert_success("Combined using custom function")
        combined
      }
    }
  )
  
  return(par_out)
}



# ============================================================================
# SigProfilerExtractor Decomposition Visualization Workflow
# ============================================================================

parse_decomposition_csv <- function(csv_file, sig_type = NULL) {
  if(is.null(sig_type) || !sig_type %in% c("SBS","ID")) {
    stop(cli::cli_alert_danger("Signature type must be either: `SBS` or `ID`"))
  }
  
  # Read the CSV file
  decomp_data <- read.csv(csv_file, header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  
  # Extract signature name
  if(sig_type == "SBS") {
    sig_name <- stringr::str_replace(
      string = decomp_data$De.novo.extracted,
      pattern = "96-",
      replacement = "SBS96"
    )
  } else if(sig_type == "ID") {
    sig_name <- stringr::str_replace(
      string = decomp_data$De.novo.extracted,
      pattern = "83-",
      replacement = "ID83"
    )
  }
  
  # Extract contributing COSMIC signatures and percentages
  global_sigs_line <- decomp_data$Global.NMF.Signatures
  
  # Parse individual signatures (format: "Signature ID1 (19.56%)" | Signature SBS1 (19.56%))
  sig_matches <- stringr::str_match_all(
    string = global_sigs_line, 
    pattern = "Signature (\\w+\\d+) \\(([0-9.]+)%\\)"
  )[[1]]
  
  cosmic_signatures <- data.frame(
    "signature" = sig_matches[, 2],
    "percentage" = as.numeric(sig_matches[, 3]),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Extract quality metrics
  metrics <- list(
    "l1_error" = as.numeric(decomp_data$L1.Error..),
    "l2_error" = as.numeric(decomp_data$L2.Error..),
    "kl_divergence" = as.numeric(decomp_data$KL.Divergence),
    "cosine_similarity" = as.numeric(decomp_data$Cosine.Similarity),
    "correlation" = as.numeric(decomp_data$Correlation)
  )
  
  return(list(
    "sig_name" = sig_name,
    "cosmic_signatures" = cosmic_signatures,
    "metrics" = metrics
  ))
}

# ============================================================================
# 2. Parse De Novo Signature Data (ID83 format)
# ============================================================================

parse_denovo_signature <- function(txt_file) {
  sig_data <- read.delim(txt_file, header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  
  # Ensure proper column naming
  colnames(sig_data) <- c("MutationType", "proportion")
  
  # Set factor levels to maintain order
  sig_data$MutationType <- factor(sig_data$MutationType, 
                                   levels = sig_data$MutationType)
  
  return(sig_data)
}

# ============================================================================
# 3. Define ID83 Color Scheme
# ============================================================================

get_signature_colors <- function(sig_type = NULL) {
  if(is.null(sig_type) || !sig_type %in% c("SBS","ID")) {
    stop(cli::cli_alert_danger("Signature type must be either: `SBS` or `ID`"))
  }
  
  # SBS96 Single-Base Substitution Colors
  if(sig_type == "SBS") {
    colors <- list(
      "C>A" = "#5ABCEB",
      "C>G" = "#050708",
      "C>T" = "#D33C32",
      "T>A" = "#CBCACB",
      "T>C" = "#ABCD72",
      "T>G" = "#E7C9C6"
    )
  }
  
  # ID83 mutation categories and their color-blind adjusted colors
  if(sig_type == "ID") {
    colors <- list(
      "1:Del:C" = "#FF50FF",
      "1:Del:T" = "#860086",
      "1:Ins:C" = "#AACF8E",
      "1:Ins:T" = "#35A02E",
      "2:Del:R" = "#E5B17E",
      "3:Del:R" = "#CC9B7A",
      "4:Del:R" = "#996035",
      "5:Del:R" = "#662F00",
      "2:Ins:R" = "#CFE0F0",
      "3:Ins:R" = "#94C3DF",
      "4:Ins:R" = "#4A98C8",
      "5:Ins:R" = "#1665AA",
      "2:Del:M" = "#E1E1EE",
      "3:Del:M" = "#B5B5D8",
      "4:Del:M" = "#8683BC",
      "5:Del:M" = "#624099"
    )
  }
  
  return(colors)
}


assign_signature_colors <- function(mutation_types, sig_type = NULL) {
  if(is.null(sig_type) || !sig_type %in% c("SBS","ID")) {
    stop(cli::cli_alert_danger("Signature type must be either: `SBS` or `ID`"))
  }
  
  # Get the appropriate color map
  color_map <- get_signature_colors(sig_type = sig_type)
  
  # SBS96 Single Base Substitution Color Assignment
  if(sig_type == "SBS") {
    # Extract substitution type from trinucleotide context
    # Format: "A[C>A]A" -> "C>A" or "ACA>AAA" -> extract middle base change
    categories <- sapply(mutation_types, function(mt) {
      mt_str <- as.character(mt)
      match <- regmatches(mt_str, regexpr("[ACGT]>[ACGT]", mt_str))
      return(match)
    })
  }
  
  # ID83 Indel Signature Color Assignment
  if(sig_type == "ID") {
    # Extract category from mutation type (e.g., "1:Del:C:0" -> "1:Del:C")
    categories <- sapply(mutation_types, function(mt) {
      parts <- strsplit(as.character(mt), ":")[[1]]
      paste(parts[1:3], collapse = ":")
    })
  }
  
  colors <- sapply(categories, function(cat) {
      return(color_map[[cat]])
  })
  
  return(unname(colors))
}

# Helper function to generate full SBS96 color vector (for bar plots)
get_sbs96_color_vector <- function() {
  # Returns a vector of 96 colors in standard trinucleotide order
  # 16 contexts per substitution type
  color_map <- get_signature_colors("SBS")
  
  col_96 <- c(
    rep(color_map[["C>A"]], 16),
    rep(color_map[["C>G"]], 16),
    rep(color_map[["C>T"]], 16),
    rep(color_map[["T>A"]], 16),
    rep(color_map[["T>C"]], 16),
    rep(color_map[["T>G"]], 16)
  )
  
  return(col_96)
}

# ============================================================================
# 4. Plot ID83 De Novo Signature
# ============================================================================

plot_id83 <- function(sig_data, title = "De Novo Signature", text_annotate = TRUE) {
  
  # Assign colors based on mutation type
  bar_colors <- assign_signature_colors(mutation_types = sig_data$MutationType, sig_type = "ID")
  
  # Create category labels for grouping
  sig_data$category <- sapply(as.character(sig_data$MutationType), function(mt) {
    parts <- strsplit(mt, ":")[[1]]
    paste(parts[1:3], collapse = ":")
  })
  
  # Create x-axis label based on homopolymer length / number of repeat units / microhomology length
  sig_data$x_axis_labels <- sapply(as.character(sig_data$MutationType), function(mt) {
    parts <- strsplit(mt, ":")[[1]]
    label <- ifelse(test = parts[4] == "5",
                    yes = "5+",
                    no = parts[4])
    return(label)
  })
  
  # Create the plot
  p <- ggplot2::ggplot(sig_data, aes(x = MutationType, y = proportion)) +
    ggplot2::geom_col(fill = bar_colors, width = 0.8) +
    ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(
      expand = c(0, 0),
      limits = c(0, max(sig_data$proportion) * 1.25),
      labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)
    ) +
    ggplot2::scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0, 0), labels = sig_data$x_axis_labels) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = title,
      x = "Homopolymer Length | Number of Repeat Units | Microhomology Length",
      y = "Percentage of InDels"
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
    ggplot2::theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 12),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 5),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5)
    )
  
  # Add category header bars
  color_map <- get_signature_colors(sig_type = "ID")
  categories <- unique(sig_data$category)
  
  # Calculate positions for category headers
  cat_positions <- sig_data %>%
    dplyr::mutate(idx = row_number()) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(category) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(
      xmin = min(idx) - 0.5,
      xmax = max(idx) + 0.5,
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  ymax <- max(sig_data$proportion) * 1.25
  ymin_rect <- max(sig_data$proportion) * 1.15
  
  # Labels for categories
  cat_labels <- c("C","T","C","T","2","2","2","3","3","3","4","4","4","5+","5+","5+")
  cat_label_cols <- c("black","white","black","white",rep("black",6),rep("white",6))
  cat_label_x_pos <- ((cat_positions$xmax - cat_positions$xmin) / 2) + cat_positions$xmin
  cat_label_y_pos <- ((ymax - ymin_rect) / 2) + ymin_rect
  
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(cat_positions))) {
    cat <- cat_positions$category[i]
    fill_col <- ifelse(cat %in% names(color_map), color_map[[cat]], "#808080")
    
    p <- p + ggplot2::annotate(
      "rect",
      xmin = cat_positions$xmin[i],
      xmax = cat_positions$xmax[i],
      ymin = ymin_rect,
      ymax = ymax,
      fill = fill_col
    )
    
    # Add the informative annotation labels if space available
    if(text_annotate) {
      p <- p + ggplot2::annotate(
        geom = "text",
        x = cat_label_x_pos[i],
        y = cat_label_y_pos,
        label = cat_labels[i],
        size = 2.25,
        color = cat_label_cols[i]
      )
    }
  }
  
  # Add the informative annotation labels if space available
  if(text_annotate) {
    # Add broad category labels
    p <- p + ggplot2::annotate(
      geom = "text",
      x = c(6.5, 18.5, 36.5, 60, 78),
      y = ymin_rect * 0.97,
      label = c("1bp Deletion", "1bp Insertion", ">1bp Deletion at Repeats", ">1bp Insertion at Repeats", "Microhomology"),
      size = 2.5,
      color = "black"
    )
  }
  
  return(p)
}

# updated version of plot_sbs96
plot_sbs96_new <- function(sig_data, title = "De Novo Signature") {
  
  # =========================================================================
  # Sort mutation types into COSMIC standard order
  # Format: N[X>Y]N where X is ref base (C or T) and Y is alt base
  # Order: C>A, C>G, C>T, T>A, T>C, T>G (16 trinucleotide contexts each)
  # =========================================================================
  
  # Define the standard COSMIC SBS96 order
  bases <- c("A", "C", "G", "T")
  substitution_types <- c("C>A", "C>G", "C>T", "T>A", "T>C", "T>G")
  
  # Generate standard order
  standard_order <- c()
  for (sub in substitution_types) {
    ref_base <- substr(sub, 1, 1)
    for (five_prime in bases) {
      for (three_prime in bases) {
        context <- paste0(five_prime, "[", sub, "]", three_prime)
        standard_order <- c(standard_order, context)
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Reorder data to match standard order
  sig_data <- sig_data %>%
    dplyr::mutate(MutationType = as.character(MutationType)) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(match(MutationType, standard_order))
  
  # Set factor levels to maintain COSMIC standard order
  sig_data$MutationType <- factor(sig_data$MutationType, levels = standard_order)

  # Extract substitution type for color assignment
  sig_data$substitution <- sapply(as.character(sig_data$MutationType), function(mt) {
    # Extract "X>Y" from "N[X>Y]N" format
    match <- regmatches(mt, regexpr("[ACGT]>[ACGT]", mt))
    return(match)
  })
  
  # Get color map
  color_map <- get_signature_colors(sig_type = "SBS")
  
  # Assign colors based on substitution type
  bar_colors <- sapply(sig_data$substitution, function(sub) {
    return(color_map[[sub]])
  })
  
  # Calculate plot dimensions
  ymax <- max(sig_data$proportion, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.15
  ymin_rect <- max(sig_data$proportion, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.05
  p <- ggplot2::ggplot(sig_data, aes(x = MutationType, y = proportion)) +
    ggplot2::geom_col(fill = bar_colors, width = 0.8) +
    ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(
      expand = c(0, 0),
      limits = c(0, ymax),
      labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)
    ) +
    ggplot2::scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = title,
      x = "Trinucleotide Context",
      y = "Single-Base Substitutions (%)"
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
    ggplot2::theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 12),
      axis.text.x = element_text(
        angle = 90, 
        hjust = 1, 
        vjust = 0.5, 
        size = 3,
      ),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5)
    )
  
  # Add category header bars for the 6 substitution types
  text_colors <- c("black", "white", "black", "black", "black", "black")
  
  p <- p +
    ggplot2::annotate(
      geom = "rect",
      xmin = c(0.5, 16.5, 32.5, 48.5, 64.5, 80.5),
      xmax = c(16.5, 32.5, 48.5, 64.5, 80.5, 96.5),
      ymin = ymin_rect,
      ymax = ymax,
      fill = c(
        color_map[["C>A"]],
        color_map[["C>G"]],
        color_map[["C>T"]],
        color_map[["T>A"]],
        color_map[["T>C"]],
        color_map[["T>G"]]
      )
    ) +
    ggplot2::annotate(
      geom = "text",
      x = c(8, 24, 40, 56, 72, 88),
      y = (ymin_rect + ymax) / 2,
      label = substitution_types,
      size = 2.75,
      color = text_colors
    )
  
  return(p)
}


# ============================================================================
# 5. Create Metrics Annotation Panel
# ============================================================================

create_metrics_text <- function(metrics, cosmic_sigs) {
  # Format COSMIC signature contributions
  sig_text <- paste(
    sprintf("%s: %.2f%%", cosmic_sigs$signature, cosmic_sigs$percentage),
    collapse = "\n"
  )
  
  metrics_text <- paste0(
    "Decomposition to COSMIC:\n",
    sig_text, "\n\n",
    "Quality Metrics:\n",
    sprintf("Cosine Similarity: %.3f\n", metrics$cosine_similarity),
    sprintf("Correlation: %.3f\n", metrics$correlation),
    sprintf("L1 Error: %.1f%%\n", metrics$l1_error),
    sprintf("L2 Error: %.1f%%\n", metrics$l2_error),
    sprintf("KL Divergence: %.4f", metrics$kl_divergence)
  )
  
  return(metrics_text)
}

# ============================================================================
# 6. Fetch COSMIC Reference Signatures (ID83)
# ============================================================================

# You'll need COSMIC reference signatures - here's a helper to load them
# Download from: https://cancer.sanger.ac.uk/signatures/downloads/

load_cosmic_ref_signatures <- function(cosmic_file = NULL) {
  if (!is.null(cosmic_file) && file.exists(cosmic_file)) {
    cosmic_sigs <- read.delim(cosmic_file, header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    return(cosmic_sigs)
  } else {
    message("COSMIC signature reference file not found. Please download from COSMIC.")
    return(NULL)
  }
}

# ============================================================================
# 7. Main Visualization Function
# ============================================================================

# TODO: need to update this to run for both current signature types, ID (current) and SBS (not yet added)
create_decomposition_figure <- function(
    sig_type = NULL,
    denovo_txt,
    decomposition_csv,
    cosmic_reference = NULL
) {
  
  if(is.null(sig_type) || !sig_type %in% c("SBS","ID")) {
    stop(cli::cli_alert_danger("Signature type must be either: `SBS` or `ID`"))
  }
  
  # Parse input files
  decomp_info <- parse_decomposition_csv(decomposition_csv, sig_type)
  denovo_sig <- parse_denovo_signature(denovo_txt)
  
  cli::cli_alert_success(paste0("Parsed de novo signature: ", decomp_info$sig_name))
  cli::cli_alert_info("Contributing COSMIC signatures:\n")
  decomp_info$cosmic_signatures <- decomp_info$cosmic_signatures %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(percentage))
  print(decomp_info$cosmic_signatures)
  
  # Create de novo signature plot
  if(sig_type == "SBS") {
    denovo_plot <- plot_sbs96_new(
      denovo_sig, 
      title = paste("De Novo:", decomp_info$sig_name)
    )
  }
  
  if(sig_type == "ID") {
    denovo_plot <- plot_id83(
      denovo_sig, 
      title = paste("De Novo:", decomp_info$sig_name)
    )
  }
  
  # Create metrics annotation
  metrics_text <- create_metrics_text(
    decomp_info$metrics, 
    decomp_info$cosmic_signatures
  )
  
  # Create text grob for metrics
  metrics_grob <- ggplot2::ggplot() +
    ggplot2::annotate(
      "text",
      x = 0.5, y = 0.5,
      label = metrics_text,
      hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0.5,
      size = 3.5,
      family = "mono"
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_void() +
    ggplot2::labs(title = "Decomposition Metrics") +
    ggplot2::theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 11))
  
  # Create COSMIC signature plots if reference available
  cosmic_plots <- list()
  
  if (!is.null(cosmic_reference)) {
    cosmic_ref_sigs <- load_cosmic_ref_signatures(cosmic_file = cosmic_reference)
    
    for (i in seq_len(nrow(decomp_info$cosmic_signatures))) {
      sig_id <- decomp_info$cosmic_signatures$signature[i]
      sig_pct <- decomp_info$cosmic_signatures$percentage[i]
      
      if (sig_id %in% colnames(cosmic_ref_sigs)) {
        sig_data <- data.frame(
          "MutationType" = factor(cosmic_ref_sigs$MutationType, 
                                  levels = cosmic_ref_sigs$MutationType),
          "proportion" = cosmic_ref_sigs[[sig_id]]
        )
        
        if(sig_type == "SBS") {
          cosmic_plots[[i]] <- plot_sbs96_new(
            sig_data,
            title = sprintf("%s (%.1f%%)", sig_id, sig_pct)
          )
        }
        
        if(sig_type == "ID") {
          cosmic_plots[[i]] <- plot_id83(
            sig_data,
            title = sprintf("%s (%.1f%%)", sig_id, sig_pct),
            text_annotate = F
          )
        }
      }
    }
  } else {
    # Create placeholder plots showing signature names and contributions
    for (i in seq_len(nrow(decomp_info$cosmic_signatures))) {
      sig_id <- decomp_info$cosmic_signatures$signature[i]
      sig_pct <- decomp_info$cosmic_signatures$percentage[i]
      
      cosmic_plots[[i]] <- ggplot2::ggplot() +
        ggplot2::annotate(
          "text",
          x = 0.5, y = 0.5,
          label = sprintf("COSMIC %s\n%.1f%%", sig_id, sig_pct),
          size = 6, fontface = "bold"
        ) +
        ggplot2::theme_void() +
        ggplot2::theme(
          panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "black"),
          plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
        )
    }
  }
  
  # Combine plots using patchwork
  # Left side: de novo signature + metrics
  left_panel <- denovo_plot / metrics_grob + 
    patchwork::plot_layout(heights = c(0.45,0.55))
  
  # Right side: COSMIC signatures stacked
  if (length(cosmic_plots) > 0) {
    right_panel <- patchwork::wrap_plots(cosmic_plots, ncol = 1, axis_titles = "collect")
  } else {
    right_panel <- ggplot2::ggplot() + ggplot2::theme_void()
  }
  
  # Final combined figure
  final_plot <- left_panel | right_panel
  final_plot <- final_plot + 
    patchwork::plot_layout(widths = c(1, 1)) +
    patchwork::plot_annotation(
      title = "De Novo Signature Decomposition to COSMIC",
      theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"))
    )
  
  return(final_plot)
}
```

## Patients

First, some basics on number of patients/samples and coverage across the dataset
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

dataset_metadata <- read_metadata_file(metadata_file = paste0(base_data_path, "metadata.txt"))

# Create table with summary of dataset
total_dataset_summary <- summary_table(metadata = dataset_metadata)
# export as 5x10 landscape

# Calculate coverage metrics for summary table
meta_and_cov_df <- dataset_metadata %>%
  dplyr::group_by(dataset_id) %>%
  distinct(patient, coverage_normal, coverage_tumor) %>%
  reshape2::melt(id.vars = c("dataset_id","patient"))

total_dataset_coverage <- ggplot(meta_and_cov_df) +
  geom_jitter(aes(x = variable, y = value, fill = variable), shape = 21, na.rm = T) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(x = variable, y = value, fill = variable), position = "dodge", outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.6, na.rm = T) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.03, 0.03), breaks = c(0, 5, 30, 60, 90, round(max(meta_and_cov_df$value, na.rm = T), digits = 0)),
                     limits = c(0, round(max(meta_and_cov_df$value, na.rm = T), digits = 0) + 2)) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("N", "T")) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Sample", labels = c("Normal", "Tumor"), values = c("black", "#8000FF")) +
  labs(y = "Coverage") +
  facet_wrap(~dataset_id, nrow = 1) +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90"),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "gray75"),
        strip.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, size = 10),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white",color = "black", size = 1.5))
# Export at A5 portrait

# Ordered distribution of coverage
norm_cov_distro_plot <- coverage_bar_plotter(coverage_metrics = dataset_metadata, normal_or_tumor = "normal")

tumor_cov_distro_plot <- coverage_bar_plotter(coverage_metrics = dataset_metadata, normal_or_tumor = "tumor")

#norm_cov_distro_plot + tumor_cov_distro_plot
# export 4x10 landscape
```
