```{r}
  dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "SMM" & mut_type == "SNV") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(fdr) %>%
  dplyr::mutate("true_gene_label" = case_when(nearest_gene == "TSPOAP1" ~ "MIR142",
                                              nearest_gene == "CSDE1" ~ "NRAS",
                                              nearest_gene == "BLOC1S5-TXNDC5" ~ "TXNDC5",
```

**Supplementary Figure 17**

```{r}
#| label: supp-fig-17ab
#| fig-cap: "**a,b.**  Evaluation of enrichment per tile by fishHook across the genome in a quantile-quantile (Q-Q) plot stratified by disease stage from MGUS. The Q-Q plot depicts all hypothesis tile tested for enrichment of SNVs (left) and InDels (right) with tiles associated with the top 10 most significant genes (FDR<0.05) highlighted in cyan. The lambda in the bottom-right corner is the genomic inflation factor. MGUS, monoclonal gammopathy of undetermined significance; SMM, smoldering multiple myeloma; MM, multiple myeloma; RRMM, relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma."
#| fig-height: 4.5
#| fig-width: 8
#| out-width: "70%"

supp_fig17ab <- mgus_snv_fish_qq + mgus_indel_fish_qq + plot_layout(ncol = 2, axis_titles = "collect_y")

supp_fig17cd <- smm_snv_fish_qq + smm_indel_fish_qq + plot_layout(ncol = 2, axis_titles = "collect_y")

supp_fig17ef <- mm_snv_fish_qq + mm_indel_fish_qq + plot_layout(ncol = 2, axis_titles = "collect_y")

supp_fig17gh <- rrmm_snv_fish_qq + rrmm_indel_fish_qq + plot_layout(ncol = 2, axis_titles = "collect_y")

supp_fig17ab
```

fishHook manhattan plots
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

#############
# MGUS
# Get the top 25 labels of both SNV and InDels
mgus_fishhook <- dx_sushi_for_plot %>%
  dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "MGUS") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(fdr) %>%
  dplyr::mutate("true_gene_label" = case_when(nearest_gene == "TSPOAP1" ~ "MIR142",
                                              nearest_gene == "CSDE1" ~ "NRAS",
                                              nearest_gene == "BLOC1S5-TXNDC5" ~ "TXNDC5",
                                              nearest_gene == "FRMD8" ~ "NEAT1",
                                              nearest_gene == "BMP6" ~ "TXNDC5",
                                              .default = nearest_gene))

mgus_top_fishhook_genes <- mgus_fishhook %>%
  dplyr::filter(fdr < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
  dplyr::distinct() %>%
  dplyr::slice_head(n = 25) %>%
  purrr::as_vector() %>%
  as.character()

# Get the gene labels, only 1 for each set of top tiles
mgus_gene_manhattan_labs <- mgus_fishhook %>%
  dplyr::mutate("top_gene_idx" = !duplicated(true_gene_label)) %>%
  dplyr::reframe("label" = case_when(true_gene_label %in% mgus_top_fishhook_genes &
                                        top_gene_idx == T ~ true_gene_label,
                                     !true_gene_label %in% mgus_top_fishhook_genes ~ NA))

mgus_fishhook_manhattan <- ggplot2::ggplot(data = mgus_fishhook) +
                    scattermore::geom_scattermore(aes(x = start_abs_linear, y = -log10(fdr), color = mut_type), alpha = 0.5, pointsize = 3.5) +
                    ggrepel::geom_label_repel(aes(x = start_abs_linear, y = -log10(fdr), label = mgus_gene_manhattan_labs$label),
                                              max.overlaps = 100, min.segment.length = 0 ,na.rm = T) +
                    ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "darkred") +
                    ggplot2::labs(y = expression("-log"[10]*"("*italic("FDR")*")")) +
                    ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1, last(linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:22])),
                                                labels = names(hg_seqlengths()[1:22]),
                                                breaks = linearized_hg38_midpoint_breaks[1:22],
                                                expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
                    ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,max(-log10(mgus_fishhook$fdr)) + 3), expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
                    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(name = "Mutation Type", values = c("#191970","#EEA9B8")) +
                    ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect",
                                      xmin = c(1, linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:21]),
                                      xmax = linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:22],
                                      ymin = 0,
                                      ymax = max(-log10(mgus_fishhook$fdr)) + 3,
                                      alpha = 0.18,
                                      fill = linearized_hg38_strip_fill[1:22]) +
                    ggplot2::theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
                                   axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                                   axis.text.x = element_blank(),
                                   legend.position = "top",
                                   legend.text = element_text(size = 14)) +
                    ggplot2::guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3)))
```

## The landscape of the non-coding genome in myeloma is characterized by a discrete set of mutationally enriched regions, Part 2

Grab significant tiles and annotate by closest protein-coding / miRNA / lncRNA gene
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# To extract the list of genes targeted by the enrichment of non-coding mutations,
# first, subset to only tiles with FDR < 0.05 then sort by genomic coordinate
sig_snv_sushi <- snv_sushi_results[fdr < 0.05][order(seqnames, start)]
sig_indel_sushi <- indel_sushi_results[fdr < 0.05][order(seqnames, start)]

# How many final tiles
message("FDR Significant SNV enriched tiles: ", nrow(sig_snv_sushi))
message("FDR Significant InDel enriched tiles: ", nrow(sig_indel_sushi))


# Create a set of stitched overlapping tiles to reduce to consensus segments
stitched_ncsnv_er <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.reduce(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(sig_snv_sushi)))[,c()])
stitched_ncsnv_er$nearest_gene <- genes$gene_name[nearest(x = stitched_ncsnv_er,
                                                          subject = gUtils::gr.stripstrand(genes))]

stitched_ncindel_er <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.reduce(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(sig_indel_sushi)))[,c()])
stitched_ncindel_er$nearest_gene <- genes$gene_name[nearest(x = stitched_ncindel_er,
                                                            subject = gUtils::gr.stripstrand(genes))]
```

Now work with set of stitched tiles to assign single combined P-value and gene
per set of stitched regions
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# Need to find all tiles that were collapsed into each stitched region then compute
# combined p-value for each stitched enriched region
# Need to make sure use exact overlap and calculate the mean

# Lets loop through each stitched mutation enriched region and grab number of tiles and combined p-value
# SNVs

ncsnv_fish_data_per_site <- data.table::data.table("k_tiles" = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncsnv_er)),
                                                   "q_value" = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncsnv_er)),
                                                   "effect_size_mean" = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncsnv_er)),
                                                   "count_density_mean" = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncsnv_er)),
                                                   "pred_count_density_mean" = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncsnv_er)),
                                                   "eligible_size_mean" = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncsnv_er)))

for(i in 1:length(stitched_ncsnv_er)) {
  tile_set_to_stitch <- gr.findoverlaps(subject = stitched_ncsnv_er[i,],
                                        query = gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(sig_snv_sushi)),
                                        type = "within",
                                        qcol = c("fdr","effectsize","count.density","count.pred.density","eligible"))
  
  # Calculate the mean FDR p-value, effect size, count density, predicted count density and the eligible region
  new_qval <- poolr::bonferroni(p = tile_set_to_stitch$fdr)
  
  ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$k_tiles[i] <- length(tile_set_to_stitch)
  ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$q_value[i] <- as.numeric(new_qval$p)
  ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$effect_size_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$effectsize)
  ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$count_density_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$count.density)
  ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$pred_count_density_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$count.pred.density)
  ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$eligible_size_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$eligible)
}

# Add the final metadata to the consensus sites
stitched_ncsnv_er$k_tiles <- ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$k_tiles
stitched_ncsnv_er$q_value <- ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$q_value
stitched_ncsnv_er$effect_size_mean <- ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$effect_size_mean
stitched_ncsnv_er$count_density_mean <- ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$count_density_mean
stitched_ncsnv_er$pred_count_density_mean <- ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$pred_count_density_mean
stitched_ncsnv_er$eligible_size_mean <- ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$eligible_size_mean

# snv_k_and_p_dt <- data.table::data.table(k_tiles = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncsnv_er)),
#                                          p_combine = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncsnv_er)))
# for(i in 1:length(stitched_ncsnv_er)) {
#   tile_set_to_stitch <- gr.findoverlaps(subject = stitched_ncsnv_er[i,],
#                                         query = gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(sig_snv_sushi)),
#                                         type = "within",
#                                         qcol = "fdr")
#   
#   # now calculate the combined p-value using the Bonferroni method on the tile FDR adjusted p-values
#   new_combined_pval <- poolr::bonferroni(p = tile_set_to_stitch$fdr)
#   
#   snv_k_and_p_dt$k_tiles[i] <- length(tile_set_to_stitch)
#   snv_k_and_p_dt$p_combine[i] <- new_combined_pval$p
# }
# 
# stitched_ncsnv_er$k_tiles <- snv_k_and_p_dt$k_tiles
# stitched_ncsnv_er$p_combine <- snv_k_and_p_dt$p_combine
stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_uniq_id <- paste0("snv_er", seq(1,length(stitched_ncsnv_er)))

# InDels
ncindel_fish_data_per_site <- data.table::data.table("k_tiles" = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncindel_er)),
                                                 "q_value" = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncindel_er)),
                                                 "effect_size_mean" = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncindel_er)),
                                                 "count_density_mean" = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncindel_er)),
                                                 "pred_count_density_mean" = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncindel_er)),
                                                 "eligible_size_mean" = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncindel_er)))

for(i in 1:length(stitched_ncindel_er)) {
  tile_set_to_stitch <- gr.findoverlaps(subject = stitched_ncindel_er[i,],
                                        query = gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(sig_indel_sushi)),
                                        type = "within",
                                        qcol = c("fdr","effectsize","count.density","count.pred.density","eligible"))
  
  # Calculate the mean FDR p-value, effect size, count density, predicted count density and the eligible region
  new_qval <- poolr::bonferroni(p = tile_set_to_stitch$fdr)
  
  ncindel_fish_data_per_site$k_tiles[i] <- length(tile_set_to_stitch)
  ncindel_fish_data_per_site$q_value[i] <- as.numeric(new_qval$p)
  ncindel_fish_data_per_site$effect_size_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$effectsize)
  ncindel_fish_data_per_site$count_density_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$count.density)
  ncindel_fish_data_per_site$pred_count_density_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$count.pred.density)
  ncindel_fish_data_per_site$eligible_size_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$eligible)
}

# Add the final metadata to the consensus sites
stitched_ncindel_er$k_tiles <- ncindel_fish_data_per_site$k_tiles
stitched_ncindel_er$q_value <- ncindel_fish_data_per_site$q_value
stitched_ncindel_er$effect_size_mean <- ncindel_fish_data_per_site$effect_size_mean
stitched_ncindel_er$count_density_mean <- ncindel_fish_data_per_site$count_density_mean
stitched_ncindel_er$pred_count_density_mean <- ncindel_fish_data_per_site$pred_count_density_mean
stitched_ncindel_er$eligible_size_mean <- ncindel_fish_data_per_site$eligible_size_mean

# indel_k_and_p_dt <- data.table::data.table(k_tiles = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncindel_er)),
#                                            p_combine = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncindel_er)))
# for(i in 1:length(stitched_ncindel_er)) {
#   tile_set_to_stitch <- gr.findoverlaps(subject = stitched_ncindel_er[i,],
#                                         query = gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(sig_indel_sushi)),
#                                         type = "within",
#                                         qcol = "fdr")
#   
#   # now calculate the combined p-value using the Bonferroni method on the tile FDR adjusted p-values
#   new_combined_pval <- poolr::bonferroni(p = tile_set_to_stitch$fdr)
#   
#   indel_k_and_p_dt$k_tiles[i] <- length(tile_set_to_stitch)
#   indel_k_and_p_dt$p_combine[i] <- new_combined_pval$p
# }
# 
# stitched_ncindel_er$k_tiles <- indel_k_and_p_dt$k_tiles
# stitched_ncindel_er$p_combine <- indel_k_and_p_dt$p_combine
stitched_ncindel_er$mut_uniq_id <- paste0("indel_er", seq(1,length(stitched_ncindel_er)))
```

Calculate the MER occupancy by disease stage, translocation subtype, or admixture
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# What percentage of patients have mutations in these regions
ncsnv_per_ser <- gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = snv_fish,
                                         subject = stitched_ncsnv_er,
                                         qcol = c("Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode", "Tumor_Sample_Barcode", "VARIANT_CLASS", "Reference_Allele",
                                                  "Tumor_Seq_Allele2", "VAF", "Variant_Classification"),
                                         scol = c("nearest_gene", "mut_uniq_id"))
ncsnv_per_ser_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(ncsnv_per_ser)

ncsnv_per_ser_dt_by_X_dt <- ncsnv_per_ser_dt %>%
  dplyr::rename("sample" = Tumor_Sample_Barcode,
                "patient" = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>% dplyr::select(patient, sample, disease_stage, igh_translocation),
                   by = c("patient","sample"))


ncindels_per_ier <- gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = indel_fish,
                                            subject = stitched_ncindel_er,
                                            qcol = c("Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode", "Tumor_Sample_Barcode", "VARIANT_CLASS", "Reference_Allele",
                                                     "Tumor_Seq_Allele2", "VAF", "Variant_Classification"),
                                            scol = c("nearest_gene", "mut_uniq_id"))
ncindels_per_ier_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(ncindels_per_ier)

ncindels_per_ier_dt_by_X_dt <- ncindels_per_ier_dt %>%
  dplyr::rename("sample" = Tumor_Sample_Barcode,
                "patient" = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>% dplyr::select(patient, sample, disease_stage, igh_translocation),
                   by = c("patient","sample"))

# Need to calculate the number of unique patients mutated per type of enriched region
mut_patients_per_ser <- ncsnv_per_ser_dt[!duplicated(ncsnv_per_ser_dt[, Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode, by = .(mut_uniq_id)])][, .(Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode, mut_uniq_id)]

mut_patients_per_ser_count <- mut_patients_per_ser[,.(.N), by = .(mut_uniq_id)]
stitched_ncsnv_er$total_mut_patients <- mut_patients_per_ser_count$N

# Count up number of MGUS / SMM / MM / RRMM at each MER
ncsnv_per_ser_dt_by_dx <- mut_patients_per_ser %>%
  dplyr::rename("patient" = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>% dplyr::select(patient, disease_stage) %>% dplyr::distinct(),
                   by = "patient") %>%
  dplyr::group_by(mut_uniq_id,disease_stage) %>%
  dplyr::count() %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  reshape2::acast(formula = mut_uniq_id ~ disease_stage) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "mut_uniq_id") %>%
  tidyr::replace_na(list(MGUS = 0, SMM = 0, MM = 0, RRMM = 0))

ncsnv_per_ser_dt_by_trx <- mut_patients_per_ser %>%
  dplyr::rename("patient" = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>% dplyr::select(patient, igh_translocation) %>% dplyr::distinct(),
                   by = "patient") %>%
  dplyr::group_by(mut_uniq_id,igh_translocation) %>%
  dplyr::count() %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  reshape2::acast(formula = mut_uniq_id ~ igh_translocation) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "mut_uniq_id") %>%
  tidyr::replace_na(list(`t(11;14)` = 0, `t(11;14)` = 0, `t(4;14)` = 0, `t(14;16)` = 0, `t(14;20)` = 0,
                         `t(6;14)` = 0, `t(8;14)` = 0, `Not Detected` = 0))

ncsnv_per_ser_dt_by_admix <- mut_patients_per_ser %>%
  dplyr::rename("patient" = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>% dplyr::select(patient, super5_cluster_id) %>% dplyr::distinct(),
                   by = "patient") %>%
  dplyr::group_by(mut_uniq_id,super5_cluster_id) %>%
  dplyr::count() %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  reshape2::acast(formula = mut_uniq_id ~ super5_cluster_id) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "mut_uniq_id") %>%
  tidyr::replace_na(list(`EUR Dominant` = 0, `AFR Dominant` = 0, `AMR Dominant` = 0,
                         `EAS Dominant` = 0, `SAS Dominant` = 0, `Highly Admixed` = 0))

#ncsnv_per_ser_dt_by_dx %>% dplyr::filter(mut_uniq_id == "snv_er47")

# Add the counts of patients mutated per disease stage
sorted_snv_patient_count_by_dx <- gr2dt(stitched_ncsnv_er) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = ncsnv_per_ser_dt_by_dx, by = "mut_uniq_id")

stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_mgus_patients <- sorted_snv_patient_count_by_dx$MGUS
stitched_ncsnv_er$pct_mut_mgus_patients <- round(sorted_snv_patient_count_by_dx$MGUS / (hq_dataset_metadata %>%
                                                                        dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "MGUS") %>%
                                                                        dplyr::select(patient) %>%
                                                                        dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100,
                                                 digits = 1) 

stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_smm_patients <- sorted_snv_patient_count_by_dx$SMM
stitched_ncsnv_er$pct_mut_smm_patients <- round(sorted_snv_patient_count_by_dx$SMM / (hq_dataset_metadata %>%
                                                                        dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "SMM") %>%
                                                                        dplyr::select(patient) %>%
                                                                        dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100,
                                                 digits = 1) 

stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_mm_patients <- sorted_snv_patient_count_by_dx$MM
stitched_ncsnv_er$pct_mut_mm_patients <- round(sorted_snv_patient_count_by_dx$MM / (hq_dataset_metadata %>%
                                                                        dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "MM") %>%
                                                                        dplyr::select(patient) %>%
                                                                        dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100,
                                                 digits = 1) 

stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_rrmm_patients <- sorted_snv_patient_count_by_dx$RRMM
stitched_ncsnv_er$pct_mut_rrmm_patients <- round(sorted_snv_patient_count_by_dx$RRMM / (hq_dataset_metadata %>%
                                                                        dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "RRMM") %>%
                                                                        dplyr::select(patient) %>%
                                                                        dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100,
                                                 digits = 1) 

# Add the counts of patients mutated per translocation
sorted_snv_patient_count_by_trx <- gr2dt(stitched_ncsnv_er) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = ncsnv_per_ser_dt_by_trx, by = "mut_uniq_id")

                                                                        dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100,
                                                 digits = 1)

stitched_ncindel_er$mut_highly_admixed_patients <- sorted_indel_patient_count_by_admix$`Highly Admixed`
stitched_ncindel_er$pct_mut_highly_admixed_patients <- round(sorted_indel_patient_count_by_admix$`Highly Admixed` / (hq_dataset_metadata %>%
                                                                        dplyr::filter(super5_cluster_id == "Highly Admixed") %>%
                                                                        dplyr::select(patient) %>%
                                                                        dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100,
                                                 digits = 1)
```

Find all overlapping genes at each site
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# TODO: functionalize this annotation process
# loop through each range and output all genes that overlap each region
stitched_ncsnv_overlapping_genes <- data.table()
for(i in 1:length(stitched_ncsnv_er)) {
  per_mer_gene_overlap <- gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = stitched_ncsnv_er[i],
                                                  subject = complete_gene_segments_gr,
                                                  qcol = "mut_uniq_id",
                                                  scol = "gene_name")
  # Only use the unique set of annotations
  uniq_gene_overlap <- unique(per_mer_gene_overlap$gene_name)
  # String the genes together for overlap column
  stitched_ncsnv_overlapping_genes <- gUtils::rrbind(stitched_ncsnv_overlapping_genes,
                                                     data.table("mut_uniq_id" = stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_uniq_id[i],
                                                                "overlapping_genes" = stringr::str_c(uniq_gene_overlap, collapse = ";")))
}
stitched_ncsnv_er$overlapping_genes <- stitched_ncsnv_overlapping_genes$overlapping_genes

stitched_ncindel_overlapping_genes <- data.table()
for(i in 1:length(stitched_ncindel_er)) {
  per_mer_gene_overlap <- gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = stitched_ncindel_er[i],
                                                  subject = complete_gene_segments_gr,
                                                  qcol = "mut_uniq_id",
                                                  scol = "gene_name")
  # Only use the unique set of annotations
  uniq_gene_overlap <- unique(per_mer_gene_overlap$gene_name)
  # String the genes together for overlap column
  stitched_ncindel_overlapping_genes <- gUtils::rrbind(stitched_ncindel_overlapping_genes,
                                                     data.table("mut_uniq_id" = stitched_ncindel_er$mut_uniq_id[i],
                                                                "overlapping_genes" = stringr::str_c(uniq_gene_overlap, collapse = ";")))
}
stitched_ncindel_er$overlapping_genes <- stitched_ncindel_overlapping_genes$overlapping_genes
```

Combine the set of SNV/InDel enriched regions into a set of Mutation Enriched Regions (MERs)
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# Combine the two sets of enriched retions
mers <- gr2dt(gr_refactor_seqs(grbind(stitched_ncsnv_er, stitched_ncindel_er)))

# Sort by combined p-value and filter and then uniquely label each MER by ranking and calculate percentage of all patients with mutation in MER
mers <- mers %>%
          dplyr::arrange(q_value) %>%
          dplyr::mutate(pct_total_mut_patients = round(total_mut_patients / length(unique(snv_fish$Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode)), 
                                                   digits = 3) * 100,
                        mer_id = paste0("mer", seq(1,nrow(mers))))

# Print some metrics on MERs
message("total MERs: ",nrow(mers))
message("Total unique genes associated to MERs: ", length(unique(mers$nearest_gene)))

# How much of genome do MERs cover (look at redundant SNV+InDel MERs)
reduced_mers <- GenomicRanges::reduce(x = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(mers)))
message("total Genomic span of MERs in Mbp: ", sum(GenomicRanges::width(reduced_mers)) / 1e6)
message("Median span MERs in Kbp: ", median(mers$width) / 1e3)
message("Range span MERs in Kbp: ", range(mers$width)[1]/ 1e3, " to ", range(mers$width)[2]/ 1e3)

# SNV and InDel MER specifics
message("SNV enriched MERs: ", nrow(mers %>% dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(mut_uniq_id, pattern = "snv_"))))
message("InDel enriched MERs: ", nrow(mers %>% dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(mut_uniq_id, pattern = "indel_"))))

# After manual inspection of nearest protein coding genes and expanded overlapping genes
# we updated the assignment of genes for the following MERs
# MER12 - TSPOAP1          ====>   MIR142
# MER14 - CSDE1            ====>   NRAS
# MER36 - BLOC1S5-TXNDC5   ====>   TXNDC5
# MER37 - BLOC1S5-TXNDC5   ====>   TXNDC5
# MER83 - FRMD8            ====>   NEAT1
mers$mer_gene_label <- replace(x = mers$nearest_gene,
                               list = c(12,14,36,37,83),
                               values = c("MIR142","NRAS","TXNDC5","TXNDC5","NEAT1"))

# Redundant MERs
redundant_mers <- gr_refactor_seqs(gr.findoverlaps(query = stitched_ncindel_er,
                                                   subject = stitched_ncsnv_er,
                                                   qcol = c("q_value","mut_uniq_id")))
redundant_mers_dt <- gr2dt(redundant_mers)
redundant_mers_dt <- left_join(x = redundant_mers_dt, y = mers, by = "mut_uniq_id")

message("InDel MERs overlapping SNV MERs: ", nrow(redundant_mers_dt))
message("Overlap at these genes: ", paste0(redundant_mers_dt$mer_gene_label, collapse = ", "))


# Plot MERs across the genome using midpoint of each MER
mers_gr <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(mers))
mers_gr <- gr_absolute_ranges(mers_gr)

# TODO: add this functionality to gr_absolute_ranges
mer_abs_midpoints <- c()
for(i in 1:length(mers_gr)) {
  mer_abs_midpoints[i] <- elliplot::midpoint(c(mers_gr$start_abs_linear[i],
                                               mers_gr$end_abs_linear[i]))[[1]][2]
}
mers_gr$midpoint_abs_linear <- mer_abs_midpoints
mers_gr$mer_type <- case_when(stringr::str_detect(mers_gr$mut_uniq_id, pattern = "indel_") ~ "InDel",
                              stringr::str_detect(mers_gr$mut_uniq_id, pattern = "snv_") ~ "SNV")
mers <- gr2dt(mers_gr)
mers$mer_type <- factor(mers$mer_type,
                        levels = c("SNV", "InDel"))

# Label some MERs of interest
mer_of_interest_labels <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "moiLabels.txt"),
                                header = T)

mer_manhattan <- ggplot(data = mers) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(aes(x = midpoint_abs_linear, y = -log10(q_value), color = mer_type), alpha = 0.7, size = 4) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(aes(x = midpoint_abs_linear, y = -log10(q_value), label = mer_of_interest_labels$gene_name), max.overlaps = 100, min.segment.length = 0) +
  ggplot2::labs(y =  expression("-log"[10]*"("*italic("q-value")*")"),) +
  ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1, last(linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:22])),
                     labels = names(hg_seqlengths()[1:22]),
                     breaks = linearized_hg38_midpoint_breaks[1:22],
                     expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
  ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,max(-log10(mers$q_value)) + 3), expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
  ggplot2::scale_color_manual(name = "MER Type", values = c("#FB832D", "black")) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect",
                    xmin = c(1, linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:21]),
                    xmax = linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:22],
                    ymin = 0,
                    ymax = max(-log10(mers$q_value)) + 3,
                    alpha = 0.18,
                    fill = linearized_hg38_strip_fill[1:22]) +
  ggplot2::theme(axis.title.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 15),
                 axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                 axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, size = 11),
                 legend.position = "top",
                 legend.text = element_text(size = 13),
                 panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))
# export 6 x 11.69 landscape

# Write out final set of MERs
mer_cols <- c("seqnames", "start", "end", "width", "mer_id", "mer_gene_label", "mer_type", "k_tiles",
              "q_value", "effect_size_mean", "count_density_mean", "pred_count_density_mean",
              "eligible_size_mean", "total_mut_patients", "pct_total_mut_patients",
              "mut_mgus_patients", "pct_mut_mgus_patients", "mut_smm_patients", "pct_mut_smm_patients",
              "mut_mm_patients", "pct_mut_mm_patients", "mut_rrmm_patients", "pct_mut_rrmm_patients",
              "mut_t11_14_patients", "pct_mut_t11_14_patients", "mut_t4_14_patients",
              "pct_mut_t4_14_patients", "mut_t14_16_patients", "pct_mut_t14_16_patients",
              "mut_t14_20_patients", "pct_mut_t14_20_patients", "mut_t6_14_patients",
              "pct_mut_t6_14_patients", "mut_t8_14_patients", "pct_mut_t8_14_patients",
              "mut_trx_not_detected_patients", "pct_mut_trx_not_detected_patients",
              "mut_EUR_patients", "pct_mut_EUR_patients", "mut_AFR_patients", "pct_mut_AFR_patients",
              "mut_AMR_patients", "pct_mut_AMR_patients", "mut_EAS_patients", "pct_mut_EAS_patients",
              "mut_SAS_patients", "pct_mut_SAS_patients", "mut_highly_admixed_patients",
              "pct_mut_highly_admixed_patients", "nearest_gene", "overlapping_genes", "mut_uniq_id")

#data.table::fwrite(x = mers %>%
#                         dplyr::arrange(q_value) %>%
#                         dplyr::select(all_of(mer_cols)),
#                   file = "MERs.txt",
#                   sep = "\t",
#                   row.names = F,
#                   col.names = T)
```

## The mutational burden at MERs is increased in later disease stages and varies by ancestry

Visualize features of the MERs in different ways - SNVs
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# Collect all unique mutations per patient that fall within the MERs
muts_per_mer <- gr2dt(gr_refactor_seqs(grbind(ncsnv_per_ser, ncindels_per_ier)))

muts_per_mer <- muts_per_mer %>%
  dplyr::mutate("mut_type" = case_when(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV" ~ "SNVs",
                                       VARIANT_CLASS %in% c("insertion", "deletion") ~ "InDels"))

# Let's add more metadata to the mutations per mer
muts_per_mer_for_plot <- rrbind(ncsnv_per_ser_dt_by_X_dt,ncindels_per_ier_dt_by_X_dt) %>%
                          dplyr::left_join(y = mers[,c("mut_uniq_id","mer_id")], by = "mut_uniq_id") #%>%
                            #dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata, by = c("patient", "sample"))
#muts_per_mer_for_plot <- muts_per_mer %>%
#                            dplyr::rename("patient" = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode, "sample" = Tumor_Sample_Barcode) %>%
#                            dplyr::left_join(y = mers[,c("mut_uniq_id","mer_id")], by = "mut_uniq_id") %>%
#                            dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata, by = c("patient", "sample"))

# Number of total unique SNVs and InDels across MERs per patient
snv_per_mer <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
  dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV") %>%
  dplyr::group_by(patient) %>%
  dplyr::count() %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

indel_per_mer <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
  dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS %in% c("insertion","deletion")) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(patient) %>%
  dplyr::count() %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

##########
#  SNVs  #
##########
message("Total SNVs in MERs: ", muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
                                  dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV") %>%
                                  dplyr::count() %>%
                                  as.numeric())
message("Percentage of all SNVs in MERs: ", round(muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
                                                              dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV") %>%
                                                              dplyr::count() %>%
                                                              as.numeric() / length(snv_fish_final) * 100,
                                                            digits = 2))

message("Median of SNVs in MERs across patients: ", median(snv_per_mer$n))
message("Range of SNVs in MERs across patients: ", paste0(range(snv_per_mer$n)[1], "-", range(snv_per_mer$n)[2]))

##########
#  InDels  #
##########
message("Total InDels in MERs: ", muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
                                  dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS %in% c("insertion","deletion")) %>%
                                  dplyr::count() %>%
                                  as.numeric())
message("Percentage of all InDels in MERs: ", round(muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
                                                              dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS %in% c("insertion","deletion")) %>%
                                                              dplyr::count() %>%
                                                              as.numeric() / length(indel_fish_final) * 100,
                                                            digits = 2))

message("Median of SNVs in MERs across patients: ", median(indel_per_mer$n))
message("Range of SNVs in MERs across patients: ", paste0(range(indel_per_mer$n)[1], "-", range(indel_per_mer$n)[2]))

```

Look at the absolute burden of MERs per patient
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

snv_per_mer_plot <- ggplot(data = snv_per_mer) +
                      geom_violin(aes(y = n, x = "SNVs"), fill = "#FB832D", color = "#FB832D", width = 0.5, linewidth = 1) +
                      geom_boxplot(aes(y = n, x = "SNVs"), outliers = FALSE, width = 0.15, fill = "#FB832D", color = "gray99", linewidth = 1.8) +
                      labs(y = "Total SNV burden") +
                      theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                            axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
                            axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, size = 14),
                            panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1))
  
# Snake plot of unique MERs per patient
uniq_snv_mers_per_patient <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
                               dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV") %>%
                               dplyr::select(patient, mer_id) %>%
                               dplyr::group_by(patient) %>%
                               dplyr::count(mer_id)

uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count <- uniq_snv_mers_per_patient %>%
                                     dplyr::select(patient, mer_id) %>%
                                     dplyr::count() %>%
                                     dplyr::arrange(n)

uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count$patient <- factor(uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count$patient,
                                                  levels = c(uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count$patient))

snv_uniq_mer_plot <- ggplot(uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count) +
                      ggplot2::geom_point(aes(x = patient, y = n), shape = 21, color = "black", fill = "#FB832D", size = 3, alpha = 0.50) +
                      ggplot2::labs(x = "Patients",
                           y = "Unique MERs") +
                      ggplot2::scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.03,0.03)) +
                      ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.015,0.015)) +
                      ggplot2::theme(axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
                            axis.text.x = element_blank(),
                            panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1)) +
                      ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment",
                               x = nrow(uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count)/2 - 60,
                               xend = nrow(uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count)/2 + 60,
                               y = median(uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count$n),
                               yend = median(uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count$n),
                               color = "gray40",
                               linewidth = 4,
                               alpha = 0.9)

message("Range of Unique SNV MERs across patients: ", paste0(range(uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count$n)[1], "-", range(uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count$n)[2]))
message("Median of Unique SNV MERs across patients: ", median(uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count$n))

# Combine the two SNV MER metric plots
#snv_per_mer_plot + snv_uniq_mer_plot + patchwork::plot_layout(nrow = 1, widths = c(1,0.5))
# export 6 x 6 portrait
```

Look at the mutation burden across MERs by type and disease stage
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# Gather all SNVs in MERs per sample
complete_mer_snv_per_sample <- left_join(
  x = gr2dt(gr.findoverlaps(query = muts_in_hq_patients %Q% (VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV"),
                            subject = mers_gr %Q% (mer_type == "SNV"),
                            qcol = c("Reference_Allele","Tumor_Seq_Allele2","Tumor_Sample_Barcode","Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode"),
                            scol = c("mer_id","mer_gene_label"))) %>%
                                  dplyr::rename("sample" = Tumor_Sample_Barcode, "patient" = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode),
                            y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
                                  dplyr::select(patient,sample,disease_stage,age,sex,igh_translocation,coverage_normal,coverage_tumor,purity,ploidy,super5_cluster_id),
                            by = c("patient","sample")) %>%
    dplyr::select(-c(6,7)) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()
complete_mer_snv_per_sample$age <- as.numeric(complete_mer_snv_per_sample$age)

# Gather all InDels in MERs per sample
complete_mer_indel_per_sample <- left_join(
  x = gr2dt(gr.findoverlaps(query = muts_in_hq_patients %Q% (VARIANT_CLASS %in% c("insertion","deletion")),
                            subject = mers_gr %Q% (mer_type == "InDel"),
                            qcol = c("Reference_Allele","Tumor_Seq_Allele2","Tumor_Sample_Barcode","Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode"),
                            scol = c("mer_id","mer_gene_label"))) %>%
                                  dplyr::rename("sample" = Tumor_Sample_Barcode, "patient" = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode),
                            y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
                                  dplyr::select(patient,sample,disease_stage,age,sex,igh_translocation,coverage_normal,coverage_tumor,purity,ploidy,super5_cluster_id),
                            by = c("patient","sample"))%>%
    dplyr::select(-c(6,7)) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()
complete_mer_indel_per_sample$age <- as.numeric(complete_mer_indel_per_sample$age)

# Calculate the broad mutational burden separate here
######## SNVs #########
total_mer_snv_tmb <- complete_mer_snv_per_sample %>%
                      dplyr::filter(!is.na(age)) %>%
                      dplyr::group_by(sample,disease_stage,age,sex,igh_translocation,coverage_normal,coverage_tumor,purity,ploidy,super5_cluster_id) %>%
                      dplyr::count(name = "total_mutation_count") %>%
                      dplyr::ungroup() %>%
                      dplyr::mutate("tumor_mutation_burden" = total_mutation_count / (sum(GenomicRanges::width(mers_gr %Q% (mer_type == "SNV"))) / 1e6))

#common_mer_snv_tmb <- complete_mer_snv_per_sample %>%
#                      dplyr::filter(!is.na(age) & mer_id %in% common_snv_mers_all_dx$mer_id) %>%
#                      dplyr::group_by(sample,disease_stage,age,sex,igh_translocation,coverage_normal,coverage_tumor,purity,ploidy,super5_cluster_id) %>%
#                      dplyr::count(name = "total_mutation_count") %>%
#                      dplyr::ungroup() %>%
#                      dplyr::mutate("tumor_mutation_burden" = total_mutation_count / (sum(GenomicRanges::width(mers_gr %Q% (mer_id %in% common_snv_mers_all_dx$mer_id) %Q% (mer_type == "SNV"))) / 1e6))

######## InDels #########
total_mer_indel_tmb <- complete_mer_indel_per_sample %>%
                      dplyr::filter(!is.na(age)) %>%
                      dplyr::group_by(sample,disease_stage,age,sex,igh_translocation,coverage_normal,coverage_tumor,purity,ploidy,super5_cluster_id) %>%
                      dplyr::count(name = "total_mutation_count") %>%
                      dplyr::ungroup() %>%
                      dplyr::mutate("tumor_mutation_burden" = total_mutation_count / (sum(GenomicRanges::width(mers_gr %Q% (mer_type == "InDel"))) / 1e6))

#common_mer_indel_tmb <- complete_mer_indel_per_sample %>%
#                      dplyr::filter(!is.na(age) & mer_id %in% common_indel_mers_all_dx$mer_id) %>%
#                      dplyr::group_by(sample,disease_stage,age,sex,igh_translocation,coverage_normal,coverage_tumor,purity,ploidy,super5_cluster_id) %>%
#                      dplyr::count(name = "total_mutation_count") %>%
#                      dplyr::ungroup() %>%
#                      dplyr::mutate("tumor_mutation_burden" = total_mutation_count / (sum(GenomicRanges::width(mers_gr %Q% (mer_id %in% common_indel_mers_all_dx$mer_id) %Q% (mer_type == "InDel"))) / 1e6))

# Correct the TMB by age by fitting a linear model
test_total_snv_mer_tmb_lm <- lm(data = total_mer_snv_tmb,
                                 formula = tumor_mutation_burden ~ age + igh_translocation + purity + ploidy)
summary(test_total_snv_mer_tmb_lm)


total_snv_mer_tmb_lm <- lm(data = total_mer_snv_tmb,
                           formula = tumor_mutation_burden ~ purity + ploidy)
summary(total_snv_mer_tmb_lm)


#message("Testing linear regression of common MER SNV burden demographic / molecular covariates")
#test_common_snv_mer_tmb_lm <- lm(data = common_mer_snv_tmb,
#                                 formula = tumor_mutation_burden ~ age + igh_translocation + purity + ploidy)
#summary(test_common_snv_mer_tmb_lm)

#common_snv_mer_tmb_lm <- lm(data = common_mer_snv_tmb, #%>%
#                                     #dplyr::mutate("igh_trx" = case_when(igh_translocation == "Not Detected" ~ 1,
#                                     #                                    igh_translocation != "Not Detected" ~ 0)),
#             formula = tumor_mutation_burden ~ purity + ploidy)
#message("Final linear regression model of common MER SNV burden across disease stages")
#summary(common_snv_mer_tmb_lm)

# Plot the overall TMB by disease stage
total_mer_snv_tmb$tmb_corrected <- resid(total_snv_mer_tmb_lm) + mean(total_mer_snv_tmb$tumor_mutation_burden)

total_mer_snv_dx_median_tmb <- total_mer_snv_tmb %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
  dplyr::reframe("median_tmb" = median(na.omit(tmb_corrected)))

total_mer_snv_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output <- rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(data = droplevels(total_mer_snv_tmb %>%
                                                                                 dplyr::select(disease_stage, tmb_corrected)),
                                                             formula = tmb_corrected ~ disease_stage,
                                                             p.adjust.method = "fdr")
total_mer_snv_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(total_mer_snv_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output$p.adj)

total_mer_snv_tmb_by_dx <- ggplot(data = total_mer_snv_tmb, aes(x = disease_stage, y = tmb_corrected, fill = disease_stage, colour = disease_stage)) +
  geom_violin(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_boxplot(outliers = FALSE, width = 0.2, color = "white", linewidth = 1.1) +
  labs(y = "MER SNVs per Mb",
       x = NULL) +
  scale_y_log10(limits = c(1,100), labels = c("1.0", "10","100"), breaks = c(1.0, 10,100), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease\nStage", values = disease_stage_colors) +
  scale_color_manual(name = "Disease\nStage", values = disease_stage_colors) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, family = "sans", size = 14),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 15),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = alpha(col = "#FB832D", alpha = 0.2))) +
   ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = total_mer_snv_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output[c(4,5),],
                              label = "P={p_string}",
                              y.position = c(1.71,1.97),
                              tip.length = 0.008,
                              label.size = 4,
                              vjust = 0.1) +
   ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = total_mer_snv_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output[2,],
                              label = "{p.adj.signif}",
                              y.position = 1.85,
                              tip.length = 0.008,
                              label.size = 4,
                              vjust = 0.1) +
   ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = total_mer_snv_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output[c(1,6),],
                              label = "P={p.adj}",
                              y.position = c(1.55,1.78),
                              tip.length = 0.008,
                              label.size = 4,
                              vjust = 0.1)

test_total_indel_mer_tmb_lm <- lm(data = total_mer_indel_tmb,
                                 formula = tumor_mutation_burden ~ age + igh_translocation + purity + ploidy)
summary(test_total_indel_mer_tmb_lm)


total_indel_mer_tmb_lm <- lm(data = total_mer_indel_tmb,
                           formula = tumor_mutation_burden ~ purity + ploidy)
summary(total_indel_mer_tmb_lm)

# Plot the overall TMB by disease stage
total_mer_indel_tmb$tmb_corrected <- resid(total_indel_mer_tmb_lm) + mean(total_mer_indel_tmb$tumor_mutation_burden)

total_mer_indel_dx_median_tmb <- total_mer_indel_tmb %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
  dplyr::reframe("median_tmb" = median(na.omit(tmb_corrected)))

total_mer_indel_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output <- rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(data = droplevels(total_mer_indel_tmb %>%
                                                                                 dplyr::select(disease_stage, tmb_corrected)),
                                                             formula = tmb_corrected ~ disease_stage,
                                                             p.adjust.method = "fdr")
total_mer_indel_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(total_mer_indel_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output$p.adj)


total_mer_indel_tmb_by_dx <- ggplot(data = total_mer_indel_tmb, aes(x = disease_stage, y = tmb_corrected, fill = disease_stage, colour = disease_stage)) +
  geom_violin(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_boxplot(outliers = FALSE, width = 0.2, color = "white", linewidth = 1.1) +
  labs(y = "MER InDels per Mb",
       x = NULL) +
  scale_y_log10(limits = c(-2,100), labels = c("0.1","1.0", "10","100"), breaks = c(0.1,1.0, 10,100), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease\nStage", values = disease_stage_colors) +
  scale_color_manual(name = "Disease\nStage", values = disease_stage_colors) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, family = "sans", size = 14),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 15),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = alpha(col = "black", alpha = 0.2))) +
  ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = total_mer_indel_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output[4,],
                             label = "P={p.adj}",
                             y.position = 1.45,
                             tip.length = 0.008,
                             label.size = 4,
                             vjust = 0.1) +
  ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = total_mer_indel_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output[c(1,2,6),],
                             label = "{p.adj.signif}",
                             y.position = c(1.3,1.71,1.60),
                             tip.length = 0.008,
                             label.size = 4,
                             vjust = 0.1)

```

Check SNV MER metrics when split by translocation subtype
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

median_snv_per_mer_by_trx <- total_mer_snv_tmb %>%
  dplyr::group_by(igh_translocation) %>%
  dplyr::reframe("median_tmb_corrected" = median(na.omit(tmb_corrected)))

snv_per_mer_by_trx_wilcox_output <- total_mer_snv_tmb %>%
  dplyr::select(igh_translocation, tmb_corrected) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!igh_translocation %in% c("t(14;16)","t(6;14)","t(8;14)")) %>%
  droplevels() %>%
  rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(formula = tmb_corrected ~ igh_translocation,
                                p.adjust.method = "fdr")
snv_per_mer_by_trx_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(snv_per_mer_by_trx_wilcox_output$p.adj)

message("Median MER SNVs per t(11;14): ", median_snv_per_mer_by_trx[1,2])
message("Median MER SNVs per t(4;14): ", median_snv_per_mer_by_trx[2,2])
message("Median MER SNVs per t(14;20): ", median_snv_per_mer_by_trx[4,2])

# SNVs in MERs by TRX
mer_snv_by_trx <- ggplot(data = total_mer_snv_tmb, aes(x = igh_translocation, y = tmb_corrected)) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect",
           xmin = c(0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5, 6.5),
           xmax = c(1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5, 6.5, 7.5),
           ymin = 1, ymax = 100,
           alpha = 0.25,
           fill = trx_colors) +
  ggbeeswarm::geom_beeswarm(method = "compactswarm", corral = "wrap") +
  labs(y = "SNVs per Mb",#expression("log"[10]*"(TMB)"),
       x = NULL) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = c("1.0", "10","100"), breaks = c(1.0, 10,100), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, family = "sans", size = 14),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 16),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "gray75"),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA)) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment",
             x = c(0.75, 1.75, 2.75, 3.75, 4.75, 5.75, 6.75),
             xend = c(1.25, 2.25, 3.25, 4.25, 5.25, 6.25, 7.25),
             y = median_snv_per_mer_by_trx$median_tmb_corrected,
             yend = median_snv_per_mer_by_trx$median_tmb_corrected,
             color = "cyan",
             linewidth = 2.25) +
    ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text",
             x = c(1,2,3,4,5,6,7),
             y = 120,
             label = as.character(round(median_snv_per_mer_by_trx$median_tmb_corrected, digits = 2)),
             size = 5)


########### INDELS #############
median_indel_per_mer_by_trx <- total_mer_indel_tmb %>%
  dplyr::group_by(igh_translocation) %>%
  dplyr::reframe("median_tmb_corrected" = median(na.omit(tmb_corrected)))

indel_per_mer_by_trx_wilcox_output <- total_mer_indel_tmb %>%
  dplyr::select(igh_translocation, tmb_corrected) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!igh_translocation %in% c("t(14;16)","t(6;14)","t(8;14)")) %>%
  droplevels() %>%
  rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(formula = tmb_corrected ~ igh_translocation,
                                p.adjust.method = "fdr")
indel_per_mer_by_trx_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(indel_per_mer_by_trx_wilcox_output$p.adj)

message("Median MER InDels per t(11;14): ", median_indel_per_mer_by_trx[1,2])
message("Median MER InDels per t(4;14): ", median_indel_per_mer_by_trx[2,2])
message("Median MER InDels per t(14;20): ", median_indel_per_mer_by_trx[4,2])

# InDels in MERs by TRX
mer_indel_by_trx <- ggplot(data = total_mer_indel_tmb, aes(x = igh_translocation, y = tmb_corrected)) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect",
           xmin = c(0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5, 6.5),
           xmax = c(1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5, 6.5, 7.5),
           ymin = 1, ymax = 100,
           alpha = 0.25,
           fill = trx_colors) +
  ggbeeswarm::geom_beeswarm(method = "compactswarm", corral = "wrap") +
  labs(y = "InDels per Mb",#expression("log"[10]*"(TMB)"),
       x = NULL) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = c("1.0", "10","100"), breaks = c(1.0, 10,100), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, family = "sans", size = 14),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 16),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "gray75"),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA)) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment",
             x = c(0.75, 1.75, 2.75, 3.75, 4.75, 5.75, 6.75),
             xend = c(1.25, 2.25, 3.25, 4.25, 5.25, 6.25, 7.25),
             y = median_indel_per_mer_by_trx$median_tmb_corrected,
             yend = median_indel_per_mer_by_trx$median_tmb_corrected,
             color = "cyan",
             linewidth = 2.25) +
    ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text",
             x = c(1,2,3,4,5,6,7),
             y = 120,
             label = as.character(round(median_indel_per_mer_by_trx$median_tmb_corrected, digits = 2)),
             size = 5)
```

Look at SNV MER metrics split by admixture ancestry and then in precursor vs late
disease in these subsets
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

median_snv_per_mer_by_admix <- total_mer_snv_tmb %>%
  dplyr::group_by(super5_cluster_id) %>%
  dplyr::reframe("median_tmb_corrected" = median(na.omit(tmb_corrected)))

snv_per_mer_by_admix_wilcox_output <- total_mer_snv_tmb %>%
  dplyr::select(super5_cluster_id, tmb_corrected) %>%
  #dplyr::filter(!igh_translocation %in% c("t(14;16)","t(6;14)","t(8;14)")) %>%
  droplevels() %>%
  rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(formula = tmb_corrected ~ super5_cluster_id,
                                p.adjust.method = "fdr")
snv_per_mer_by_admix_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(snv_per_mer_by_admix_wilcox_output$p.adj)

message("Median MER SNVs per EUR Dominant ancestry: ", median_snv_per_mer_by_admix[1,2])
message("Median MER SNVs per AFR Dominant ancestry: ", median_snv_per_mer_by_admix[2,2])

# SNVs in MERs by TRX
mer_snv_by_admix <- ggplot(data = total_mer_snv_tmb, aes(x = super5_cluster_id, y = tmb_corrected)) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect",
           xmin = c(0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5),
           xmax = c(1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5, 6.5),
           ymin = 1, ymax = 100,
           alpha = 0.25,
           fill = super_pop_admix_cluster_colors) +
  ggbeeswarm::geom_beeswarm(method = "compactswarm", corral = "wrap") +
  labs(y = "SNVs per Mb",#expression("log"[10]*"(TMB)"),
       x = NULL) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = c("1.0", "10","100"), breaks = c(1.0, 10,100), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, family = "sans", size = 14),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 16),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "gray75"),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA)) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment",
             x = c(0.75, 1.75, 2.75, 3.75, 4.75, 5.75),
             xend = c(1.25, 2.25, 3.25, 4.25, 5.25, 6.25),
             y = median_snv_per_mer_by_admix$median_tmb_corrected,
             yend = median_snv_per_mer_by_admix$median_tmb_corrected,
             color = "cyan",
             linewidth = 2.25) +
    ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text",
             x = c(1,2,3,4,5,6),
             y = 120,
             label = as.character(round(median_snv_per_mer_by_admix$median_tmb_corrected, digits = 2)),
             size = 5) +
  ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = snv_per_mer_by_admix_wilcox_output[1,],
                             label = "P={p.adj}",
                             y.position = 1.75,
                             tip.length = 0.005,
                             label.size = 3.5,
                             vjust = 0.1)# +
  #ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = snv_per_mer_by_admix_wilcox_output[c(1,2,6),],
  #                           label = "{p.adj.signif}",
  #                           y.position = c(1.3,1.70,1.60),
  #                           tip.length = 0.005,
  #                           label.size = 3.5,
  #                           vjust = 0.1)

############ INDELS ###################
median_indel_per_mer_by_admix <- total_mer_indel_tmb %>%
  dplyr::group_by(super5_cluster_id) %>%
  dplyr::reframe("median_tmb_corrected" = median(na.omit(tmb_corrected)))

indel_per_mer_by_admix_wilcox_output <- total_mer_indel_tmb %>%
  dplyr::select(super5_cluster_id, tmb_corrected) %>%
  #dplyr::filter(!igh_translocation %in% c("t(14;16)","t(6;14)","t(8;14)")) %>%
  droplevels() %>%
  rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(formula = tmb_corrected ~ super5_cluster_id,
                                p.adjust.method = "fdr")
indel_per_mer_by_admix_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(indel_per_mer_by_admix_wilcox_output$p.adj)

# InDels in MERs by ancestry
mer_indel_by_admix <- ggplot(data = total_mer_indel_tmb, aes(x = super5_cluster_id, y = tmb_corrected)) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect",
           xmin = c(0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5),
           xmax = c(1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5, 6.5),
           ymin = 1, ymax = 100,
           alpha = 0.25,
           fill = super_pop_admix_cluster_colors) +
  ggbeeswarm::geom_beeswarm(method = "compactswarm", corral = "wrap") +
  labs(y = "InDels per Mb",
       x = NULL) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = c("1.0", "10","100"), breaks = c(1.0, 10,100), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, family = "sans", size = 14),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 16),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "gray75"),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA)) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment",
             x = c(0.75, 1.75, 2.75, 3.75, 4.75, 5.75),
             xend = c(1.25, 2.25, 3.25, 4.25, 5.25, 6.25),
             y = median_indel_per_mer_by_admix$median_tmb_corrected,
             yend = median_indel_per_mer_by_admix$median_tmb_corrected,
             color = "cyan",
             linewidth = 2.25) +
    ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text",
             x = c(1,2,3,4,5,6),
             y = 120,
             label = as.character(round(median_indel_per_mer_by_admix$median_tmb_corrected, digits = 2)),
             size = 5) #+
  #ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = indel_per_mer_by_admix_wilcox_output[1,],
  #                           label = "P={p.adj}",
  #                           y.position = 1.75,
  #                           tip.length = 0.005,
  #                           label.size = 3.5,
  #                           vjust = 0.1)
```

Check SNV MER metrics when split by disease stage
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# Disease Stage
# For accurate accounting of mutations per disease stage, need to allow for mutations to carry
# over from disease stage to another for multi-sample patients
muts_per_sample <- muts %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata$sample)) %Q% (Variant_Classification != "Silent")

# get all SNV per sample in SNV MERs
ncsnv_per_sample <- gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = muts_per_sample %Q% (VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV"),
                                            subject = mers_gr %Q% (mer_type == "SNV"),
                                            qcol = c("Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode", "Tumor_Sample_Barcode", "VARIANT_CLASS", "Reference_Allele",
                                                     "Tumor_Seq_Allele2", "VAF", "Variant_Classification"),
                                            scol = c("mer_gene_label", "mer_id","mut_uniq_id"))
ncsnv_per_sample_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(ncsnv_per_sample)

# Count per sample
ncsnv_per_sample_count <- ncsnv_per_sample_dt %>%
  dplyr::rename("patient" = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode, "sample" = Tumor_Sample_Barcode) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
  dplyr::count() %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# Add metadata to DT
snv_per_mer_per_sample <- left_join(x = ncsnv_per_sample_count,
                                    y = hq_dataset_metadata %>% dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage, age),
                                    by = "sample")

# group the counts by precursor and late disease
snv_per_mer_per_sample <- snv_per_mer_per_sample %>%
                            dplyr::mutate(precursor_late = case_when(disease_stage %in% c("MGUS", "SMM") ~ "Precursor",
                                                                     disease_stage %in% c("MM", "RR") ~ "Late"))
snv_per_mer_per_sample$precursor_late <- factor(snv_per_mer_per_sample$precursor_late,
                                                levels = c("Precursor", "Late"))

# Calculate the median for each group and run stats test
# median_snv_per_mer_by_precursor_late <- snv_per_mer_per_sample %>%
#   dplyr::group_by(precursor_late) %>%
#   dplyr::summarise(median_n = median(na.omit(n)))
# 
# snv_per_mer_by_precursor_late_wilcox_output <- snv_per_mer_per_sample %>%
#   dplyr::ungroup() %>%
#   dplyr::select(precursor_late, n) %>%
#   rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(formula = n ~ precursor_late,
#                                 p.adjust.method = "fdr")
# snv_per_mer_by_precursor_late_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(snv_per_mer_by_precursor_late_wilcox_output$p.adj)
# 
# print("Median MER SNVs per Precursor samples:")
# median_snv_per_mer_by_precursor_late[2,2]
# print("Median MER SNVs per Late samples:")
# median_snv_per_mer_by_precursor_late[1,2]

median_snv_per_mer_by_dx <- snv_per_mer_per_sample %>%
  dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
  dplyr::reframe(median_n = median(na.omit(n)))

median_snv_per_mer_by_dx_wilcox_output <- snv_per_mer_per_sample %>%
  dplyr::select(disease_stage, n) %>%
  droplevels() %>%
  rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(formula = n ~ disease_stage,
                                p.adjust.method = "fdr")
median_snv_per_mer_by_dx_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(median_snv_per_mer_by_dx_wilcox_output$p.adj)
                       

snvs_within_mers_by_dx_violin_plot <- ggplot(data = snv_per_mer_per_sample) +
  geom_violin(aes(x = disease_stage, y = n, fill = disease_stage), alpha = 0.6, color = "gray70") +
   geom_boxplot(aes(x = disease_stage, y = n, fill = disease_stage), outliers = FALSE, width = 0.15, color = "gray99", linewidth = 1.5) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,325)) +
  labs(y = "Total SNV count") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = disease_stage_colors) +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, size = 14),
        legend.position = "none",
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1)) +
  ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = median_snv_per_mer_by_dx_wilcox_output[c(1,4:6),],
                             label = "P={p_string}",
                             y.position = c(255,285,300,315),
                             tip.length = 0.005,
                             label.size = 3.5,
                             vjust = 0.1) +
  ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = median_snv_per_mer_by_dx_wilcox_output[2,],
                             label = "{p.adj.signif}",
                             y.position = 270,
                             tip.length = 0.005,
                             label.size = 3.5,
                             vjust = 0.1)
# export 5 x 5 portrait
```

Run similar analysis for InDels
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# For accurate accounting of mutations per disease stage, need to allow for mutations to carry
# over from disease stage to another for multi-sample patients
ncindel_per_sample <- gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = muts_per_sample %Q% (VARIANT_CLASS %in% c("insertion", "deletion")),
                                         subject = mers_gr %Q% (mer_type == "InDel"),,
                                         qcol = c("Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode", "Tumor_Sample_Barcode", "VARIANT_CLASS", "Reference_Allele",
                                                  "Tumor_Seq_Allele2", "VAF", "Variant_Classification"),
                                         scol = c("nearest_gene", "mer_id","mut_uniq_id"))
ncindel_per_sample_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(ncindel_per_sample)

ncindel_per_sample_count <- ncindel_per_sample_dt %>%
  dplyr::rename("patient" = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode, "sample" = Tumor_Sample_Barcode) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
  dplyr::count() %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

indel_per_mer_per_sample <- left_join(x = ncindel_per_sample_count,
                                      y = hq_dataset_metadata %>% dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage, age),
                                      by = "sample")

median_indel_per_mer_by_dx <- indel_per_mer_per_sample %>%
                                                dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
                                                dplyr::reframe(median_n = median(na.omit(n)))

indel_per_mer_by_dx_wilcox_output <- droplevels(indel_per_mer_per_sample) %>%
                                                  dplyr::select(disease_stage, n) %>%
                                                  rstatix::wilcox_test(formula = n ~ disease_stage)
indel_per_mer_by_dx_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(indel_per_mer_by_dx_wilcox_output$p)


indel_within_mers_by_dx_violin_plot <- ggplot(data = indel_per_mer_per_sample) +
  geom_violin(aes(x = disease_stage, y = n, fill = disease_stage), alpha = 0.6) +
  labs(y = "Total InDel count") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = disease_stage_colors) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,10), breaks = seq(0,8,2)) +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, size = 14),
        legend.position = "none",
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent")) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "point",
           x = c(1,2,3,4),
           y = median_indel_per_mer_by_dx$median_n,
           size = 4,
           shape = 21,
           color = "black",
           fill = "white") +
  ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = indel_per_mer_by_dx_wilcox_output[c(4),],
                             label = "P={p_string}",
                             y.position = 8,
                             bracket.nudge.y = 0.5,
                             size = 4.5,
                             tip.length = 0.005) +
  ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = indel_per_mer_by_dx_wilcox_output[c(1,6),],
                             label = "{p.adj.signif}",
                             y.position = c(7,9),
                             bracket.nudge.y = 0.5,
                             size = 4.5,
                             tip.length = 0.005)
# export 5 x 5 portrait

# Now correct for subtype, disease stage, and AA vs EA
indel_per_mer_by_trx <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
  dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS %in% c("insertion", "deletion") & igh_translocation != "t(8;14)") %>%
  droplevels() %>%
  dplyr::group_by(patient, igh_translocation) %>%
  dplyr::count() %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

median_indel_per_mer_by_trx <- indel_per_mer_by_trx %>%
  dplyr::group_by(igh_translocation) %>%
  dplyr::reframe(median_n = median(na.omit(n)))

indel_per_mer_by_trx_wilcox_output <- indel_per_mer_by_trx %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::select(igh_translocation, n) %>%
  rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(formula = n ~ igh_translocation,
                                p.adjust.method = "fdr")

indel_per_mer_by_trx_plot <- ggplot(data = indel_per_mer_by_trx) +
  geom_violin(aes(x = igh_translocation, y = n, fill = igh_translocation)) +
  labs(y = "Total InDel count") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,9), breaks = seq(0,8,2)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = trx_colors) +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, size = 14),
        legend.position = "none",
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent")) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "point",
           x = seq(1,6,1),
           y = median_indel_per_mer_by_trx$median_n,
           size = 3,
           shape = 21,
           color = "black",
           fill = "white")
```

Snake plot of percentage of unique MERs per sample
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# Snake plot of unique MERs per sample
# First gather all the SNV MERs per sample per disease stage
all_uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx <- complete_mer_snv_per_sample %>%
                                         dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage, mer_id) %>%
                                         dplyr::distinct()

uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_count <- all_uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx %>%
                                     dplyr::group_by(sample, disease_stage) %>%
                                     dplyr::count(name = "uniq_snv_mers") %>%
                                     dplyr::ungroup() %>%
                                     dplyr::mutate("pct_mers_occupied" = round((uniq_snv_mers / 280 * 100), digits = 3))

# Order all records by % of SNV MERs occupied by sample per dx
uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered <- data.table()
for(i in 1:length(disease_stage_order)) {
  uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_count_per_dx <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_count %>%
                                    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == disease_stage_order[i]) 
  
  uniq_snv_mer_pct_order_per_dx <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_count_per_dx %>%
                                    dplyr::arrange(pct_mers_occupied) %>%
                                    dplyr::select(sample) %>%
                                    purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                    as.character()
  # Build ordered plot data
  uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered_per_dx <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_count_per_dx
                                       
  uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered_per_dx$sample <- factor(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered_per_dx$sample,
                                                             levels = uniq_snv_mer_pct_order_per_dx)
  
  # Combine per disease stage ordered data to final set
  uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered <- rrbind(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered,
                                               uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered_per_dx)
}


# Calculate the medians
uniq_snv_mer_pct_occupied_median <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered %>%
                                 dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
                                 dplyr::summarize("median_pct" = median(pct_mers_occupied)) %>%
                                 dplyr::ungroup()


uniq_snv_mer_pct_occupied_wilcox_output <- rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(data = droplevels(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered),
                                                                          formula = pct_mers_occupied ~ disease_stage,
                                                                          p.adjust.method = "fdr") %>%
                                            rstatix::add_xy_position(x = "disease_stage")
uniq_snv_mer_pct_occupied_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(uniq_snv_mer_pct_occupied_wilcox_output$p.adj)

# TESTING
uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered %>%
  group_by(disease_stage) %>%
  mutate("stage_median" = median(pct_mers_occupied))

uniq_snv_mer_snake_plot <- ggpubr::ggdotchart(data = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered,
                   x = "sample",
                   y = "pct_mers_occupied",
                   color = "disease_stage",
                   palette = disease_stage_colors,
                   dot.size = 2.5) +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = "log1p", breaks = c(0, 1, 10, 100)) +
  geom_hline(data = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered %>% distinct(disease_stage, stage_median),
             aes(yintercept = stage_median, color = disease_stage),
             linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(y = "SNV MERs mutated (%)") +
  facet_grid(~disease_stage, space = "free") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 13),
        axis.text.x.bottom = element_blank(),
        axis.title.x.bottom = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "none",
        strip.background.y = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
        strip.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, size = 14),
        strip.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, size = 14))
```

Compare the occupancy of MERs across clinical, genomic, and demographic features
Firt, look at disease stage.
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

mer_snv_by_dx_upset <- list("MGUS" = mers %>% dplyr::filter(mut_mgus_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
                                        dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                        as.character() %>% gtools::mixedsort(),
                              "SMM" = mers %>% dplyr::filter(mut_smm_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
                                        dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                        as.character() %>% gtools::mixedsort(),
                              "MM" = mers %>% dplyr::filter(mut_mm_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
                                       dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                       as.character() %>% gtools::mixedsort(),
                              "RRMM" = mers %>% dplyr::filter(mut_rrmm_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
                                        dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                         as.character() %>% gtools::mixedsort())

mer_snv_by_dx_upset_plot <- upset(data = fromList(input = mer_snv_by_dx_upset),
      sets = rev(disease_stage_order),
      keep.order = T,
      order.by = "freq",
      mainbar.y.label = "Pair Intersection",
      sets.x.label = "Unique SNV MERs\nPer Disease Stage",
      point.size = 5.25,
      line.size = 1.8,
      text.scale = c(1.75, 1.5, 1.5, 1.25, 1.25, 1.5),
      sets.bar.color = rev(disease_stage_colors),
      matrix.color = "black",
      queries = list(
          list(
              query = intersects,
              params = list(rev(disease_stage_order)),
              active = TRUE
          )
      ))

common_snv_mers_all_dx <- mers %>% dplyr::filter(mer_type == "SNV" &
                                                 mut_mgus_patients > 0 &
                                                 mut_smm_patients > 0 &
                                                 mut_mm_patients > 0 &
                                                 mut_rrmm_patients > 0)

message("Percentage of SNV MERs common across MGUS + SMM + MM + RRMM: ", round(229 / 280 * 100, digits = 1))
message("Percentage of SNV MERs common across SMM or MGUS + MM + RRMM: ", round(51 / 280 * 100, digits = 1))


mer_indel_by_dx_upset <- list("MGUS" = mers %>% dplyr::filter(mut_mgus_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
                                        dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                        as.character() %>% gtools::mixedsort(),
                        "SMM" = mers %>% dplyr::filter(mut_smm_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
                                  dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                  as.character() %>% gtools::mixedsort(),
                        "MM" = mers %>% dplyr::filter(mut_mm_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
                                 dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                 as.character() %>% gtools::mixedsort(),
                        "RRMM" = mers %>% dplyr::filter(mut_rrmm_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
                                  dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                   as.character() %>% gtools::mixedsort())

mer_indel_by_dx_upset_plot <- upset(data = fromList(input = mer_indel_by_dx_upset),
      sets = rev(disease_stage_order),
      keep.order = T,
      order.by = "freq",
      mainbar.y.label = "Pair Intersection",
      sets.x.label = "Unique InDel MERs\nPer Disease Stage",
      point.size = 5.25,
      line.size = 1.8,
      text.scale = c(1.75, 1.5, 1.5, 1.25, 1.25, 1.5),
      sets.bar.color = rev(disease_stage_colors),
      matrix.color = "black",
      queries = list(
          list(
              query = intersects,
              params = list(rev(disease_stage_order)),
              color = "hotpink3",
              active = TRUE
          ),
          list(
              query = intersects,
              params = list(rev(disease_stage_order[-1])),
              color = "cyan3",
              active = TRUE
          )
      ))

common_indel_mers_all_dx <- mers %>% dplyr::filter(mer_type == "InDel" &
                                                 mut_mgus_patients > 0 &
                                                 mut_smm_patients > 0 &
                                                 mut_mm_patients > 0 &
                                                 mut_rrmm_patients > 0)
```
