Snake plot of unique MERs per disease stage
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# Snake plot of unique MERs per patient
uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
                               dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV") %>%
                               dplyr::select(sample, mer_id, disease_stage) %>%
                               dplyr::group_by(sample, disease_stage) %>%
                               dplyr::count(mer_id) %>%
                               dplyr::ungroup()

uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx %>%
                                         dplyr::group_by(sample,disease_stage) %>%
                                         droplevels() %>%
                                         dplyr::count() %>%
                                         dplyr::ungroup()

uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count$sample <- factor(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count$sample,
                                                      levels = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count %>%
                                                                dplyr::arrange(n) %>%
                                                                dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
                                                                dplyr::arrange(disease_stage) %>%
                                                                dplyr::ungroup() %>%
                                                                dplyr::select(sample) %>%
                                                                purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                                                as.character())

# by Dx
uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count %>%
  dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
  dplyr::reframe("median_uniq_mers" = median(n))

uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_wilcox_output <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count %>%
                                                  rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(formula = n ~ disease_stage,
                                                                                p.adjust.method = "fdr")
uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_wilcox_output$p.adj)

# violn box plot
uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_violinbox <- ggplot(data = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count) +
  geom_violin(aes(x = disease_stage, y = n, fill = disease_stage), alpha = 0.6, color = "gray70") +
   geom_boxplot(aes(x = disease_stage, y = n, fill = disease_stage), outliers = FALSE, width = 0.15, color = "gray99", linewidth = 1.5) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,110)) +
  labs(y = "Unique SNV MERs") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = disease_stage_colors) +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, size = 14),
        legend.position = "none",
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1)) +
   ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_wilcox_output[4,],
                             label = "P={p_string}",
                             y.position = 93,
                             tip.length = 0.005,
                             label.size = 3.5,
                             vjust = 0.1) +
  ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_wilcox_output[c(1,5),],
                             label = "P={p.adj}",
                             y.position = c(85,98),
                             tip.length = 0.005,
                             label.size = 3.5,
                             vjust = 0.1) +
  ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_wilcox_output[c(2,6),],
                             label = "{p.adj.signif}",
                             y.position = c(105,108),
                             tip.length = 0.005,
                             label.size = 3.5,
                             vjust = 0.1)


# find point for median bar
uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count %>%
  dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
  dplyr::count() %>%
  dplyr::mutate("group_midpoint" = n / 2)

uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$group_midpoint +
  c(0,uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$n[1],sum(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:2)]),sum(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:3)]))

uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$group_range <- c(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$n[1],
                                                sum(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:2)]),
                                                sum(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:3)]),
                                                sum(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:4)]))


uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_snake_plot <- ggplot(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count) +
  geom_point(aes(x = sample, y = n, color = disease_stage), size = 3) +
  labs(x = "Samples",
       y = "Unique SNV MERs") +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.01,0.01), limits = c(0,max(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count$n) + 15)) +
  scale_color_manual(name = "Disease Stage", values = disease_stage_colors) +
  theme(axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
        legend.position = "top",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 11)) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment",
           x = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint - 8,
           xend = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint + 8,
           y = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians$median_uniq_mers,
           yend = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians$median_uniq_mers,
           color = "hotpink",
           linewidth = 2.25) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment",
           x = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint[2],
           xend = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint[3],
           y = max(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count$n) + 7,
           yend = max(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count$n) + 7,
           linewidth = 0.33) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text",
           x = midpoint(x = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint[2:3])[[1]][2],
           y = max(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count$n) + 9,
           label = paste0("P=",uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_wilcox_output$p_string[4]),
           size = 4.5) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect",
           xmin = c(0, uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$group_range[-4]),
           xmax = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$group_range,
           ymin = 0,
           ymax = max(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count$n) + 15,
           alpha = 0.30,
           fill = c("gray75","gray35","gray75","gray35"))
```

Check the relationship between MER SNVs and total SNV burden
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# linear corr between total SNVs in MERs and unique MERs
# All together
uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx <- left_join(x = gr2dt(ncsnv_per_sample) %>% dplyr::rename("sample" = Tumor_Sample_Barcode),
                                            y = hq_dataset_metadata %>% dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage),
                                            by = "sample") %>%
                                    dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage, mer_id) %>%
                                    dplyr::group_by(sample, disease_stage) %>%
                                    dplyr::count(mer_id)

uniq_indel_mers_per_sample_by_dx <- left_join(x = gr2dt(ncindel_per_sample) %>% dplyr::rename("sample" = Tumor_Sample_Barcode),
                                            y = hq_dataset_metadata %>% dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage),
                                            by = "sample") %>%
                                    dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage, mer_id) %>%
                                    dplyr::group_by(sample, disease_stage) %>%
                                    dplyr::count(mer_id)

uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx %>%
                                           dplyr::select(sample, mer_id) %>%
                                           dplyr::count() %>%
                                           dplyr::arrange(desc(n)) %>%
                                           dplyr::ungroup()

uniq_indel_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count <- uniq_indel_mers_per_sample_by_dx %>%
                                           dplyr::select(sample, mer_id) %>%
                                           dplyr::count() %>%
                                           dplyr::arrange(desc(n)) %>%
                                           dplyr::ungroup()

# Goal is to describe the number of unique MERs per sample as a function of the total SNV burden
mer_correlations <- left_join(x = fish_muts_hq %>%
                                    dplyr::filter(mut_type == "SNVs") %>%
                                    dplyr::group_by(sample, disease_stage) %>%
                                    dplyr::count() %>%
                                    dplyr::ungroup(),
                              y = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count,
                              by = c("sample", "disease_stage")) %>%
                     dplyr::rename("total_snv_burden" = n.x,
                                   "uniq_mers" = n.y)

indel_mer_correlations <- left_join(x = fish_muts_hq %>%
                                    dplyr::filter(mut_type == "InDels") %>%
                                    dplyr::group_by(sample, disease_stage) %>%
                                    dplyr::count() %>%
                                    dplyr::ungroup(),
                              y = uniq_indel_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count,
                              by = c("sample", "disease_stage")) %>%
                     dplyr::rename("total_indel_burden" = n.x,
                                   "uniq_mers" = n.y)

# convert mutations from raw total to per 1k 
mer_correlations$total_snv_per_1k <-  mer_correlations$total_snv_burden / 1000

indel_mer_correlations$total_indel_per_1k <-  indel_mer_correlations$total_indel_burden / 1000

uniq_mer_total_burden_corr_plot <- ggplot(mer_correlations) +
  geom_point(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k, y = uniq_mers, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black") +
  geom_smooth(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k, y = uniq_mers), method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "#FB832D", alpha = 0.75) +
  ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k, y = uniq_mers), label.x = 7, label.y = 10, size = 4.5) +
  ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k, y = uniq_mers), label.x = 7, label.y = 5, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
  labs(x = "SNV Burden (1 k)",
       y = "Unique SNV MERs") +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage", values = disease_stage_colors) +
  theme(legend.position = "top",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 13),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1))

uniq_indel_mer_total_burden_corr_plot <- ggplot(indel_mer_correlations) +
  geom_point(aes(x = total_indel_burden, y = uniq_mers, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black", na.rm = T) +
  geom_smooth(aes(x = total_indel_burden, y = uniq_mers), method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "black", alpha = 0.75, na.rm = T) +
  ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = total_indel_burden, y = uniq_mers), label.x = 550, label.y = 1.0, size = 4.5, na.rm = T) +
  ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = total_indel_burden, y = uniq_mers), label.x = 550, label.y = 0.6, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001, na.rm = T) +
  labs(x = "InDel Burden",
       y = "Unique InDel MERs") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,7)) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage", values = disease_stage_colors) +
  theme(legend.position = "top",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 13),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1))

# export 6 x 6 portrait
##### SNVs #######
glance(mer_correlations %>%
        lm(data = ., formula = uniq_mers ~ total_snv_per_1k))

summary(mer_correlations %>%
          lm(data = ., formula = uniq_mers ~ total_snv_per_1k))

####### InDels ##########
glance(indel_mer_correlations %>%
        lm(data = ., formula = uniq_mers ~ total_indel_per_1k))

summary(indel_mer_correlations %>%
          lm(data = ., formula = uniq_mers ~ total_indel_per_1k))

# Check this relationship in each disease stage
mer_total_snv_correlations_by_dx <- ggplot(mer_correlations) +
  geom_point(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k, y = uniq_mers, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black") +
  geom_smooth(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k, y = uniq_mers), method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "#FB832D", alpha = 0.75) +
  ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k, y = uniq_mers), label.x = 7, label.y = 11, size = 4.5) +
  ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k, y = uniq_mers), label.x = 7, label.y = 1, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
  labs(x = "SNV Burden (1 k)",
       y = "Unique SNV MERs") +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage", values = disease_stage_colors) +
  facet_wrap(facets = ~ disease_stage) +
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 13),
        legend.position = "top",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

mer_total_indel_correlations_by_dx <- ggplot(indel_mer_correlations) +
  geom_point(aes(x = total_indel_burden, y = uniq_mers, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black", na.rm = T) +
  geom_smooth(aes(x = total_indel_burden, y = uniq_mers), method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "black", alpha = 0.75, na.rm = T) +
  ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = total_indel_burden, y = uniq_mers), label.x = 500, label.y = 1.1, size = 4.5, na.rm = T) +
  ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = total_indel_burden, y = uniq_mers), label.x = 500, label.y = 0.6, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001, na.rm = T) +
  labs(x = "InDel Burden",
       y = "Unique InDel MERs") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,7)) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage", values = disease_stage_colors) +
  facet_wrap(facets = ~ disease_stage) +
  theme(legend.position = "top",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 13),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1))
```

Check if linear relationship between age and unique MERs
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

mer_age_correlations <- left_join(x = hq_dataset_metadata %>% dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage, age),
                                  y = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count,
                                  by = c("sample","disease_stage")) %>%
                        dplyr::rename("uniq_mers" = n) %>%
                        dplyr::filter(!is.na(age))

uniq_mer_age_corr_plot <- ggplot(mer_age_correlations) +
  geom_point(aes(x = age, y = uniq_mers, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black") +
  geom_smooth(aes(x = age, y = uniq_mers), method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "#FB832D", alpha = 0.75) +
  ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = age, y = uniq_mers), label.x = 65, label.y = 7, size = 4.5) +
  ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = age, y = uniq_mers), label.x = 65, label.y = 2, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
  labs(x = "Age (years)",
       y = "Unique SNV MERs") +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage", values = disease_stage_colors[1:4]) +
  theme(legend.position = "top",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 13),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1))


indel_mer_age_correlations <- left_join(x = hq_dataset_metadata %>% dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage, age),
                                  y = uniq_indel_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count,
                                  by = c("sample","disease_stage")) %>%
                        dplyr::rename("uniq_mers" = n) %>%
                        dplyr::filter(!is.na(age))

uniq_indel_mer_age_corr_plot <- ggplot(indel_mer_age_correlations) +
  geom_point(aes(x = age, y = uniq_mers, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black") +
  geom_smooth(aes(x = age, y = uniq_mers), method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "black", alpha = 0.75) +
  ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = age, y = uniq_mers), label.x = 30, label.y = 6.9, size = 4.5) +
  ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = age, y = uniq_mers), label.x = 30, label.y = 6.5, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
  labs(x = "Age (years)",
       y = "Unique InDel MERs") +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage", values = disease_stage_colors[1:4]) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,7)) +
  theme(legend.position = "top",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 13),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1))

# export 6 x 6 portrait

# Faceted by disease stage
mer_age_correlations_by_dx <- ggplot(mer_age_correlations) +
  geom_point(aes(x = age, y = uniq_mers, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black") +
  geom_smooth(aes(x = age, y = uniq_mers), method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "#FB832D", alpha = 0.75) +
  ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = age, y = uniq_mers), label.x = 70, label.y = 95, size = 4.5) +

indel_mer_age_correlations_by_dx <- ggplot(indel_mer_age_correlations) +
  geom_point(aes(x = age, y = uniq_mers, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black") +
  geom_smooth(aes(x = age, y = uniq_mers), method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "black", alpha = 0.75) +
  ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = age, y = uniq_mers), label.x = 30, label.y = 6.9, size = 4.5) +
  ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = age, y = uniq_mers), label.x = 30, label.y = 6.3, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +

# Confirm total SNV count is also linear with age
age_total_snv_corr <- ggplot(hq_dataset_metadata) +
  geom_point(aes(x = age, y = snv_count / 1000, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black", na.rm = T) +
  geom_smooth(aes(x = age, y = snv_count/ 1000), method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "#FB832D", alpha = 0.75, na.rm = T) +
  ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = age, y = snv_count / 1000), label.x = 55, label.y = 24.1, size = 4.5, na.rm = T) +
  ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = age, y = snv_count / 1000), label.x = 55, label.y = 21, size = 4.5, na.rm = T, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
  labs(x = "Age (years)",
       y = "SNV Burden (thousands)") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(20,100), expand = c(0.03,0.03)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,25), expand = c(0.03,0.03), labels = scales::label_comma(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage", values = disease_stage_colors) +
  facet_wrap(facets = ~ disease_stage) +
  theme(legend.position = "top",
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

age_total_indel_corr <- ggplot(hq_dataset_metadata) +
  geom_point(aes(x = age, y = indel_count / 1000, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black", na.rm = T) +
  geom_smooth(aes(x = age, y = indel_count / 1000), method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "black", alpha = 0.75, na.rm = T) +
  ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = age, y = indel_count / 1000), label.x = 60, label.y = 8.2, size = 4.5, na.rm = T) +
  ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = age, y = indel_count / 1000), label.x = 60, label.y = 7.2, size = 4.5, na.rm = T, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
  labs(x = "Age (years)",
       y = "InDel Burden") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(20,100), expand = c(0.03,0.03)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,9), expand = c(0.03,0.03), labels = scales::label_comma(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage", values = disease_stage_colors) +
  facet_wrap(facets = ~ disease_stage) +
  theme(legend.position = "top",
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))
```

## SBS signatures at MERs have features of punctuated lineage-specific mutagenesis from endogenous mechanisms and off-target AID, Part 1

Complete SBS profile across all samples
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# WITH SBSBLOOD AND WITHOUT SBS5

# For proper SBS signatures to be called, we must have sufficient number of SNVs
# Typical minimum cutoff is 1000 SNVs (2000 is default for SigProfilerTopography)
# First, collect all SNVs across all MERs
hq_dataset_sigs <- fish_muts_hq[VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV", .(sample, seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2)] %>%
                    dplyr::filter(sample %in% tmb_fish_eligbile$sample)

# Now prepare the data for mmsig
# Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(hq_dataset_sigs) <- c("sample","chr","pos","ref","alt")

# Prep the input for mmsig using deconstructSig
hq_dataset_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = hq_dataset_sigs,
                                                 sample.id = "sample",
                                                 chr = "chr",
                                                 pos = "pos",
                                                 ref = "ref",
                                                 alt = "alt",
                                                 bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)
# Generate a 96 class SBS profile
hq_dataset_sig_96_p <- plot_sbs96(hq_dataset_sig_96) +
                        labs(title = "High-qualty genomic regions")

# Translate data for use in mmsig
hq_dataset_sig_96$Total<- rowSums(hq_dataset_sig_96)
hq_dataset_sig_96_transpose<- as.data.frame.matrix(t(hq_dataset_sig_96))

# Prep the signature reference for the fitted approach
data(signature_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38)

# SBS1 & SBS5 for clock-like (priority to use SBS5 for all rather than split into SBS5 / SBS40)
# SBS2 & SBS13 for APOBEC
# SBS8 still unknown
# SBS9 for Pol Eta somatic hyper mutation
# SBS18 for Damage by reactive oxygen species (ROS)
# SBS84 & SBS85 for canonical AID (excluded in analysis)
# SBS99 for melphalan exposure (included from previous analysis as known signature)
# SBS45, SBS46, and SBS49 for sequencing artifacts (used to identify possilbe exclusion of samples, if not excluded via de novo extraction)
# Add SBSblood as more appropriate description of SBS5
sbs_blood <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "sbsBloodRefSig.txt"),
                   sep = "\t",
                   header = T)

sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38 <- signature_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38 %>%
                              dplyr::select(all_of(c("sub", "tri", "SBS1", "SBS2", "SBS8", "SBS9",
                                                     "SBS13", "SBS18", "SBS84", "SBS-MM1"))) %>%
                              dplyr::rename("SBS99" = `SBS-MM1`) %>%
                              dplyr::left_join(y = sbs_blood,
                                               by = c("sub","tri"))

# Fit the signatures
# Complete set of all MER mutations
# Break them up in to smaller groups to see if this works
# chunk 1 
hq_dataset_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = hq_dataset_sig_96_transpose,
                                    sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38,
                                    input.format = "classes",
                                    sample.sigt.profs = NULL,
                                    strandbias = FALSE,
                                    bootstrap = FALSE,
                                    genome = "hg38",
                                    dbg = FALSE)


# SBS1, SBSblood, SBS8, SBS9, SBS84, SBS18, SBS99, SBS2, SBS13
mmsig_signames_updated <- c("SBS1", "SBSblood", "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS84", "SBS18", "SBS99", "SBS2", "SBS13")
mmsig_colors <- c("#1B9E77FF","#7570B3FF","#E7298AFF","#66A61EFF",
                  "#FB8072","#A6761DFF","#666666FF", "#D95F02FF","#E6AB02FF")

hq_dataset_sigs_t <- reshape2::melt(data = hq_dataset_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
                                              tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
                                              dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
                                              dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
                                                                    dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage) %>%
                                                                    droplevels(),
                                                               by = "sample") %>%
                                              as.data.table(),
                                    id.vars = c("sample","disease_stage"),
                                    variable.name = "signature",
                                    value.name = "rel_contrib")

hq_dataset_sigs_order <- hq_dataset_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
  dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
                         dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage) %>%
                         droplevels(),
                  by = "sample") %>%
  dplyr::mutate("sum_aid" = SBS9 + SBS84) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(sum_aid) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(disease_stage) %>%
  dplyr::select(sample)

hq_dataset_sigs_t$sample <- factor(hq_dataset_sigs_t$sample,
                                 levels = hq_dataset_sigs_order$sample)

hq_dataset_sigs_t$signature <- factor(hq_dataset_sigs_t$signature,
                                      levels = mmsig_signames_updated)


hq_dataset_mmsig_bar_p <- ggplot(hq_dataset_sigs_t, 
                                 aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib, fill = signature)) +
  geom_col(width = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature", values = mmsig_colors) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +
  labs(x = "Samples",
       y = "Relative Contribution") +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))


######## RELATIONSHIPS OF SBS AND METADATA ###########


# SBS1, SBSblood, and the combined
# SBS1 Relative
sbs1_rel_vs_age <- hq_dataset_sigs_t %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
                     dplyr::select(sample,age),
                   by = "sample") %>%
  dplyr::filter(signature == "SBS1") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = age, y = rel_contrib)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = disease_stage)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "darkred", alpha = 0.75, na.rm = T) +
  ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(label.x = 55, label.y = 0.17, size = 4.5, na.rm = T) +
  ggpubr::stat_cor(label.x = 55, label.y = 0.15, size = 4.5, na.rm = T, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
  labs(x = "Age (years)",
       y = "SBS1 Contribution (%)") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(25,90), expand = c(0.03,0.03)) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.03,0.03), labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)) +
  scale_color_manual(name = "Disease Stage", values = disease_stage_colors)

# SBS1 Absolute
sbs1_abs_vs_age <- hq_dataset_sigs_t %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
                     dplyr::select(sample,age),
                   by = "sample") %>%
  dplyr::filter(signature == "SBS1") %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
                        tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
                        dplyr::select(sample,mutations),
                   by = "sample") %>%
  dplyr::mutate("abs_burden" = round(rel_contrib * mutations, digits = 0)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = age, y = abs_burden)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = disease_stage)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "darkred", alpha = 0.75, na.rm = T) +
  ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(label.x = 55, label.y = 600, size = 4.5, na.rm = T) +
  ggpubr::stat_cor(label.x = 55, label.y = 570, size = 4.5, na.rm = T, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
  labs(x = "Age (years)",
       y = "SBS1 Burden") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(25,90), expand = c(0.03,0.03)) +
  scale_color_manual(name = "Disease Stage", values = disease_stage_colors)

# Clock-like Relative
clock_rel_vs_age <- hq_dataset_sigs_t %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
                         dplyr::select(sample,age),
                   by = "sample") %>%
  dplyr::filter(signature %in% c("SBSblood","SBS1")) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(sample,age,disease_stage) %>%
  dplyr::reframe("clock_like" = sum(rel_contrib)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = age, y = clock_like)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = disease_stage)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "darkred", alpha = 0.75, na.rm = T) +
  ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(label.x = 55, label.y = 0.17, size = 4.5, na.rm = T) +
  ggpubr::stat_cor(label.x = 55, label.y = 0.15, size = 4.5, na.rm = T, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
  labs(x = "Age (years)",
       y = "Clock-like (SBS1+SBSblood) Contribution (%)") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(25,90), expand = c(0.03,0.03)) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.03,0.03), labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)) +
  scale_color_manual(name = "Disease Stage", values = disease_stage_colors)

###########

```

Let's look at SBS signatures in MERs
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

######## WITH SBSBLOOD ##########

# For proper SBS signatures to be called, we must have sufficient number of SNVs
# Typical minimum cutoff is 1000 SNVs (2000 is default for SigProfilerTopography)
# First, collect all SNVs across all MERs
mer_sig_muts <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
                 dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV") %>%
                 dplyr::select(mer_id, seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2)

# Unfortunately, there aren't enough mutations per MER to assess relative signatures individually
# TODO: try spliting mers by SE overlap group
#muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
#  dplyr::group_by(mer_id) %>%
#  dplyr::count() %>%
#  dplyr::arrange(desc(n))

# We'll overcome this by looking at all SNVs within the MERs
# First, given that there are now duplicated mutations per MER (from different samples/patients etc.), remove them here
mer_sig_muts <- mer_sig_muts[!duplicated(mer_sig_muts),]
mer_sig_muts$mer_id <- "MERs"

# Now prepare the data for mmsig
# Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(mer_sig_muts) <- c("sample","chr","pos","ref","alt")

# Prep the input for mmsig using deconstructSig
mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = mer_sig_muts,
                                                 sample.id = "sample",
                                                 chr = "chr",
                                                 pos = "pos",
                                                 ref = "ref",
                                                 alt = "alt",
                                                 bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# Generate a 96 class SBS profile
mer_sig_96_p <- plot_sbs96(mer_sig_96) +
                  labs(title = "MERs")

# Translate data for use in mmsig
mer_sig_96$Total<- rowSums(mer_sig_96)
mer_sig_96_transpose<- as.data.frame.matrix(t(mer_sig_96))

# Prep the signature reference for the fitted approach
data(signature_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38)

# SBS1 & SBS5 for clock-like (priority to use SBS5 for all rather than split into SBS5 / SBS40)
# SBS2 & SBS13 for APOBEC
# SBS8 still unknown
# SBS9 for Pol Eta somatic hyper mutation
# SBS18 for Damage by reactive oxygen species (ROS)
# SBS84 & SBS85 for canonical AID (excluded in analysis)
# SBS-MM1 for melphalan exposure (included from previous analysis as known signature)
# SBS45, SBS46, and SBS49 for sequencing artifacts (used to identify possilbe exclusion of samples, if not excluded via de novo extraction)
# Add SBSblood as more appropriate description of SBS5
# Fit the signatures
# Complete set of all MER mutations
mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = mer_sig_96_transpose,
                                    sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38,
                                    input.format = "classes",
                                    sample.sigt.profs = NULL,
                                    strandbias = FALSE,
                                    bootstrap = FALSE,
                                    genome = "hg38",
                                    dbg = FALSE,
                                    force_include = NULL)

mer_sigs_t <- reshape2::melt(data = mer_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
                                              tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
                                              dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
                                              as.data.table(),
                                    id.vars = "sample",
                                    variable.name = "signature",
                                    value.name = "rel_contrib")

mer_sigs_t$signature <- factor(mer_sigs_t$signature,
                                      levels = c("SBS1", "SBSblood", "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS84",
                                                 "SBS18", "SBS99", "SBS2", "SBS13"))

mer_mmsig_bar_p <- ggplot(mer_sigs_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib, fill = signature)) +
  geom_col(width = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature", values = mmsig_colors) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +
  labs(x = "Samples",
       y = "Relative Contribution") +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

#mer_sig_96_p + mer_mmsig_bar_p + plot_layout(widths = c(1.1,0.075))
# export 4 x 11 landscape

message("SBS Signatures found in MERs: ")
round(t(mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10]) * 100, digits = 2)

# Convert from relative to absolute for the signatures
mer_abs_sig_contrib <- as.data.frame(t(mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10])) %>%
                        tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "signatures") %>%
                        dplyr::mutate("abs_burden" = round(MERs * mer_sig_96$Total, digits = 0))
#ggplot(mer_abs_sig_contrib) +
#  ggplot2::geom_col(aes(x = signatures, y = abs_burden))


######## WITH SBS5 ##########

# # For proper SBS signatures to be called, we must have sufficient number of SNVs
# # Typical minimum cutoff is 1000 SNVs (2000 is default for SigProfilerTopography)
# # First, collect all SNVs across all MERs
# mer_sig_muts <- muts_per_mer_for_plot[VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV", .(mer_id, seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2)]

```

Let's look at the signatures in eligible region of non-coding analysis excluding the MERs
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# Collect all mutations that fall within the eligible regions of the genome (same as input for fishHook model)
fish_muts <- grbind(snv_fish, indel_fish)
muts_eligible <- gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = fish_muts,
                                         subject = eligible_bed,
                                         qcol = c("Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode", "Tumor_Sample_Barcode", "VARIANT_CLASS",
                                                  "Reference_Allele", "Tumor_Seq_Allele2", "VAF", "Variant_Classification", "SYMBOL"))
muts_eligible_dt <- gr2dt(muts_eligible)

# Let's look at SBS signatures in non-MER
# First, collect all SNVs across eligible region
eligible_sig_muts <- muts_eligible_dt[VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV", .(Tumor_Sample_Barcode, seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2)]

# Now prepare the data for mmsig and compatability with MER SBS mutations
# Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(eligible_sig_muts) <- c("sample","chr","pos","ref","alt")

# Subtract out the mutations within the MERs from the eligible region mutations
non_mer_sig_muts <- dplyr::setdiff(x = eligible_sig_muts[,-1], y = mer_sig_muts[,-1])

# TODO: try to match samples to mutations in nonMERs, could gain some better insight depending on signal
non_mer_sig_muts$sample <- "non-MERs"

# Prep the input for mmsig using deconstructSig
non_mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = non_mer_sig_muts,
                                                     sample.id = "sample",
                                                     chr = "chr",
                                                     pos = "pos",
                                                     ref = "ref",
                                                     alt = "alt",
                                                     bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# Generate a 96 class SBS profile
non_mer_sig_96_p <- plot_sbs96(non_mer_sig_96) +
                      labs(title = "Total Non-MERs")

# Translate data for use in mmsig
non_mer_sig_96$Total<- rowSums(non_mer_sig_96)
non_mer_sig_96_transpose<- as.data.frame.matrix(t(non_mer_sig_96))

# Fit the signatures
non_mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = non_mer_sig_96_transpose,
                                        sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38,
                                        input.format = "classes",
                                        sample.sigt.profs = NULL,
                                        strandbias = FALSE,
                                        bootstrap = FALSE,
                                        genome = "hg38",
                                        dbg = FALSE,
                                        force_include = NULL)

non_mer_sigs_t <- reshape2::melt(data = non_mer_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
                                              tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
                                              dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
                                              as.data.table(),
                                    id.vars = "sample",
                                    variable.name = "signature",
                                    value.name = "rel_contrib")

non_mer_sigs_t$signature <- factor(non_mer_sigs_t$signature,
                                      levels = c("SBS1", "SBSblood", "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS84",
                                                 "SBS18", "SBS99", "SBS2", "SBS13"))

mer_mmsig_bar_p <- ggplot(non_mer_sigs_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib, fill = signature)) +
  geom_col(width = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature", values = mmsig_colors) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +
  labs(x = "Samples",
       y = "Relative Contribution") +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

#non_mer_mmsig_bar_p <- mmsig::plot_signatures(sig = non_mer_sig_96_out$estimate,
#                                              samples = F,
#                                              sig_order = c("SBS1", "SBSblood", "SBS2", "SBS13",
#                                                            "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS18", "SBS84", "SBS99")) +
#                       labs(title = "Simulated\nNon-MERs") +
#                       theme(legend.position = "right",
#                             panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
#                             plot.title = element_text(size = 14, hjust = 0.5))

# Combo plot
#non_mer_sig_96_p + non_mer_mmsig_bar_p + plot_layout(widths = c(1.25,0.1))
# export 4 x 11 landscape

message("SBS Signatures found in Non-MERs:")
round(t(non_mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10]) * 100, digits = 2)
```

## Simulating the genomic background

Create a background genome for random sampling that is the eligible regions excluding the MERs
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

non_mer_eligible <- eligible_bed %_% gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers))

# To control for best representation, random regions will be restricted to range of MERs (i.e. control the variance to the same as the observed)
# Look at the distribution of MER widths for proper simulation of similar distribution derived from the non-MER background
#mer_min_width <- min(GenomicRanges::width(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers))))

#mer_q1_width <- mer_median_width - (IQR(x = GenomicRanges::width(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers)))) / 2)
#mer_q3_width <- mer_median_width + (IQR(x = GenomicRanges::width(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers)))) / 2)
#mer_max_width <- max(GenomicRanges::width(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers))))

# Visualize the observed distribution
mer_median_width <- median(GenomicRanges::width(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers))))
mer_width_distro <- data.table::data.table(gr2dt(mers)[,1],
                                           gr2dt(mers)[,2],
                                           gr2dt(mers)[,3],
                                           "width" = GenomicRanges::width(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers))) / 1000,
                                           "region" = "MERs",
                                           "simulation" = 0)

mer_width_distro_plot <- ggplot(data = mer_width_distro) +
  ggdist::stat_slab(aes(x = width, y = region, fill = region), show.legend = F) +
  ggdist::stat_dotsinterval(aes(x = width, y = region, fill = region),
                            quantiles = 200, side = "bottom", slab_linewidth = NA, show.legend = F) +
  labs(y = "MERs", x = "Width (kbp)") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(10,100,10), expand = c(0.03,0.03)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = "#9467BD") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 0, vjust = 0.5)) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text",
           x = mer_median_width / 1000,
           y = 1.07,
           label = paste(mer_median_width / 1000, "kbp"))

# https://stats.stackexchange.com/questions/132652/how-to-determine-which-distribution-fits-my-data-best
# https://aursiber.github.io/fitdistrplus/articles/fitdistrplus_vignette.html#Choice
# # Let's fit the MER width distribution instead of making up from quantiles
# # First, test the observed evaluation of kurtosis and squared skewness and a number of bootstrap runs
# # to help identify the best fit of the observed data
#fitdistrplus::plotdist(data = mer_width_distro$width,
#                       histo = T,
#                       demp = T,
#                       discrete = F)
#fitdistrplus::descdist(data = mer_width_distro$width,
#                       discrete = F,
#                       boot = 500)
# maybe gamma or log-normal
width_lognorm_fit <- fitdistrplus::fitdist(data = mer_width_distro$width,
                                           distr = "lnorm",
                                           discrete = F,
                                           method = "mle")
width_gamma_fit <- fitdistrplus::fitdist(data = mer_width_distro$width,
                                         distr = "gamma",
                                         discrete = F,
                                         method = "mle")
width_beta_fit <- fitdistrplus::fitdist(data = mer_width_distro$width / 100,
                                        distr = "beta",
                                        discrete = F,
                                        optim.method = "BFGS",
                                        method = "mle")

# Let's look at the fit summaries
#summary(width_lognorm_fit)
#summary(width_gamma_fit)
#summary(width_beta_fit)

# now some fit metrics
# fitdistrplus::denscomp(ft = list(width_lognorm_fit, width_gamma_fit),
#                        xlim = c(0, max(mer_width_distro$width)),
#                        xlab = "Kilobases",
#                        plotstyle = "ggplot",
#                        fitcol = c("#5ABCEB", "#CF4E9C")) +
#   scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0))
# 
# fitdistrplus::cdfcomp(ft = list(width_lognorm_fit, width_gamma_fit),
#                       xlab = "Kilobases",
#                       plotstyle = "ggplot",
#                       fitcol = c("#5ABCEB", "#CF4E9C"))
# 
# fitdistrplus::qqcomp(ft = list(width_lognorm_fit, width_gamma_fit),
#                      plotstyle = "ggplot",
#                      fitcol = c("#5ABCEB", "#CF4E9C"))
# 
# fitdistrplus::ppcomp(ft = list(width_lognorm_fit, width_gamma_fit),
#                      plotstyle = "ggplot",
#                      fitcol = c("#5ABCEB", "#CF4E9C"))
# 
# # Check the goodness of fit statistics
# fitdistrplus::gofstat(f = list(width_lognorm_fit, width_gamma_fit),
#                       discrete = F,
#                       fitnames = c("lognorm", "gamma"))
```

Now run simulations of the background to see what they look like compared to MERs 
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# The regular log-normal fit is best but could be improved by limiting the width to same limitations as MERs (i.e. must be 10kbp or greater),
# This requires a truncated log-normal fit with a minimum of 10.0 (kbp)
# https://stackoverflow.com/questions/16947799/fitting-a-lognormal-distribution-to-truncated-data-in-r
dtruncated_log_normal <- function(x, meanlog, sdlog) {
  a_min = min(mer_width_distro$width)
  b_max = max(mer_width_distro$width)
  dtrunc(x, "lnorm", a=a_min, b=b_max, meanlog=meanlog, sdlog=sdlog)
}
  
ptruncated_log_normal <- function(q, meanlog, sdlog) {
  a_min = min(mer_width_distro$width)
  b_max = max(mer_width_distro$width)
  ptrunc(q, "lnorm", a=a_min, b=b_max, meanlog=meanlog, sdlog=sdlog)
}

qtruncated_log_normal <- function(x, meanlog, sdlog) {
  a_min = min(mer_width_distro$width)
  b_max = max(mer_width_distro$width)
  qtrunc(x, "lnorm", a=a_min, b=b_max, meanlog=meanlog, sdlog=sdlog)
}

width_trunc_lognorm_fit <- fitdistrplus::fitdist(data = mer_width_distro$width,
                                                 distr = "truncated_log_normal",
                                                 discrete = F,
                                                 method = "mle",
                                                 start = list(meanlog = 0, sdlog = 1))

summary(width_trunc_lognorm_fit)

# QQ and CDF plot
trunc_lognorm_fit_cdf <- cdfcomp(width_trunc_lognorm_fit, plotstyle = "ggplot", legendtext = "Truncated Log-Normal",
                                 fitcol = "#91D1C2", fitlwd = 1, datacol = "#9467BD", xlab = "Data", ylab = "Cumulative Density",
                                 main = "Empirical & Theoretical CDFs") +
                           theme(legend.position = "inside",
                                 legend.position.inside = c(0.70,0.05),
                                 panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
                                 panel.grid = element_blank(),
                                 plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
                                 axis.text = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),
                                 axis.title = element_text(size = 13, color = "black"))

trunc_lognorm_fit_qq <- qqcomp(width_trunc_lognorm_fit, xlim = c(10,90), ylim = c(10,90), plotstyle = "ggplot",
                               legendtext = "Truncated Log-Normal", fitcol = "#91D1C2", fitlwd = 4, fitpch = 18) +
                          theme(legend.position = "inside",
                                legend.position.inside = c(0.3,0.95),
                                panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))
# export 5 x 5 landscape

# Let's look at the fitted distribution
trunc_lognorm_fit_testsim_dist <- EnvStats::rlnormTrunc(n = 300,
                                                        meanlog = width_trunc_lognorm_fit$estimate[1],
                                                        sdlog = width_trunc_lognorm_fit$estimate[2],
                                                        min = min(mer_width_distro$width),
                                                        max = max(mer_width_distro$width))
#ggplot() +
#  geom_histogram(aes(x = trunc_lognorm_fit_testsim_dist))

# # Save/load simulation data to save time
# saveRDS(object = trunc_complete_sim_distro, file = paste0(base_data_path, "nonMerSimulationWidthDistro.rds"))
# saveRDS(object = trunc_complete_sim_sbs, file = paste0(base_data_path, "nonMerSimulation96Sbs.rds"))
# saveRDS(object = trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs, file = paste0(base_data_path, "nonMerSimulationMmsigSbs.rds"))

trunc_complete_sim_distro <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "nonMerSimulationWidthDistro.rds"))
trunc_complete_sim_sbs <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "nonMerSimulation96Sbs.rds"))
trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "nonMerSimulationMmsigSbs.rds"))

# Test each distribution with a wilcox test and test the entire set of random simulations
mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width <- gUtils::rrbind(mer_width_distro, trunc_complete_sim_distro)
mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width$simulation <- as.character(mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width$simulation)
mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width$simulation <- factor(mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width$simulation)
mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width$region <- factor(mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width$region,
                                    levels = c("MERs", "Simulated\nNon-MERs"))

mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width_pvals <- data.table("pvals" = rep("NA",300))
for(i in 1:300) {
  mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width_pvals$pvals[i] <- rstatix::wilcox_test(data = mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width %>%
                                                                           dplyr::filter(simulation %in% c(0,i)) %>%
                                                                           droplevels(),
                                                                  formula = width ~ simulation)$p
  
}
mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width_median_pval <- data.table("median_pval" = round(median(as.numeric(mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width_pvals$pvals)), digits = 3))

mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width_distro_vln <- ggplot(data = mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width) +
  ggdist::stat_eye(aes(x = width, y = region, fill = region), scale = 0.90, show.legend = F) +
  labs(y = NULL,
       x = "Width (kb)") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(10,max(mer_width_distro$width) + 18), expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "", values = c("#9467BD", "#91D1C2")) +
  theme(axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 0, vjust = 0.5),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent")) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text",
           x = c(round(median(trunc_complete_sim_distro$width), digits = 0), mer_median_width / 1000),
           y = c(2.07, 1.07),
           label = c(paste(round(median(trunc_complete_sim_distro$width), digits = 0), "kb"), paste(mer_median_width / 1000, "kb")),
           color = c("black","white"),
           size = 4) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment",
           x = 103,
           xend = 103,
           y = 1,
           yend = 2,
           linewidth = 0.33) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text",
           x = 105,
           y = 1.5,
           size = 4.5,
           angle = 270,
           label = paste0("P=", mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width_median_pval$median_pval))
# export 4 x 6 landscape
```

**Supplementary Figure 18**

```{r}
#| label: supp-fig-18ab
#| fig-cap: "**a.** Cumulative density functions from the empirical widths of MERs and the theoretical widths sampled from the truncated log-normal fitted distribution. **b.** Q-Q plot evaluating the quality of fit using theoretical widths from the truncated log-normal fitted distribution. Together these plots demonstrate a high-quality goodness-of-fit of the distribution for the random sampling of the genomic background."
#| fig-height: 4.5
#| fig-width: 8
#| out-width: "70%"

supp_fig18 <- (trunc_lognorm_fit_cdf + trunc_lognorm_fit_qq) / (mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width_distro_vln + plot_spacer()) + plot_layout(nrow = 2)

trunc_lognorm_fit_cdf + trunc_lognorm_fit_qq
```

```{r}
#| label: supp-fig-18c
#| fig-cap: "**c.** Comparison of the distributions of widths between 300 iterations of simulated non-MERs and MERs. Each simulation was tested for statistical difference between the median widths, and the median of all Wilcoxon test P-values is displayed on the plot."
#| fig-height: 4.5
#| fig-width: 4.5
#| out-width: "70%"

supp_fig18 <- (trunc_lognorm_fit_cdf + trunc_lognorm_fit_qq) / (mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width_distro_vln + plot_spacer()) + plot_layout(nrow = 2)

mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width_distro_vln
```

## SBS signatures at MERs have features of punctuated lineage-specific mutagenesis from endogenous mechanisms and off-target AID, Part 2

Visualize the sim profiles
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# Plots for figure panel
sbs_per_sim <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(trunc_complete_sim_sbs[-97])) %>%
                 tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "trinuc_context")
colnames(sbs_per_sim)[-1] <- paste0("sim",seq(1,300))
sbs_per_sim_errorbars <- data.table()
for(i in 1:nrow(trunc_complete_sim_sbs)) {
  sbs_pct_per_sim <- sbs_per_sim[,c(1,i+1)] %>%
    dplyr::rename("count" = colnames(sbs_per_sim)[i+1])
  
  sbs_pct_per_sim$pct_sbs <- (sbs_pct_per_sim$count / sum(sbs_pct_per_sim$count)) * 100
  
  sbs_per_sim_errorbars <- rrbind(sbs_per_sim_errorbars,
                                  data.table("trinuc_context" = sbs_pct_per_sim$trinuc_context,
                                             "sim" = colnames(sbs_per_sim)[i+1],
                                             "pct_sbs" = sbs_pct_per_sim$pct_sbs))
}

# Set COSMIC standard color scheme
col_96 <- c(rep("#5ABCEB",16), rep("#050708",16), rep("#D33C32",16),
            rep("#CBCACB",16), rep("#ABCD72", 16), rep("#E7C9C6",16))

per_sim_errorbars_sbs <- sbs_per_sim_errorbars %>%
  dplyr::group_by(trinuc_context) %>%
  dplyr::reframe("median_sbs_pct" = median(pct_sbs),
                 "sd_sbs_pct" = sd(pct_sbs))

per_sim_errorbars_sbs_plot <- ggplot(per_sim_errorbars_sbs) +
  ggplot2::geom_col(aes(x = trinuc_context, y = median_sbs_pct), fill = col_96) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(x = trinuc_context, ymin = median_sbs_pct-sd_sbs_pct, ymax = median_sbs_pct+sd_sbs_pct), width = 0.3) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0),
                     limits = c(0,max(per_sim_errorbars_sbs$median_sbs_pct) * 0.2 + max(per_sim_errorbars_sbs$median_sbs_pct)),
                     labels = scales::label_percent(scale = 1, accuracy = 0.1)) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0,0)) +
  labs(title = "Simulated Non-MERs",
       x = "Trinucleotide Context",
       y = "Single Base Substitutions (%)") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, size = 5, color = col_96, face = "bold"),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA)) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect",
           xmin = c(0.5,16.5,32.5,48.5,64.5,80.5),
           xmax = c(16.5,32.5,48.5,64.5,80.5,96.5),
           ymin = max(per_sim_errorbars_sbs$median_sbs_pct) * 0.125 + max(per_sim_errorbars_sbs$median_sbs_pct),
           ymax = max(per_sim_errorbars_sbs$median_sbs_pct) * 0.2 + max(per_sim_errorbars_sbs$median_sbs_pct),
           fill = col_96[c(1,17,33,49,65,81)]) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text",
           x = c(8,24,40,56,72,88),
           y = max(per_sim_errorbars_sbs$median_sbs_pct) * 0.16 + max(per_sim_errorbars_sbs$median_sbs_pct),
           label = c("C>A", "C>G", "C>T", "T>A", "T>C", "T>G"),
           size = 4,
           color = c("black", "white", "black", "black", "black", "black"))

# Combo plot of SBS 96s MERs and sim Non-MERs
#mer_sig_96_p / per_sim_errorbars_sbs_plot + plot_layout(ncol = 1, nrow = 2, axes = "collect")
# export 

# Sort the simulated non-MER signatures to show APOBEC clearly detected in nearly all,
# to align well with the other supplementary figure
#non_mer_per_sim_sigs <- trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs %>%
#  dplyr::mutate("cum_apobec" = SBS2 + SBS13,
#                "sample" = paste0("sim",dplyr::row_number())) %>%
#  dplyr::arrange(cum_apobec) %>%
#  dplyr::select(sample,SBS1,SBS2,SBS5,SBS8,SBS9,SBS13,SBS18,SBS84,`SBS99`)


non_mer_per_sim_sigs <- trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs %>%
  dplyr::mutate("cum_apobec" = SBS2 + SBS13,
                "sample" = paste0("sim",dplyr::row_number())) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(cum_apobec) %>%
  dplyr::select(sample,SBS1,SBS2,SBSblood,SBS8,SBS9,SBS13,SBS18,SBS84,`SBS99`)


non_mer_per_sim_sigs$sample <- factor(non_mer_per_sim_sigs$sample,
                                      levels = non_mer_per_sim_sigs$sample)

non_mer_per_sim_sigs_t <- reshape2::melt(data = non_mer_per_sim_sigs,
                                          id.vars = "sample",
                                          variable.name = "signature",
                                          value.name = "rel_contrib")

#non_mer_per_sim_sigs_t$signature <- factor(non_mer_per_sim_sigs_t$signature,
#                                           levels = c("SBS1", "SBS5", "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS84",
#                                                      "SBS18", "SBS99", "SBS2", "SBS13"))

non_mer_per_sim_sigs_t$signature <- factor(non_mer_per_sim_sigs_t$signature,
                                           levels = c("SBS1", "SBSblood", "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS84",
                                                      "SBS18", "SBS99", "SBS2", "SBS13"))

non_mer_per_sim_mmsigs <- ggplot(non_mer_per_sim_sigs_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib, fill = signature)) +
  geom_col(width = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature", values = mmsig_colors) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +
  labs(x = "Simulated Non-MERs",
       y = "Relative Contribution") +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

#non_mer_per_sim_mmsig <- mmsig::plot_signatures(sig = trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs,
#                       samples = F,
#                       sig_order = c("SBS1", "SBS5", "SBS2", "SBS13",
#                                     "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS18", "SBS84", "SBS99")) +
#  labs(title = "Non-MER Simulations") +
#  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 15))
# export 5 x 10 landscape

#mean_non_mer_sim_mmsig_sbs <- data.table("SBS1" = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS1),
#                                         "SBS2" = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS2),
#                                         "SBS5" = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS5),
#                                         "SBS8" = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS8),
#                                         "SBS9" = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS9),
#                                         "SBS13" = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS13),
#                                         "SBS18" = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS18),
#                                         "SBS84" = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS84),
#                                         "SBS99" = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$`SBS99`),
#                                         "mutations" = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$mutations))

mean_non_mer_sim_mmsig_sbs <- data.table("SBS1" = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS1),
                                         "SBS2" = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS2),
                                         "SBSblood" = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBSblood),
                                         "SBS8" = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS8),
                                         "SBS9" = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS9),
                                         "SBS13" = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS13),
                                         "SBS18" = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS18),
                                         "SBS84" = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS84),
                                         "SBS99" = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$`SBS99`),
                                         "mutations" = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$mutations))


mean_non_mer_sim_sigs_t <- reshape2::melt(data = mean_non_mer_sim_mmsig_sbs %>%
                                              tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
                                              dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
                                              as.data.table(),
                                    id.vars = "sample",
                                    variable.name = "signature",
                                    value.name = "rel_contrib")

mean_non_mer_sim_sigs_t$sample <- "Simulated\nNon-MERs"

mean_non_mer_sim_sigs_t$signature <- factor(mean_non_mer_sim_sigs_t$signature,
                                           levels = c("SBS1", "SBSblood", "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS84",
                                                      "SBS18", "SBS99", "SBS2", "SBS13"))

mean_non_mer_sim_mmsigs <- ggplot(mean_non_mer_sim_sigs_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib, fill = signature)) +
  geom_col(width = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature", values = mmsig_colors) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +
  labs(x = "Simulated Non-MERs",
       y = "Relative Contribution") +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

#round(mean_non_mer_sim_sigs_t$rel_contrib * 100, digits = 2)

#non_mer_mean_sim_mmsig_plot <- mmsig::plot_signatures(sig = mean_non_mer_sim_mmsig_sbs,
#                                              samples = F,
#                                              sig_order = c("SBS1", "SBS5", "SBS2", "SBS13",
#                                                            "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS18", "SBS84", "SBS99")) +
#                       labs(title = "Simulated\nNon-MERs") +
#                       theme(legend.position = "right",
#                             panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
#                             plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14))

# Combo plot
#mer_mmsig_bar_p + non_mer_mean_sim_mmsig_plot + plot_layout(ncol = 2, guides = "collect", axes = "collect")

# Calculate the TMB per simulation compared to MERs
# normalize by number of patients contributing SNVs then get TMB
mer_tmb <- (mer_sig_96$Total / (muts_per_mer_for_plot %>% dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV") %>% dplyr::select(patient) %>% dplyr::n_distinct())) / (sum(mers$width) / 1e6)

trunc_complete_sim_distro$simulation <- factor(trunc_complete_sim_distro$simulation,
                                               levels = seq(1,300))
non_mer_sim_tmb <- trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$mutations / (trunc_complete_sim_distro %>%
  dplyr::group_by(simulation) %>%
  dplyr::reframe("tot_sim_size" = (sum(width) * 1e3) / 1e6) %>%
  dplyr::select(tot_sim_size))

# normalize by number of patients contributing SNVs then get TMB
non_mer_sim_tmb <- (trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$mutations / trunc_complete_sim_sbs$n_patients) / (trunc_complete_sim_distro %>%
  dplyr::group_by(simulation) %>%
  dplyr::reframe("tot_sim_size" = (sum(width) * 1e3) / 1e6) %>%
  dplyr::select(tot_sim_size))


# combine the two TMB sets for plotting
mer_vs_sim_tmb <- data.table("region" = c("MERs",rep("Simulated\nNon-MERs", nrow(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs))),
                              "tmb" = c(mer_tmb, as.vector(t(non_mer_sim_tmb))))
mer_vs_sim_tmb$region <- factor(mer_vs_sim_tmb$region,
                                levels = c("MERs", "Simulated\nNon-MERs"))

# now run Z-test of real obs mean vs simulated means
#non_mer_tmb_sim_set <- dplyr::slice_sample(non_mer_sim_tmb, n = 5)
mer_vs_non_mer_tmb_ztest <- BSDA::z.test(x = non_mer_sim_tmb,
                                         mu = mer_tmb,
                                         alternative = "two.sided",
                                         sigma.x = sd(non_mer_sim_tmb$tot_sim_size))
message("Results of testing means between MERs and Sim Non-MERs:")
mer_vs_non_mer_tmb_ztest
mer_vs_non_mer_tmb_fc <- data.table("fc" = round(mer_tmb / mean(non_mer_sim_tmb$tot_sim_size), digits = 2),
                                    "p_string" = "P<0.0001")

tmb_mer_vs_sim_plot <- ggplot(mer_vs_sim_tmb) +
  ggbeeswarm::geom_quasirandom(aes(x = "Genomic Region", y = tmb, fill = region), size = 4.25, shape = 21, color = "black") +
  labs(y = "TMB (muts. per Mb)",
       x = NULL) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Genomic\nRegions", values = c("#9467BD", "#91D1C2")) +
  scale_y_log10(expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank()) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment",
           x = 0.75,
           xend = 1.25,
           y = mean(non_mer_sim_tmb$tot_sim_size),
           yend = mean(non_mer_sim_tmb$tot_sim_size),
           color = "black",
           linewidth = 2.7) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text",
           x = 1,
           y = 8,
           label = paste0("Mean FC=", mer_vs_non_mer_tmb_fc$fc,
                          "\n", mer_vs_non_mer_tmb_fc$p_string))
# export 4 x 5 portrait

message("MER per patient TMB: ", round(mer_tmb, digits = 2))
message("Simulated Non-MER per patient TMB: ", round(mean(non_mer_sim_tmb$tot_sim_size), digits = 2))

# Difference in signatures in MERs vs Non-MERs
# Look at distribution of SBS contribution
sim_mmsig_contrib_per_sig <- reshape2::melt(data = trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs[,-10],
                                            value.name = "rel_contrib_per_sim",
                                            variable.name = "signature") %>%
                               dplyr::group_by(signature) %>%
                               dplyr::reframe("rel_contrib" = mean(rel_contrib_per_sim) * 100)
sim_mmsig_contrib_per_sig$region <- "Simulated\nNon-MERs"

sim_mmsig_contrib_per_sig <- rrbind(sim_mmsig_contrib_per_sig,
                                    data.table("signature" = rownames(t(mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10])),
                                               t(mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10]) * 100,
                                               "region" = "MERs") %>%
                                      dplyr::rename("rel_contrib" = MERs))


sim_mmsig_contrib_per_sig$region <- factor(sim_mmsig_contrib_per_sig$region,
                                           levels = c("MERs", "Simulated\nNon-MERs"))

sim_mmsig_contrib_per_sig$signature <- factor(x = sim_mmsig_contrib_per_sig$signature,
                                              levels = c("SBS1", "SBSblood", "SBS2", "SBS13",
                                                         "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS18", "SBS84", "SBS99"))

mer_vs_non_mer_sig_diffs <- ggplot(data = sim_mmsig_contrib_per_sig,
       aes(x = signature, y = rel_contrib, fill = region, label = round(rel_contrib, digits = 2))) +
  ggplot2::geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  ggfittext::geom_bar_text(position = "dodge", outside = T, contrast = T) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Region", values = c("#9467BD", "#91D1C2")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1, scale = 1)) +
  labs(y = "Contribution (%)") +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, color = c("#1B9E77","#7570B3","#D95F02","#E6AB02","#E7298A","#66A61E","#A6761D","#FB8072","#666666")),
        legend.position = "top",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))
# export 6 x 10 landscape
```

Extracted MER de novo SBS signature SBS96A decomposition into COSMIC signatures
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# Run the workflow
denovo_sbs96a_decomp_plot <- create_decomposition_figure(
  denovo_txt = paste0(base_data_path, "sigProfilerSBSandIDsigs/mers/SBS96/Suggested_Solution/SBS96_De-Novo_Solution/Signatures/SBS96_De-Novo_Signatures.txt"),
  decomposition_csv = paste0(base_data_path, "sigProfilerSBSandIDsigs/mers/SBS96/Suggested_Solution/COSMIC_SBS96_Decomposed_Solution/De_Novo_map_to_COSMIC_SBS96.csv"),
  cosmic_reference = paste0(base_data_path, "sigProfilerSBSandIDsigs/mers/SBS96/Suggested_Solution/COSMIC_SBS96_Decomposed_Solution/Signatures/COSMIC_SBS96_Signatures.txt"),
  sig_type = "SBS"
)
```

**Supplementary Figure 5**

```{r}
#| label: supp-fig-5a
#| fig-cap: "**a.** De novo extraction and decomposition of COSMIC v3.3 reference single-base substitutions (SBS) using SigProfilerExtractor in a de novo approach. Interestingly, this yielded a decomposition that implicated homologous recombination deficiency associated SBS3 with significant contribution."
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-width: 7
#| out-width: "70%"

supp_fig5_a <- denovo_sbs96a_decomp_plot
supp_fig5_a
```

```{r}
#| label: supp-fig-5b
#| fig-cap: "**b.** SBS decomposition using mmsig in a fitted approach for all samples genome-wide (top) and 300 iterations of simulated non-MERs (bottom). This decomposition illustrates that the mutational signatures observed in MERs and non-MERs are not confounded due to the small cumulative size of the regions."
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-width: 7
#| out-width: "70%"

supp_fig5_b <- hq_dataset_mmsig_bar_p / non_mer_per_sim_mmsigs + plot_layout(guides = "collect", axis_titles = "collect_y")
supp_fig5_b
```

Look at the contribution across disease stages
Starting with signatures in MGUS MERs
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

#####################################
# SPLIT BY DISEASE STAGE
raw_mgus_mer_sig_muts <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
                           dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV" & disease_stage == "MGUS") %>%
                           dplyr::select(sample, mer_id, seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2)

# We'll overcome this by looking at all SNVs within the MERs
# First, given that there are now duplicated mutations per MER (from different samples/patients etc.), remove them here
mgus_mer_sig_muts <- raw_mgus_mer_sig_muts %>%
                      dplyr::select(-sample) %>%
                      dplyr::distinct()
mgus_mer_sig_muts$mer_id <- "MGUS"

# Now prepare the data for mmsig
# Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(mgus_mer_sig_muts) <- c("sample","chr","pos","ref","alt")

# Prep the input for mmsig using deconstructSig
mgus_mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = mgus_mer_sig_muts,
                                                 sample.id = "sample",
                                                 chr = "chr",
                                                 pos = "pos",
                                                 ref = "ref",
                                                 alt = "alt",
                                                 bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# Generate a 96 class SBS profile
mgus_mer_sig_96_p <- plot_sbs96(mgus_mer_sig_96) +
                      labs(title = "MGUS MERs") +
                      theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, hjust = 0.5))

# Translate data for use in mmsig
mgus_mer_sig_96$Total<- rowSums(mgus_mer_sig_96)
mgus_mer_sig_96_transpose<- as.data.frame.matrix(t(mgus_mer_sig_96))

# Fit the signatures
# Complete set of all MER mutations
mgus_mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = mgus_mer_sig_96_transpose,
                                    sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38,
                                    input.format = "classes",
                                    sample.sigt.profs = NULL,
                                    strandbias = FALSE,
                                    bootstrap = FALSE,
                                    genome = "hg38",
                                    dbg = FALSE,
                                    force_include = NULL)

mgus_mer_sigs_t <- reshape2::melt(data = mgus_mer_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
                                              tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
                                              dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
                                              as.data.table(),
                                    id.vars = "sample",
                                    variable.name = "signature",
                                    value.name = "rel_contrib")

mgus_mer_sigs_t$signature <- factor(mgus_mer_sigs_t$signature,
                                      levels = mmsig_signames_updated)

mgus_mer_mmsig_bar_p <- ggplot(mgus_mer_sigs_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib, fill = signature)) +
  geom_col(width = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature", values = mmsig_colors) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +
  labs(x = "MGUS",
       y = "Relative Contribution") +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

#mer_sig_96_p + mer_mmsig_bar_p + plot_layout(widths = c(1.1,0.075))
# export 4 x 11 landscape

message("SBS Signatures found in MERs:")
round(t(mgus_mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10]) * 100, digits = 2)

# Convert from relative to absolute for the signatures
mgus_mer_abs_sig_contrib <- as.data.frame(t(mgus_mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10])) %>%
                        tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "signatures") %>%
                        dplyr::mutate("abs_burden" = round(MGUS * (mgus_mer_sig_96$Total / n_distinct(raw_mgus_mer_sig_muts$sample)), digits = 0),
                                      "group" = "MGUS")
#ggplot(mgus_mer_abs_sig_contrib) +
#  ggplot2::geom_col(aes(x = signatures, y = abs_burden))
```

```{r}
#| tidy: true
```

```{r}
raw_rrmm_mer_sig_muts <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%

                           dplyr::select(sample, mer_id, seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2)

                      dplyr::select(-sample) %>%
                      dplyr::distinct()

# Now prepare the data for mmsig
# Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(rrmm_mer_sig_muts) <- c("sample","chr","pos","ref","alt")

# Prep the input for mmsig using deconstructSig
```
