Now look at the MER mutation overlap with lncRNA and miRNA gene bodies
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# Loop through all MERs and find the overlap of each mutation in relation to protein coding gene bodies
ncgene_body_overlap_pct <- data.table::data.table("mer_id" = mers$mer_id,
                                                  "mer_gene_label" = mers$mer_gene_label,
                                                  "lncrna_overlap_pct" = 0.0,
                                                  "mirna_overlap_pct" = 0.0)

for(i in 1:n_distinct(ncgene_body_overlap_pct$mer_id)) {
  # Get the total number of mutations at the MER
  per_mer_muts <- muts_per_mer_gr %Q% (mer_id == unique(ncgene_body_overlap_pct$mer_id)[i])
  mer_mut_denom <- length(per_mer_muts)
  
  # Find where each mutation in the MER overlaps and calculate the percentage
  # miRNA
  mer_mut_mirna_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = mirna_elements_gr,
                                                                    subject = per_mer_muts,
                                                                    qcol = "gene_name",
                                                                    scol = c("mer_gene_label", "mer_id")))
  ncgene_body_overlap_pct$mirna_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(mer_mut_mirna_overlap) / mer_mut_denom * 100, digits = 2)
  
  # lncRNA
  mer_mut_lncrna_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = lncrna_elements_gr,
                                                                     subject = per_mer_muts,
                                                                     qcol = "gene_name",
                                                                     scol = c("mer_gene_label", "mer_id")))
  ncgene_body_overlap_pct$lncrna_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(mer_mut_lncrna_overlap) / mer_mut_denom * 100, digits = 2)
}

# Add the lncRNA and miRNA gene bodies to MER overlap
mers_overlap <- mers_overlap %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = ncgene_body_overlap_pct,
                   by = c("mer_id","mer_gene_label"))

ncgene_mers <- mers_overlap %>%
  dplyr::filter(lncrna_overlap_pct > 0) %>%
  dplyr::select(mer_id,mer_gene_label,total_mut_patients,pct_total_mut_patients,
                cluster_label,gene_body_pct,lncrna_overlap_pct,mirna_overlap_pct,
                overlapping_genes) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))

# All lncRNA
mers_overlap %>%
  dplyr::filter(lncrna_overlap_pct > 0) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(lncrna_overlap_pct)) %>%
  dplyr::select(mer_id,mer_gene_label,cluster_label,gene_body_pct,
                lncrna_overlap_pct,mirna_overlap_pct,overlapping_genes)

# mean overlap of all
lncrna_mers <- mers_overlap %>%
  dplyr::mutate("lncrna" = case_when(lncrna_overlap_pct > 0.0 ~ "lncrna",
                                     lncrna_overlap_pct < 0 ~ NA))
mers_overlap %>%
  dplyr::filter(lncrna_overlap_pct > 0) %>%
  dplyr::reframe("mean_lncrna_overlap" = mean(lncrna_overlap_pct))

# Co-localization of protein-coding and lncRNA gene body MERs
ncgene_mers %>%
  dplyr::filter(lncrna_overlap_pct > 0 & cluster_label != "Intergenic") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(lncrna_overlap_pct))

# lncRNA specific MERs (i.e. no protein coding overlap)
mers_overlap %>%
  dplyr::filter(lncrna_overlap_pct > 0 & cluster_label == "Intergenic") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(lncrna_overlap_pct)) %>%
  dplyr::select(mer_id,mer_gene_label,cluster_label,gene_body_pct,
                lncrna_overlap_pct,mirna_overlap_pct,overlapping_genes)

# How many FAM genes
mers_overlap %>%
  dplyr::filter(lncrna_overlap_pct > 0 &
                  cluster_label == "Intergenic" &
                  str_detect(string = overlapping_genes, pattern = "FAM")) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(lncrna_overlap_pct)) %>%
  dplyr::select(mer_id,mer_gene_label,cluster_label,gene_body_pct,
                lncrna_overlap_pct,mirna_overlap_pct,overlapping_genes)

# miRNA MERs
mirna_mers <- mers_overlap %>%
  dplyr::mutate("mirna" = case_when(mirna_overlap_pct > 0 ~ "mirna",
                                    mirna_overlap_pct == 0 ~ NA))
mers_overlap %>%
  dplyr::filter(mirna_overlap_pct > 0) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(mirna_overlap_pct)) %>%
  dplyr::select(mer_id,mer_gene_label,cluster_label,gene_body_pct,
                lncrna_overlap_pct,mirna_overlap_pct,overlapping_genes)
```

Enhancers/Super-Enhancers and Promoters
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# Loop through all MERs and find the overlap of each mutation in relation to protein coding gene bodies
cre_overlap_pct <- data.table::data.table("mer_id" = mers$mer_id,
                                          "mer_gene_label" = mers$mer_gene_label,
                                          "enhancer_overlap_pct" = 0.0,
                                          "enhancer_id" = NA,
                                          "tissue" = NA,
                                          "enhancer_type" = NA,
                                          "promoter_overlap_pct" = 0.0,
                                          "prom_id" = NA)

for(i in 1:n_distinct(cre_overlap_pct$mer_id)) {
  # Get the total number of mutations at the MER
  per_mer_muts <- muts_per_mer_gr %Q% (mer_id == unique(cre_overlap_pct$mer_id)[i])
  mer_mut_denom <- length(per_mer_muts)
  
  # E/SEs
  mer_mut_ee_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = complete_ee,
                                                                 subject = per_mer_muts,
                                                                 qcol = c("enhancer_id","tissue","enhancer_type"),
                                                                 scol = c("mer_gene_label", "mer_id")))
  
  cre_overlap_pct$enhancer_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$subject.id)) / mer_mut_denom * 100, digits = 2)
  
  # Get cell line specific EE ID
  if(length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_id)) > 1) {
    cre_overlap_pct$enhancer_id[i] <- stringr::str_flatten(string = sort(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_id)), collapse = ",")
  } else if(length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_id)) == 1) {
    cre_overlap_pct$enhancer_id[i] <- unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_id)
  }
  
  # Get cell line
  if(length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$tissue)) > 1) {
    cre_overlap_pct$tissue[i] <- stringr::str_flatten(string = sort(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$tissue)), collapse = ",")
  } else if(length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$tissue)) == 1) {
    cre_overlap_pct$tissue[i] <- unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$tissue)
  }
  
  # Get EE enhancer_type
  if(length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_type)) > 1) {
    cre_overlap_pct$enhancer_type[i] <- stringr::str_flatten(string = sort(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_type)), collapse = ",")
  } else if(length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_type)) == 1) {
    cre_overlap_pct$enhancer_type[i] <- unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_type)
  }
  
  # Promoters
  
  mer_mut_promoter_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = promoter_sites_gr,
                                                                       subject = per_mer_muts,
                                                                       qcol = "prom_id",
                                                                       scol = c("mer_gene_label", "mer_id")))
  cre_overlap_pct$promoter_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(mer_mut_promoter_overlap) / mer_mut_denom * 100, digits = 2)
  
  # Get all overlapping promoters
  if(length(unique(mer_mut_promoter_overlap$prom_id)) > 1) {
    cre_overlap_pct$prom_id[i] <- stringr::str_flatten(string = sort(unique(mer_mut_promoter_overlap$prom_id)), collapse = ",")
  } else if(length(unique(mer_mut_promoter_overlap$prom_id)) == 1) {
    cre_overlap_pct$prom_id[i] <- unique(mer_mut_promoter_overlap$prom_id)
  }
}

# Add the Enhancer / Promoter to MER overlap
mers_overlap <- mers_overlap %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = cre_overlap_pct,
                   by = c("mer_id","mer_gene_label"))

# E/SE MERs
e_se_mers <- mers_overlap %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(tissue))
e_se_mers_gr <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(e_se_mers))

# How many MERs / genes
message("Number of significant MERs that overlap E/SE elements: ", length(unique(e_se_mers$mer_id)))
message("Number of unique coding genes associated: ", length(unique(e_se_mers$nearest_gene)))

e_se_mers %>%
  dplyr::reframe("mean_enhancer_overlap" = mean(enhancer_overlap_pct)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(mean_enhancer_overlap))

# How many mutations in E/SE-MERs
message("Total SNVs in E/SE-MERs: ",
        muts_per_mer %>%
          dplyr::filter(mut_type == "SNVs" & mut_uniq_id %in% e_se_mers$mut_uniq_id) %>%
          dplyr::count())
message("% of all MER SNVs in E/SEs: ",(muts_per_mer %>%
          dplyr::filter(mut_type == "SNVs" & mut_uniq_id %in% e_se_mers$mut_uniq_id) %>%
          dplyr::count()) /
          (muts_per_mer %>%
            dplyr::filter(mut_type == "SNVs") %>%
            dplyr::count()) * 100)

message("Total InDels in E/SE-MERs: ",
        muts_per_mer %>%
          dplyr::filter(mut_type == "InDels" & mut_uniq_id %in% e_se_mers$mut_uniq_id) %>%
          dplyr::count())
message("% of all MER InDels in E/SEs: ",(muts_per_mer %>%
          dplyr::filter(mut_type == "InDels" & mut_uniq_id %in% e_se_mers$mut_uniq_id) %>%
          dplyr::count()) /
          (muts_per_mer %>%
            dplyr::filter(mut_type == "InDels") %>%
            dplyr::count()) * 100)

# Range of % mutated patients
message("Range of Percentage of patients mutated at E/SE-MERs: ", paste0(range(e_se_mers$pct_total_mut_patients), collapse = " - "))
```

LOLA analysis of SE 
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

#####################################################
# TEST 1: ARE SEs ENRICHED IN MERs
# TEST 2: ARE SEs ENRICHED IN SIMULATED RANDOM NON-MERs (negative control)

# query will be SEs
# universe will be all E/SE elements
# ref set database will be MERs

e_se_universe <- LOLA::readBed(paste0(base_data_path, "enhancerElements.hg38.bed"))
se_query <- e_se_universe[str_detect(string = names(e_se_universe), pattern = "SE_")]

# Use simulated Non-MERs in LOLA as negative control
# Write them all to single BED then split into per simulation BEDs to be tested
#fwrite(x = trunc_complete_sim_distro[c(1:3,6)],
#       file = "simNonMERs.bed",
#       sep = "\t",
#       col.names = F)
#LOLA::splitFileIntoCollection(filename = paste0(base_data_path, "simNonMERs.bed"),
#                              splitCol = 4,
#                              collectionFolder = paste0(base_data_path, "lolaAnalysis/mers/collection2/regions/"),
#                              filenamePrepend = "simulatedNonMERs-")

# Run the LOLA analysis
mer_and_non_mer_db <- loadRegionDB(dbLocation = paste0(base_data_path, "lolaAnalysis/mers_and_simulations/"))
#mer_and_non_mer_se_lola <- runLOLA(userSets = se_query, userUniverse = e_se_universe, regionDB = mer_and_non_mer_db)
#saveRDS(object = mer_and_non_mer_se_lola, file = paste0(base_data_path, "lolaAnalysis/mers_and_simulations/mer_vs_non_mer_se_lola.rds"))
mer_and_non_mer_se_lola <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "lolaAnalysis/mers_and_simulations/mer_vs_non_mer_se_lola.rds"))

# convert log10 p-value to empirical and then add correction
message("SE-MER overlap FDR adj. P-value: ", scales::scientific(x = mer_and_non_mer_se_lola$qValue[1]))
message("Odds ratio of SE-MER overlap: ", round(mer_and_non_mer_se_lola$oddsRatio[1], digits = 1))
message("SE-Simulated Non-MER overlap median P-value: ", round(median(mer_and_non_mer_se_lola$qValue[-1]), digits = 3))
message("Odds ratio of SE-Simulated Non-MER overlap: ", round(median(mer_and_non_mer_se_lola$oddsRatio[-1]), digits = 1))

# Transform LOLA output for plotting
mer_and_non_mer_se_lola_for_plot <- mer_and_non_mer_se_lola %>%
                                      dplyr::group_by(antibody) %>%
                                      dplyr::reframe("median_or" = median(oddsRatio),
                                                     "sd_or" = sd(oddsRatio))
message("95% CI of SE-Simulated Non-MER overlap odds ratio: ",
        mer_and_non_mer_se_lola_for_plot$median_or[2] - mer_and_non_mer_se_lola_for_plot$sd_or[2], " - ",
        mer_and_non_mer_se_lola_for_plot$median_or[2] + mer_and_non_mer_se_lola_for_plot$sd_or[2])
```

Look at the genes related to each E/SE-MER group
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# GCB MERs
gcb_se_mers <- e_se_mers %>%
  dplyr::filter(tissue == "GCB")

message("Number of GCB E/SE-MERs: ", length(unique(gcb_se_mers$mer_id)))
message("Number of unique coding genes associated: ", length(unique(gcb_se_mers$nearest_gene)))

gcb_se_mers %>%
  dplyr::select(mer_id,mer_type,mer_gene_label,total_mut_patients,pct_total_mut_patients,seqnames,start,
                end,cluster_label,cds_overlap_pct,three_prime_utr_overlap_pct,five_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
                intron_overlap_pct,intergenic_pct,lncrna_overlap_pct,mirna_overlap_pct,
                enhancer_overlap_pct,tissue,enhancer_type,promoter_overlap_pct,prom_id) %>%
  dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label %in% c("RPIA","SERPINA9","ZCCHC7","LTB","DTX1","TCL1A",
                                      "PAX5","CXCR4","LPP","RFTN1","BTG1","CD83")) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))


# PC MERs
pc_se_mers <- e_se_mers %>%
  dplyr::filter(tissue == "PC")

message("Number of PC E/SE-MERs: ", length(unique(pc_se_mers$mer_id)))

pc_se_mers %>%
  dplyr::filter(enhancer_type == "SE") %>%
  dplyr::select(mer_id,mer_type,mer_gene_label,total_mut_patients,pct_total_mut_patients,seqnames,start,
                end,cluster_label,cds_overlap_pct,three_prime_utr_overlap_pct,five_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
                intron_overlap_pct,intergenic_pct,lncrna_overlap_pct,mirna_overlap_pct,
                enhancer_overlap_pct,tissue,enhancer_type,promoter_overlap_pct,prom_id) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))

pc_se_mers %>%
  dplyr::filter(enhancer_type == "E") %>%
  dplyr::select(mer_id,mer_type,mer_gene_label,total_mut_patients,pct_total_mut_patients,seqnames,start,
                end,cluster_label,cds_overlap_pct,three_prime_utr_overlap_pct,five_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
                intron_overlap_pct,intergenic_pct,lncrna_overlap_pct,mirna_overlap_pct,
                enhancer_overlap_pct,tissue,enhancer_type,promoter_overlap_pct,prom_id,overlapping_genes) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))

# Dual GCB / PC MERs
dual_se_mers <- e_se_mers %>%
  dplyr::filter(tissue == "GCB,PC")

message("Number of Dual GCB and PC E/SE-MERs: ", length(unique(dual_se_mers$mer_id)))

dual_se_mers %>%
  #dplyr::filter(enhancer_type == "E") %>%
  dplyr::select(mer_id,mer_type,mer_gene_label,total_mut_patients,pct_total_mut_patients,seqnames,start,
                end,cluster_label,cds_overlap_pct,three_prime_utr_overlap_pct,five_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
                intron_overlap_pct,intergenic_pct,lncrna_overlap_pct,mirna_overlap_pct,
                enhancer_overlap_pct,tissue,enhancer_type,promoter_overlap_pct,prom_id,overlapping_genes) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))
```

Look at the BCL6 locus for comparison to Dalla Favera's DLBCL analysis
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

bcl6_intron_window <- "chr3:187744850-187745350"

# Create window labels for additional layers
#bcl6_intron_woi <- data.table("win_start" = c(187744872, 187745199, 187744678),
#                              "win_end" = c(187744917, 187745246, 187744698),
#                              "win_name" = c(window_labeler(window = "chr3:187744872-187744917", padding = 0,
#                                                            name_of_window = "Normal MBC Hotspot 1\n(Iqbal et al. 2007)"),
#                                             window_labeler(window = "chr3:187745199-187745246", padding = 0,
#                                                            name_of_window = "Normal MBC Hotspot 2\n(Iqbal et al. 2007)"),
#                                             window_labeler(window = "chr3:187744678-187744698", padding = 0,
#                                                            name_of_window = "DLBCL Hotspot\n(Bal et al. 2022)")),
#                              "win_color" = c("#9BD08A", "#C2EFB4", "#DC766F"))

bcl6_intron_woi <- data.table("win_start" = c(187744872, 187745199, 187744678),
                              "win_end" = c(187744917, 187745246, 187744698),
                              "win_name" = c("Normal MBC Hotspot 1*",
                                             "Normal MBC Hotspot 2**",
                                             "DLBCL Hotspot***"),
                              "win_color" = c("#9BD08A", "#C2EFB4", "#DC766F"))

bcl6_intron_woi$win_name <- factor(bcl6_intron_woi$win_name,
                                   levels = bcl6_intron_woi$win_name)

ideogram_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_single_chrom_ideogrom(window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
                                                         flank = 250)

scale_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_locus_scale(window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
                                            flank = 250)

gene_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_gene_track(window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
                                          flank = 250,
                                          genes_of_interest = "BCL6",
                                          gene_name_side = "five_prime",
                                          gene_label_nudge = 30,
                                          gene_label_size = 4) +
                            labs(y = "BCL6 Intron 1")

gene_track_bcl6_intron_lines <- insert_gg_layer(ggplot_base = gene_track_bcl6_intron, insert_layer_after = 0,
                                  ggplot2::geom_rect(data = bcl6_intron_woi,
                                                     aes(xmin = win_start,
                                                         xmax = win_end,
                                                         ymin = 0,
                                                         ymax = 2,
                                                         fill = win_name),
                                                     alpha = 0.3)) +
                                  scale_fill_manual(name = "Windows",values = bcl6_intron_woi$win_color)

enhancer_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_bed_track(data = complete_ee,
                                             window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
                                             flank = 250,
                                             aes_y_column = "tissue",
                                             aes_fill_column = "enhancer_type",
                                             x_axis_theme = "blank",
                                             legend_title = "Element",
                                             aes_fill_colors = "#DC9445") +
                            labs(y = "Enhancers") +
  ggplot2::geom_rect(aes(xmin = 187744872, xmax = 187744917, ymin = 0, ymax = 2), alpha = 0.3, fill = "#9BD08A", color = alpha("#9BD08A", alpha = 0.2)) +
  ggplot2::geom_rect(aes(xmin = 187745199, xmax = 187745246, ymin = 0, ymax = 2), alpha = 0.3, fill = "#C2EFB4", color = alpha("#C2EFB4", alpha = 0.2)) +
  ggplot2::geom_rect(aes(xmin = 187744678, xmax = 187744698, ymin = 0, ymax = 2), alpha = 0.3, fill = "#DC766F", color = alpha("#DC766F", alpha = 0.2))

mer_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_bed_track(data = mers_gr,
                                        window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
                                        flank = 250,
                                        aes_y_column = "mer_type",
                                        aes_fill_column = "mer_type",
                                        x_axis_theme = "blank",
                                        aes_fill_colors = c("black","#FB832D")) +
                           labs(y = "MERs") +
  ggplot2::geom_rect(aes(xmin = 187744872, xmax = 187744917, ymin = 0, ymax = 3), alpha = 0.2, fill = "#9BD08A", color = alpha("#9BD08A", alpha = 0.06)) +
  ggplot2::geom_rect(aes(xmin = 187745199, xmax = 187745246, ymin = 0, ymax = 3), alpha = 0.2, fill = "#C2EFB4", color = alpha("#C2EFB4", alpha = 0.06)) +
  ggplot2::geom_rect(aes(xmin = 187744678, xmax = 187744698, ymin = 0, ymax = 3), alpha = 0.2, fill = "#DC766F", color = alpha("#DC766F", alpha = 0.06))

# create SNV track layer to add on top of
snv_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_count_mutations(data = snv_muts_hq_dt,
                                               window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
                                               flank = 250,
                                               aes_y_column = "Reference_Allele",
                                               aes_fill_color = "#191970",
                                               x_axis_theme = "blank") +
                                         ylab("Myeloma\nSNVs") +
  scale_y_continuous(, breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3), expand = c(0.01,0.01))

snv_track_bcl6_intron_lines <- insert_gg_layer(ggplot_base = snv_track_bcl6_intron, insert_layer_after = 0,
                                ggplot2::geom_rect(data = bcl6_intron_woi,
                                                   aes(xmin = win_start,
                                                       xmax = win_end,
                                                       ymin = 0,
                                                       ymax = max(snv_track_bcl6_intron$data$n) + 1,
                                                       fill = win_name),
                                                   alpha = 0.3)) +
                                scale_fill_manual(name = "Windows",values = bcl6_intron_woi$win_color) +
  scale_y_continuous(, breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3), expand = c(0.01,0.01))

#snv_vaf_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_vaf_landscape(data = snv_muts_hq_dt,
#                                                window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
#                                                flank = 250,
#                                                contrast = F,
#                                                x_axis_theme = "blank")  +
#                            ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn(name = "Density", colours = rev(paletteer_c("grDevices::GnBu", 15)))

#snv_vaf_track_bcl6_intron_lines <- insert_gg_layer(ggplot_base = snv_vaf_track_bcl6_intron, insert_layer_after = 0,
#                                    ggplot2::geom_rect(data = bcl6_intron_woi,
#                                                       aes(xmin = win_start,
#                                                           xmax = win_end,
#                                                           ymin = 0,
#                                                           ymax = 1,
#                                                           fill = win_name),
#                                                       alpha = 0.2)) +
#                                    scale_fill_manual(name = "Windows",values = bcl6_intron_woi$win_color) +
#                                    theme(legend.position = "none")

indel_track_bcl6_intron  <- geom_count_mutations(data = indel_muts_hq_dt,
                                               window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
                                               flank = 250,
                                               aes_y_column = "Reference_Allele",
                                               aes_fill_color = "#EEA9B8",
                                               x_axis_theme = "blank") +
                         ylab("Myeloma\nInDels") +
  scale_y_continuous(, breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3), expand = c(0.01,0.01))

indel_track_bcl6_intron_lines <- insert_gg_layer(ggplot_base = indel_track_bcl6_intron, insert_layer_after = 0,
                                  ggplot2::geom_rect(data = bcl6_intron_woi,
                                                     aes(xmin = win_start,
                                                         xmax = win_end,
                                                         ymin = 0,
                                                         ymax = max(indel_track_bcl6_intron$data$n) + 1,
                                                         fill = win_name),
                                                     alpha = 0.3)) +
                                  scale_fill_manual(name = "Windows",values = bcl6_intron_woi$win_color) +
  scale_y_continuous(, breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3), expand = c(0.01,0.01))

normal_snv_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_count_mutations(data = gr2dt(normal_lymphocyte_muts_for_model) %>% dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV"),
                                               window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
                                               flank = 250,
                                               aes_y_column = "Reference_Allele",
                                               aes_fill_color = "gray",
                                               x_axis_theme = "blank") +
                                         ylab("Lymphocyte\nSNVs") +
  scale_y_continuous(, breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3), expand = c(0.01,0.01))

normal_snv_track_bcl6_intron_lines <- insert_gg_layer(ggplot_base = normal_snv_track_bcl6_intron, insert_layer_after = 0,
                                  ggplot2::geom_rect(data = bcl6_intron_woi,
                                                     aes(xmin = win_start,
                                                         xmax = win_end,
                                                         ymin = 0,
                                                         ymax = max(normal_snv_track_bcl6_intron$data$n) + 1,
                                                         fill = win_name),
                                                     alpha = 0.3)) +
                                  scale_fill_manual(name = "Windows",values = bcl6_intron_woi$win_color) +
  scale_y_continuous(, breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3), expand = c(0.01,0.01))


bcl6_intron_locus_view <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(ideogram_track_bcl6_intron,
                                                     scale_track_bcl6_intron,
                                                     normal_snv_track_bcl6_intron_lines,
                                                     indel_track_bcl6_intron_lines,
                                                     snv_track_bcl6_intron_lines,
                                                     enhancer_track_bcl6_intron,
                                                     mer_track_bcl6_intron,
                                                     gene_track_bcl6_intron_lines),
                                                ncol = 1,
                                                guides = "collect",
                                                heights = c(0.01,0.015,0.03,0.03,0.03,0.015,0.015,0.01))
# 9 x 14 portrait


#bcl6_intron_locus_view <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(ideogram_track_bcl6_intron,
#                                                     scale_track_bcl6_intron,
#                                                     normal_snv_track_bcl6_intron,
#                                                     snv_track_bcl6_intron_lines,
#                                                     snv_vaf_track_bcl6_intron_lines,
#                                                     enhancer_track_bcl6_intron,
#                                                     mer_track_bcl6_intron,
#                                                     gene_track_bcl6_intron_lines),
#                                                ncol = 1,
#                                                guides = "collect",
#                                                heights = c(0.1,0.075,0.15,0.15,0.15,0.075,0.1,0.075))

muts_per_mer %>%
  dplyr::filter(mut_uniq_id %in% c("snv_er42","indel_er7","snv_er43","snv_er44")) %>%
  dplyr::select(Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
  dplyr::distinct() %>%
  dplyr::count() / n_distinct(muts_per_mer$Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode)
```

Look at MER-promoter overlap
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# Main gene set list will include any B cell related pathway from the immunesigdb 
#bcell_c7_immunesigdb_pathways <- fgsea::gmtPathways(paste0(base_data_path, "functionalGenomicElements/c7.bcell.immunesigdb.v2025.1.Hs.symbols.gmt"))

prom_mers <- mers_overlap %>%
  dplyr::filter(promoter_overlap_pct > 0) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))

# mean overlap of all
prom_mers %>%
  dplyr::reframe("mean_promotor_overlap" = mean(promoter_overlap_pct))

message("Number of Promoter MERs: ", length(unique(prom_mers$mer_id)))

prom_mers_genes <- mers_overlap %>%
  dplyr::filter(promoter_overlap_pct > 0) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients)) %>%
  dplyr::select(prom_id) %>%
  dplyr::distinct() %>%
  purrr::as_vector() %>%
  stringr::str_split(pattern = ",") %>%
  unlist() %>%
  stringr::str_remove(pattern = "prom_")

message("Number of Genes linked to Promoter MERs: ", length(unique(prom_mers_genes)))

# All promoter - E/SE-MER overlap
prom_mers %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(enhancer_id)) %>%
  dplyr::select(mer_id,mer_type,mer_gene_label,total_mut_patients,pct_total_mut_patients,seqnames,start,
                end,cluster_label,cds_overlap_pct,three_prime_utr_overlap_pct,five_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
                intron_overlap_pct,intergenic_pct,lncrna_overlap_pct,mirna_overlap_pct,
                enhancer_overlap_pct,tissue,enhancer_type,promoter_overlap_pct,prom_id,overlapping_genes) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))

# Just GCB E/SE-MERs
prom_mers %>%
  dplyr::filter(tissue == "GCB") %>%
  dplyr::select(mer_id,mer_type,mer_gene_label,total_mut_patients,pct_total_mut_patients,seqnames,start,
                end,cluster_label,cds_overlap_pct,three_prime_utr_overlap_pct,five_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
                intron_overlap_pct,intergenic_pct,lncrna_overlap_pct,mirna_overlap_pct,
                enhancer_overlap_pct,tissue,enhancer_type,promoter_overlap_pct,prom_id,overlapping_genes) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))


# Both GCB/PC E/SE-MERs
prom_mers %>%
  dplyr::filter(tissue == "GCB,PC") %>%
  dplyr::select(mer_id,mer_type,mer_gene_label,total_mut_patients,pct_total_mut_patients,seqnames,start,
                end,cluster_label,cds_overlap_pct,three_prime_utr_overlap_pct,five_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
                intron_overlap_pct,intergenic_pct,lncrna_overlap_pct,mirna_overlap_pct,
                enhancer_overlap_pct,tissue,enhancer_type,promoter_overlap_pct,prom_id,overlapping_genes) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))

# Just PC E/SE-MERs
prom_mers %>%
  dplyr::filter(tissue == "PC") %>%
  dplyr::select(mer_id,mer_type,mer_gene_label,total_mut_patients,pct_total_mut_patients,seqnames,start,
                end,cluster_label,cds_overlap_pct,three_prime_utr_overlap_pct,five_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
                intron_overlap_pct,intergenic_pct,lncrna_overlap_pct,mirna_overlap_pct,
                enhancer_overlap_pct,tissue,enhancer_type,promoter_overlap_pct,prom_id,overlapping_genes) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))
  
# driver gene promoter MERs
mm_driver_genes[which(mm_driver_genes$gene %in% prom_mers_genes),]
```

Assess the overlap of MERs and normal lymphocyte MERs
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

lymph_overlap_pct <- data.table::data.table("mer_id" = mers$mer_id,
                                            "mer_gene_label" = mers$mer_gene_label,
                                            "lymphocyte_overlap_pct" = 0.0)

for(i in 1:n_distinct(lymph_overlap_pct$mer_id)) {
  # Get the total number of mutations at the MER
  per_mer_muts <- muts_per_mer_gr %Q% (mer_id == unique(lymph_overlap_pct$mer_id)[i])
  mer_mut_denom <- length(per_mer_muts)
  
  # Normal Lymphocyte MER overlap
  mer_mut_lymph_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(lymphocyte_mers)),
                                                                    subject = per_mer_muts,
                                                                    qcol = "nearest_gene",
                                                                    scol = c("mer_gene_label","mer_id")))
  lymph_overlap_pct$lymphocyte_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(unique(mer_mut_lymph_overlap$subject.id)) / mer_mut_denom * 100, digits = 2)
}

# Add the Enhancer / Promoter to MER overlap
mers_overlap <- mers_overlap %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = lymph_overlap_pct,
                   by = c("mer_id","mer_gene_label"))

lymph_mers_overlap <- mers_overlap %>%
  dplyr::filter(lymphocyte_overlap_pct > 0)

# mean overlap of all
lymph_mers_overlap %>%
  dplyr::reframe("mean_lymphocyte_overlap" = mean(lymphocyte_overlap_pct))

message("Number of Lymphocyte overlap MERs: ", length(unique(lymph_mers_overlap$mer_id)))

# E/SE-MERs that overlap with normal lymphocyte MERs
lymph_mers_overlap %>%
  dplyr::filter(tissue == "GCB") %>%
  dplyr::select(mer_id,mer_type,mer_gene_label,total_mut_patients,pct_total_mut_patients,seqnames,start,
                end,cluster_label,cds_overlap_pct,three_prime_utr_overlap_pct,five_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
                intron_overlap_pct,intergenic_pct,lncrna_overlap_pct,mirna_overlap_pct,
                enhancer_overlap_pct,tissue,enhancer_type,promoter_overlap_pct,prom_id,overlapping_genes,lymphocyte_overlap_pct) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))

lymph_mers_overlap %>%
  dplyr::filter(tissue == "GCB,PC") %>%
  dplyr::select(mer_id,mer_type,mer_gene_label,total_mut_patients,pct_total_mut_patients,seqnames,start,
                end,cluster_label,cds_overlap_pct,three_prime_utr_overlap_pct,five_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
                intron_overlap_pct,intergenic_pct,lncrna_overlap_pct,mirna_overlap_pct,
                enhancer_overlap_pct,tissue,enhancer_type,promoter_overlap_pct,prom_id,overlapping_genes,lymphocyte_overlap_pct) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))
```

Visualize the distribution of MERs across different genomic feature sets
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

mer_distro_by_protein_coding <- mers_overlap %>%
                                  dplyr::select(mer_id,midpoint_abs_linear,q_value,cluster_label) %>%
                                  dplyr::rename("feature" = cluster_label) %>%
                                  dplyr::mutate("group" = "Protein-coding")

mer_distro_by_enhancer <- e_se_mers %>%
                           dplyr::mutate("feature" = case_when(!is.na(enhancer_id) ~ "Enhancers")) %>%
                           dplyr::select(mer_id,midpoint_abs_linear,q_value,feature) %>%
                           dplyr::mutate("group" = "Enhancers")

mer_distro_by_promoter <- prom_mers %>%
                           dplyr::mutate("feature" = case_when(!is.na(prom_id) ~ "Promoters")) %>%
                           dplyr::select(mer_id,midpoint_abs_linear,q_value,feature) %>%
                           dplyr::mutate("group" = "Promoters")

mer_distro_by_lncrna <- lncrna_mers %>%
                          dplyr::mutate("feature" = case_when(!is.na(lncrna) ~ "lncRNA")) %>%
                          dplyr::filter(!is.na(lncrna)) %>%
                          dplyr::select(mer_id,midpoint_abs_linear,q_value,feature) %>%
                          dplyr::mutate("group" = "lncRNA")

mer_distro_by_mirna <- mirna_mers %>%
                          dplyr::mutate("feature" = case_when(!is.na(mirna) ~ "miRNA")) %>%
                          dplyr::filter(!is.na(mirna)) %>%
                          dplyr::select(mer_id,midpoint_abs_linear,q_value,feature) %>%
                          dplyr::mutate("group" = "miRNA")

mer_distro_by_lymphocyte <- lymph_mers_overlap %>%
                              dplyr::mutate("feature" = "Normal Lymphocytes") %>%
                              dplyr::select(mer_id,midpoint_abs_linear,q_value,feature) %>%
                              dplyr::mutate("group" = "Normal Lymphocytes")

mer_distro_by_feature <- rbind(mer_distro_by_protein_coding, mer_distro_by_enhancer,
                               mer_distro_by_promoter, mer_distro_by_lncrna, 
                               mer_distro_by_mirna, mer_distro_by_lymphocyte)

mer_distro_by_feature$group <- factor(mer_distro_by_feature$group,
                                      levels = c("Protein-coding", "Enhancers","Promoters",
                                                     "lncRNA", "miRNA","Normal Lymphocytes"))
mer_distro_by_feature$feature <- factor(mer_distro_by_feature$feature,
                                      levels = c("CDS","Intronic","UTR","Intergenic",
                                                 "Enhancers","Promoters", "lncRNA",
                                                 "miRNA","Normal Lymphocytes"))

mer_distro_by_feature_plot <- ggplot(mer_distro_by_feature,
       aes(x = midpoint_abs_linear, y = -log10(q_value), color = feature)) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 3.5) +
  facet_wrap(facets = ~ group, ncol = 1) +
  ggplot2::labs(y =  expression("-log"[10]*"("*italic("q-value")*")")) +
  ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1, last(linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:22])),
                     labels = names(hg_seqlengths()[1:22]),
                     breaks = linearized_hg38_midpoint_breaks[1:22],
                     expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
  ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
  ggplot2::scale_color_manual(name = "Genomic\nFeature", values = paletteer_d("ggthemes::stata_s2color")[1:9]) +
  ggplot2::theme(axis.title.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 15),
                 axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                 axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, size = 11),
                 legend.position = "top",
                 legend.text = element_text(size = 13),
                 panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
                 strip.text = element_text(size = 13))


# Pie chart for each facet plot

mer_distro_pie_protein_coding <- ggpie::ggpie(data = mer_distro_by_feature %>%
                                                       dplyr::filter(group == "Protein-coding"),
                                               group_key = "feature",
                                               count_type = "full",
                                               label_info = c("count", "ratio"),
                                               label_type = "horizon",
                                               label_size = 6,
                                               label_pos = "out",
                                               border_color = NA) +
                                    scale_fill_manual(values = paletteer_d("ggthemes::stata_s2color")[1:4]) +
                                    theme(legend.position = "none")

mer_distro_pie_enhnacer <- ggpie::ggpie(data = mers_overlap %>%
                                                dplyr::mutate("feature" = case_when(!is.na(enhancer_id) ~ "Enhancers",
                                                                                    is.na(enhancer_id) ~ "non-Enhancers")) %>%
                                                dplyr::select(mer_id,feature),
                                               group_key = "feature",
                                               count_type = "full",
                                               label_info = c("count", "ratio"),
                                               label_type = "horizon",
                                               label_size = 6,
                                               label_pos = "out",
                                               border_color = NA) +
                                    scale_fill_manual(values = c("gray85", paletteer_d("ggthemes::stata_s2color")[5])) +
                                    theme(legend.position = "none")

mer_distro_pie_promoter <- ggpie::ggpie(data = mers_overlap %>%
                                                dplyr::mutate("feature" = case_when(!is.na(prom_id) ~ "Promoters",
                                                                                    is.na(prom_id) ~ "non-Promoters")) %>%
                                                dplyr::select(mer_id,feature),
                                               group_key = "feature",
                                               count_type = "full",
                                               label_info = c("count", "ratio"),
                                               label_type = "horizon",
                                               label_size = 6,
                                               label_pos = "out",
                                               border_color = NA) +
                                    scale_fill_manual(values = c("gray85", paletteer_d("ggthemes::stata_s2color")[6])) +
                                    theme(legend.position = "none")

mer_distro_pie_lncrna <- ggpie::ggpie(data = lncrna_mers %>%
                                                dplyr::mutate("feature" = case_when(!is.na(lncrna) ~ "lncRNA",
                                                                                    is.na(lncrna) ~ "non-lncRNA")) %>%
                                                dplyr::select(mer_id,feature),
                                               group_key = "feature",
                                               count_type = "full",
                                               label_info = c("count", "ratio"),
                                               label_type = "horizon",
                                               label_size = 6,
                                               label_pos = "out",
                                               border_color = NA) +
                                    scale_fill_manual(values = c(paletteer_d("ggthemes::stata_s2color")[7], "gray85")) +
                                    theme(legend.position = "none")

mer_distro_pie_mirna <- ggpie::ggpie(data = mirna_mers %>%
                                                dplyr::mutate("feature" = case_when(!is.na(mirna) ~ "miRNA",
                                                                                    is.na(mirna) ~ "non-miRNA")) %>%
                                                dplyr::select(mer_id,feature),
                                               group_key = "feature",
                                               count_type = "full",
                                               label_info = c("count", "ratio"),
                                               label_type = "horizon",
                                               label_size = 6,
                                               label_pos = "out",
                                               border_color = NA) +
                                    scale_fill_manual(values = c("gray85", paletteer_d("ggthemes::stata_s2color")[8])) +
                                    theme(legend.position = "none")

mer_distro_pie_lymphocyte <- ggpie::ggpie(data = mers_overlap %>%
                                                  dplyr::mutate("feature" = case_when(lymphocyte_overlap_pct > 0.0 ~ "Lymphocyte",
                                                                                      lymphocyte_overlap_pct <= 0.0 ~ "non-Lymphocyte")) %>%
                                                  dplyr::select(mer_id,feature),
                                               group_key = "feature",
                                               count_type = "full",
                                               label_info = c("count", "ratio"),
                                               label_type = "horizon",
                                               label_size = 6,
                                               label_pos = "out",
                                               border_color = NA) +
                                    scale_fill_manual(values = c("gray85", paletteer_d("ggthemes::stata_s2color")[9])) +
                                    theme(legend.position = "none")

#mer_distro_pie_protein_coding / mer_distro_pie_enhnacer / mer_distro_pie_promoter / mer_distro_pie_lncrna / mer_distro_pie_mirna / mer_distro_pie_lymphocyte

# UpSet plot of overlap between all types
mer_by_feature_upset <- list("CDS" = mer_distro_by_protein_coding %>% dplyr::filter(feature == "CDS") %>%
                                        dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                        as.character() %>% gtools::mixedsort(),
                             "Intronic" = mer_distro_by_protein_coding %>% dplyr::filter(feature == "Intronic") %>%
                                          dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                          as.character() %>% gtools::mixedsort(),
                             "UTR" = mer_distro_by_protein_coding %>% dplyr::filter(feature == "UTR") %>%
                                        dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                        as.character() %>% gtools::mixedsort(),
                             "Intergenic" = mer_distro_by_protein_coding %>% dplyr::filter(feature == "Intergenic") %>%
                                            dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                            as.character() %>% gtools::mixedsort(),
                             "Enhancers" = mer_distro_by_enhancer %>% dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                            as.character() %>% gtools::mixedsort(),
                             "Promoters" = mer_distro_by_promoter %>% dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                            as.character() %>% gtools::mixedsort(),
                             "lncRNA" = mer_distro_by_lncrna %>% dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                          as.character() %>% gtools::mixedsort(),
                             "miRNA" = mer_distro_by_mirna %>% dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                        as.character() %>% gtools::mixedsort(),
                             "Normal Lymphocytes" = mer_distro_by_lymphocyte %>% dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                                     as.character() %>% gtools::mixedsort())

mer_by_feature_upset_order <- c("CDS","Intronic","UTR","Intergenic","Enhancers",
                                "Promoters","lncRNA","miRNA","Normal Lymphocytes")

mer_by_feature_upset_plot <- upset(data = fromList(input = mer_by_feature_upset),
      sets = rev(mer_by_feature_upset_order),
      keep.order = T,
      order.by = "freq",
      mainbar.y.label = "Intersection",
      sets.x.label = "MERs annotated by feature",
      point.size = 5.25,
      line.size = 1.8,
      text.scale = c(1.75, 1.5, 1.5, 1.25, 1.25, 1.5),
      sets.bar.color = rev(paletteer_d("ggthemes::stata_s2color")[1:9]),
      matrix.color = "black")
```

**Figure 4**

```{r}
#| label: main-fig-4a
#| fig-cap: "**a.** Distribution of MERs stratified by the overlap with each group of functional genomic features. Each pie-chart displays the percentage of all 307 MERs that overlap with the genomic feature in the legend. The gray shaded area represents the MERs for each that do not overlap with the feature. Protein-coding gene annotations are subclassified by the overlap with coding sequences (CDS), introns, untranslated regions (UTRs), or intergenic sequences. Enhancer annotations are any germinal center B-cell or plasma cell enhancer or super-enhancer. Long non-coding RNA (lncRNA) and micro-RNA (miRNA) annotations include any part of the gene. Normal lymphocyte annotations are mutationally enriched regions identified in the aggregated set of mutations from hematopoietic stem progenitor cells, naïve B-cells, or memory B-cells."
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-width: 7
#| out-width: "70%"

fig4_a <- (mer_distro_pie_protein_coding + mer_distro_pie_enhnacer + mer_distro_pie_promoter) / (mer_distro_pie_lncrna + mer_distro_pie_mirna + mer_distro_pie_lymphocyte)
fig4_b <- mer_by_feature_upset_plot
fig4_a
```

```{r}
#| fig-width: 7
#| out-width: "70%"

fig4_b
```

**Supplementary Figure 8**

```{r}
#| label: supp-fig-8
#| fig-cap: "Locus view of intron 1 of BCL6, the most recurrently mutated MER. The spectrum of mutations more similarly reflects that of normal memory B-cells (MBC) and lymphocytes than what is observed in diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL). The windows for most common sites of mutations are: *normal MBC hotspot 1 at chr3:187,744,872-187,744,917 and **normal MBC hotspot 2 at chr3:187,745,199-187,745,246 described by Iqbal et al. and ***DLBCL hotspot at chr3:187,744,678-187,744,698 described by Bal et al. From the top down the locus view shows the ideogram chromosome, cytoband, genomic window, and scale for reference, the count of SNVs and InDels in myeloma samples and normal lymphocytes from Machado et al., the site of MERs with type of mutation enrichment on the y-axis, the site of enhancer elements colored by type and tissue of origin on the y-axis, and lastly the gene body UTRs are solid black segments, and introns are thin black lines."
#| fig-height: 9
#| fig-width: 7
#| out-width: "70%"

supp_fig8 <- bcl6_intron_locus_view
#supp_fig8
```

Results - Kataegis and Omikli are associated with E/SE-MERs

Analyze MM genomes for clustered mutation scars
Collect all clustered mutations per sample and calculate 
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# Read in the total set of clustered vs non-clustered mutations
sigpro_clusters_all_clustered <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "clusteredMutationsAnalysis/clusters_clustered_vaf.txt"),
                                       header = T,
                                       sep = "\t")

sigpro_clusters_non_clustered <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "clusteredMutationsAnalysis/clusters_nonClustered.txt"),
                                       header = F,
                                       sep = "\t",
                                       skip = 1,
                                       col.names = colnames(sigpro_clusters_all_clustered)[-14])

# Extract the high-quality samples
hq_sigpro_clusters_all_clustered <- sigpro_clusters_all_clustered %>%
                                      dplyr::mutate("patient" = str_split(string = samples, pattern = "_vs_", simplify = T)[,2],
                                                    "sample" = str_split(string = samples, pattern = "_vs_", simplify = T)[,1]) %>%
                                      dplyr::filter(sample %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata$sample))

hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered <- sigpro_clusters_non_clustered %>%
                                      dplyr::mutate("patient" = str_split(string = samples, pattern = "_vs_", simplify = T)[,2],
                                                    "sample" = str_split(string = samples, pattern = "_vs_", simplify = T)[,1]) %>%
                                      dplyr::filter(sample %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata$sample))

# Calculate the clustered mutational burden and percentage clustered vs non clustered
hq_clustered_metrics <- hq_sigpro_clusters_all_clustered %>%
  dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
  dplyr::count() %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::rename("tot_clustered" = n) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered %>%
                        dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
                        dplyr::count() %>%
                        dplyr::ungroup() %>%
                        dplyr::rename("tot_non_clustered" = n),
                   by = "sample") %>%
  dplyr::mutate("clustered_tmb" = tot_clustered / 2800,
                "clustered_pct" = round((tot_clustered / (tot_clustered + tot_non_clustered)) * 100, digits = 1)) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>% dplyr::select(patient,sample,disease_stage,age,SBS5,SBS9),
                   by = "sample") %>%
  dplyr::mutate("age_group" = case_when(age < 65 ~ "Younger than 65",
                                        age >= 65 ~ "65 and older"))

# TODO: work on this snake plot
hq_clustered_metrics$sample <- factor(hq_clustered_metrics$sample,
                                       levels = hq_clustered_metrics %>%
                                                 dplyr::arrange(clustered_pct) %>%
                                                 dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
                                                 dplyr::arrange(disease_stage) %>%
                                                 dplyr::ungroup() %>%
                                                 dplyr::select(sample) %>%
                                                 purrr::as_vector() %>%
                                                 as.character())

# Calculate some median
# All
#hq_clustered_metrics %>%
#  dplyr::group_by(patient) %>%
#  dplyr::reframe("median_pct_clustered" = median(clustered_pct))

# by Dx
clustered_muts_by_dx_medians <- hq_clustered_metrics %>%
  dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
  dplyr::reframe("median_pct_clustered" = median(clustered_pct))

# find point for median bar
clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x <- hq_clustered_metrics %>%
  dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
  dplyr::count() %>%
  dplyr::mutate("group_midpoint" = n / 2)

clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint <- clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$group_midpoint +
  c(0,clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[1],sum(clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:2)]),sum(clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:3)]))

clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$group_range <- c(clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[1],
                                                sum(clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:2)]),
                                                sum(clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:3)]),
                                                sum(clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:4)]))

clustered_pct_by_dx_snake_plot <- ggplot(hq_clustered_metrics) +
  geom_point(aes(x = sample, y = clustered_pct, color = disease_stage), size = 3.5) +
  labs(x = "Samples",
       y = "Mutations in\nclustered events (%)") +
  scale_color_manual(name = "Disease\nStage", values = disease_stage_colors) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.01,0.01), limits = c(0,38)) +
  theme(axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent")) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment",
           x = clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint - 7,
           xend = clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint + 7,
           y = clustered_muts_by_dx_medians$median_pct_clustered,
           yend = clustered_muts_by_dx_medians$median_pct_clustered,
           color = "red",
           linewidth = 2.25) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect",
           xmin = c(0, clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$group_range[-4]),
           xmax = clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$group_range,
           ymin = 0,
           ymax = 38,
           alpha = 0.30,
           fill = c("gray75","gray35","gray75","gray35"))

# Read in each set of clustered event type
cluster_subclass <- c("DBS","MBS","Omikli","KataegisProcessiveKetones","KataegisNonProcessive",
                      "KataegisProcessiveStrong","KataegisProcessivePyramidine","Other")
cluster_mainclass <- c("DBS","MBS","Omikli","Kataegis","Kataegis","Kataegis","Kataegis","Other")
sigpro_clusters_output <- data.table()
for(i in 1:length(cluster_subclass)) {
  cluster_subclass_data <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path,
                                               "clusteredMutationsAnalysis/subclasses/",
                                               list.files(path = paste0(base_data_path, "clusteredMutationsAnalysis/subclasses"),
                                                          pattern = paste0("*",cluster_subclass[i],".txt"))),
                                 fill = T)[,c(1:15)] %>%
                             dplyr::mutate("seqnames" = paste0("chr",chr),
                                           "sample" = str_split(string = samples, pattern = "_vs_", simplify = T)[,1],
                                           "patient" = str_split(string = samples, pattern = "_vs_", simplify = T)[,2],
                                           "subclass" = cluster_subclass[i],
                                           "mainclass" = cluster_mainclass[i]) %>%
                             dplyr::select(seqnames,start,end,ref,alt,patient,sample,group,IMD,`VAF/CCF`,subclass,mainclass)
                             
  sigpro_clusters_output <- rrbind(sigpro_clusters_output,
                                  cluster_subclass_data)
}

# Tally the composition of each subclass of clustered event 
per_patient_cluster_composition <- sigpro_clusters_output %>%
  dplyr::filter(sample %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata$sample)) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = hq_clustered_metrics,
                   by = c("patient","sample")) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(patient,sample,disease_stage,mainclass) %>%
  dplyr::count(name = "n_per_class") %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::group_by(patient,sample,disease_stage) %>%
  dplyr::mutate("sum_clustered_muts" = sum(n_per_class)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate("pct_per_class" = round(n_per_class / sum_clustered_muts * 100, digits = 2)) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# Make same organization as snake plot
per_patient_cluster_composition$sample <- factor(per_patient_cluster_composition$sample,
                                                levels = hq_clustered_metrics$sample)
per_patient_cluster_composition$mainclass <- factor(per_patient_cluster_composition$mainclass,
                                                   levels = c("DBS","MBS","Omikli","Kataegis","Other"))

clustered_events_colors <- c("#000000","#E69F00","#56B4E9","#009E73","#D55E00","#767F8B")

clustered_muts_composition_barplot <- ggplot(per_patient_cluster_composition) +
  geom_col(aes(x = sample, y = pct_per_class, fill = mainclass), width = 1) +
  labs(x = "Samples",
       y = "Relative\nContribution (%)") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.01,0.01)) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Clustered\nEvent\nSubclass", values = clustered_events_colors[2:6]) +
  theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank())

# Calculate some median
per_class_median_by_dx <- per_patient_cluster_composition %>%
  dplyr::group_by(disease_stage,mainclass) %>%
  dplyr::reframe("sum_n_per_class_dx" = sum(n_per_class)) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = per_patient_cluster_composition %>%
                        dplyr::select(disease_stage,patient,sample,sum_clustered_muts) %>%
                        unique() %>%
                        dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
                        dplyr::reframe("sum_clustered_dx" = sum(sum_clustered_muts)),
                   by = "disease_stage") %>%
  dplyr::mutate("pct_per_class_dx" = round(sum_n_per_class_dx / sum_clustered_dx * 100, digits = 2))

#ggplot(per_class_median_by_dx) +
#  geom_col(aes(x = disease_stage, y = pct_per_class_dx, fill = mainclass)) +
#  scale_fill_manual(name = "Clustered\nEvent\nSubclass", values = clustered_events_colors[2:6])


per_patient_cluster_composition %>% dplyr::filter(mainclass == "Kataegis") %>% dplyr::select(patient) %>% dplyr::n_distinct() /
  (per_patient_cluster_composition %>% dplyr::select(patient) %>% dplyr::n_distinct())

# What is the burden of clustered events outside of the IG regions
hq_sigpro_clusters_muts <- sigpro_clusters_output %>%
                             dplyr::filter(sample %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata$sample))
hq_sigpro_clusters_muts_gr <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(hq_sigpro_clusters_muts))

# clustered_event_regions <- data.table()

#                                             "vaf_median" = median(per_sample_cluster_subclass_event_group$`VAF/CCF`)))
#     }
#   }
# }

#saveRDS(object = clustered_event_regions, file = paste0(base_data_path, "clusteredMutationsAnalysis/clusteredEvents.rds"))
clustered_event_regions <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "clusteredMutationsAnalysis/clusteredEvents.rds"))

# Convert to GR obj
clustered_event_regions_gr <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(clustered_event_regions))

# Restrict to autosomes 
hq_clustered_event_regions_gr <- clustered_event_regions_gr %Q% (!seqnames %in% c("chrX","chrY"))

# Remove any duplicated event per patient as analysis is not across disease stage
uniq_hq_clustered_event_regions_gr <- hq_clustered_event_regions_gr[!duplicated(hq_clustered_event_regions_gr[,2])]

# Now mask regions that coincide with Ig regions , annotate with nearest gene and convert to DT
uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_gr <- gr_refactor_seqs(gr.setdiff(query = uniq_hq_clustered_event_regions_gr, subject = ig_region_mask_bed))
uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_gr$nearest_gene <- genes$gene_name[nearest(x = uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_gr, subject = gr.stripstrand(genes))]
uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_dt <- gr2dt(uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_gr)

# Count the events and compare to the total with Ig regions
uniq_ig_clustered_events_gr <- gr.findoverlaps(query = uniq_hq_clustered_event_regions_gr,
                                            subject = ig_region_mask_bed,
                                            qcol = c("sample","patient","mainclass","subclass","group","mut_count","vaf_median"))
uniq_ig_clustered_events_dt <- gr2dt(uniq_ig_clustered_events_gr)

message("Percentage of clustered events in Ig and non-Ig regions:")
uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_dt %>%
  dplyr::group_by(mainclass) %>%
  dplyr::count(name = "non_ig") %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = uniq_ig_clustered_events_dt %>%
                        dplyr::group_by(mainclass) %>%
                        dplyr::count(name = "ig") %>%
                        dplyr::ungroup(),
                   by = "mainclass") %>%
  dplyr::mutate("pct_non_ig_clustered_events" = round((non_ig / (non_ig + ig)) * 100, digits = 2))
```

Identify the most recurrent regions of clustered mutations
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# To find the consensus regions of clustered mutations, first need to define the
# consensus per main class of clustered event
# DBS, MBS, Omikli, Kataegis, Other
# Loop through the non-Ig clustered events by main class and find consensus regions
consensus_clustered_event_regions <- data.table()
for(i in 1:length(c("Omikli","Kataegis","Other"))) {
  class_specific_clustered_events <- uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_dt %>%
                                       dplyr::filter(mainclass == c("Omikli","Kataegis","Other")[i]) %>%
                                       dplyr::rename("chrom" = seqnames,
                                                     "sample_name" = patient) %>%
                                       dplyr::select(chrom,start,end,sample_name)
  
  # peakCombiner workflow
  # 1. Input preparation, computes center genomic coordinate per chromosome
  prep_class_specific_clustered_events <- peakCombiner::prepare_input_regions(data = class_specific_clustered_events,
                                                                show_messages = F)
  
  # 2. Add small flank to each region using defined value or default of median span of all input
  # will use half of the median of all sizes
  prep_class_specific_clustered_events_expand <- peakCombiner::center_expand_regions(data = prep_class_specific_clustered_events,
                                                                       expand_by = median(GenomicRanges::width(dt2gr(prep_class_specific_clustered_events))) * 0.25,
                                                                       show_messages = F)
  
  # 3. Consensus peak calling
  # found_in_samples = threshold of minimumn sample overlap for outpuut
  # combined_center  =  nearest - the 'center' of the input region that is closest to mean of the 'center's of all overlapping input regions
  consensus_class_specific_clustered_events <- peakCombiner::combine_regions(data = prep_class_specific_clustered_events_expand,
                                                               found_in_samples = 2,
                                                               combined_center = "nearest",
                                                               annotate_with_input_names = T,
                                                               combined_sample_name = c("Omikli","Kataegis","Other")[i],
                                                               show_messages = F)
  
  # Add to the final combined set of peaks
  consensus_clustered_event_regions <- rrbind(consensus_clustered_event_regions,
                                              consensus_class_specific_clustered_events)
}

# Tabulate the consensus set of each class
consensus_clustered_event_regions_dt <- consensus_clustered_event_regions %>%
  dplyr::mutate("patient_count" = stringr::str_count(string = input_names, pattern = ";") + 1,
                "pct_patients" = round(patient_count / 294 * 100, digits = 1)) %>%
  dplyr::rename("event_id" = name, "event_type" = sample_name, "patient_events_at_region" = input_names) %>%
  dplyr::select(chrom,start,end,event_type,event_id,patient_events_at_region,patient_count,pct_patients)

message("Number of consensus regions of omikli, kataegis, and other class events:")
table(consensus_clustered_event_regions_dt$event_type)

consensus_clustered_event_regions_gr <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(consensus_clustered_event_regions_dt))
```

```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true

all_mer_consensus_clustered_event_overlap <- gr.findoverlaps(query = consensus_clustered_event_regions_gr,
                                                             subject = gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers_overlap)),
                                                             qcol = c("event_type", "event_id", "patient_events_at_region", "patient_count", "pct_patients"),
                                                             scol = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label","cluster_label","tissue","enhancer_type","prom_id","overlapping_genes"))

all_mer_consensus_clustered_event_overlap_dt %>%
  dplyr::filter(is.na(tissue))

# Look at overlap between E/SE-MERs and consensus clustered regions
e_se_mer_consensus_clustered_event_overlap_gr <- gr.findoverlaps(query = consensus_clustered_event_regions_gr,
                                                                    subject = gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(e_se_mers)),
                                                                    qcol = c("event_type", "event_id", "patient_events_at_region", "patient_count", "pct_patients"),

# Overlap each type at each E/SE-MER

message("Percentage of Other clustered events that overlap with E/SE-MERs: ",
```

Investigate the percentage of mutations within E/SE-MERs are from clustered events and relate to SBS signatures
```{r}
#| tidy: true
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true
#| collapse: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-overflow: wrap

# Collect all unique clustered mutations
uniq_hq_sigpro_clusters_muts_gr <- hq_sigpro_clusters_muts_gr[!duplicated(hq_sigpro_clusters_muts_gr[,2])]

# Collect all unique non-clustered mutations
hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered_gr <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered))
uniq_hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered_gr <- hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered_gr[!duplicated(hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered_gr[,2])]


# All MERs
all_mers_non_clustered_muts <- gr.findoverlaps(query = gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers_overlap)),
                                               subject = uniq_hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered_gr,
                                               qcol = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label","cluster_label","tissue","enhancer_type","prom_id","overlapping_genes"),
                                               scol = c("patient","sample","ref","alt"))
all_mers_non_clustered_muts$mainclass <- "Non-clustered"

all_mers_clustered_muts <- gr.findoverlaps(query = gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers_overlap)),
                                                subject = uniq_hq_sigpro_clusters_muts_gr,
                                                qcol = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label","cluster_label","tissue","enhancer_type","prom_id","overlapping_genes"),
                                                scol = c("patient","sample","ref","alt","mainclass"))
# How many MERs have clustered mutations
n_distinct(all_mers_clustered_muts$mer_id) / 307

gr2dt(all_mers_clustered_muts) %>%
  dplyr::select(mer_id,cluster_label) %>%
  dplyr::distinct() %>%
  dplyr::count(cluster_label)


# GCB E/SE-MERs
gcb_e_se_mers_non_clustered_muts <- gr.findoverlaps(query = e_se_mers_gr %Q% (tissue == "GCB"),
                                                    subject = uniq_hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered_gr,
                                                    qcol = c("mer_id","mer_gene_label","tissue","enhancer_type"),
                                                    scol = c("patient","sample","ref","alt"))
gcb_e_se_mers_non_clustered_muts$mainclass <- "Non-clustered"

gcb_e_se_mers_clustered_muts <- gr.findoverlaps(query = e_se_mers_gr %Q% (tissue == "GCB"),
                                                subject = uniq_hq_sigpro_clusters_muts_gr,
                                                qcol = c("mer_id","mer_gene_label","tissue","enhancer_type"),
                                                scol = c("patient","sample","ref","alt","mainclass"))
# How many GCB E/SE-MERs have clustered mutations
n_distinct(gcb_e_se_mers_clustered_muts$mer_id)

gcb_e_se_mers_cluster_composition <- gr2dt(grbind(gcb_e_se_mers_non_clustered_muts, gcb_e_se_mers_clustered_muts)) %>%
                                       dplyr::group_by(mainclass,tissue) %>%
                                       dplyr::count(name = "mut_count") %>%
                                       dplyr::ungroup() %>%
                                       dplyr::mutate("pct_per_class" = round(mut_count / sum(mut_count) * 100, digits = 3))

gcb_e_se_mers_cluster_composition$mainclass <- factor(gcb_e_se_mers_cluster_composition$mainclass,
                                                      levels = c("Non-clustered","DBS","MBS","Omikli","Kataegis","Other"))
# Total clustered vs non %
```
